# Disproof Log ‚ÄĒ ABF26 Proximity Prize Grand Challenge 1 (Issue #232)


IDEA [DSAR-BMT] (#444, route: Bernstein‚ÄďMarkov‚ÄďTur√°n on ő∑). `ő∑_b = ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n} e_p(bx)` is a SPARSE
trig polynomial: as `P(z)=ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n} z^x` it has exactly `n` nonzero Fourier coefficients (the indicator
of `őľ_n ‚äā ‚Ą§/p‚Ą§`), all unit, spread over `[0,p)`. PROPOSED: the sparsity (`n` coeffs) + multiplicative-
subgroup support structure gives a Tur√°n/Bernstein‚ÄďMarkov-type ceiling `M(n)=max_{b‚Č†0}‚ÄĖő∑_b‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ f(n,sparsity)`
better than trivial `n` ‚ÄĒ WITHOUT the BGK coefficient/support arithmetic.

VERDICT: **REDUCES TO THE BGK WICK BOUND AT LOG DEPTH (no sparsity escape).** Two faces:
(i) The sparsity COUNT alone gives *exactly* the trivial `‚Ąď¬Ļ` ceiling `‚ÄĖő∑_b‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ n` (triangle inequality on
`n` unit terms), and that ceiling is REALISED. (ii) Tur√°n's power-sum method ‚ÄĒ the only "sparse-support"
tool that engages the coefficient LOCATIONS ‚ÄĒ bounds the sup from BELOW (`max_őĹ|ő£ z_k^őĹ| ‚Č• c‚ąön`), the
WRONG direction. The genuine UPPER bound on `ő£_{x‚ąąH}Ōą(x)` for a multiplicative subgroup `H` is precisely
the Bourgain‚ÄďGlibichuk‚ÄďKonyagin / Heath-Brown‚ÄďKonyagin problem (`ő£Ōą ‚Č™ p^{-ő≤}|H|`), proven via the
sum‚Äďproduct/Stepanov ARITHMETIC of `H` = the named BGK wall, open at the prize depth `n=2^őľ‚Čąp^{1/4}`,
`r‚ąľlog q`.

PROBE (`scripts/probes/probe_dsar_sparsity.py`; proper `őľ_n`, `n=2^őľ`, `p` prime, `p‚Čęn¬≥`):

| n | p | M | M/‚ąön | M/‚ąö(n¬∑log q) | **arbitrary `n`-sparse (consecutive) max** |
|---|---|---|------|--------------|--------------------------------------------|
| 8 | 4129 | 7.56 | 2.67 | 1.07 | **8.00 = trivial n** |
| 16 | 65537 | 13.84 | 3.46 | 1.20 | **16.00 = trivial n** |
| 32 | 1048609 | 22.98 | 4.06 | 1.26 | **32.00 = trivial n** |

DECISIVE KILL (last column): a DIFFERENT `n`-sparse unit support (`n` consecutive frequencies) attains the
trivial value `n` at a genuine NONZERO frequency. So the subgroup `M‚ČąC‚ąö(n¬∑log q) ‚Č™ n` is a property of
*which* `n` frequencies (the multiplicative arithmetic), never of *how many* (the sparsity count). Any
inequality keyed only to the coefficient COUNT cannot distinguish `őľ_n` from the consecutive set, hence
cannot certify `M<n`. (Same class as the [I027] PAPR‚ÜĒmerit and exhausted-moment findings: bounding `L^‚ąě`
above from a count/`L^p` datum needs the sub-Gaussian flatness that IS the conjecture.)

LEAN BRICK (axiom-clean, `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no sorry/native_decide):
`Frontier/_AR_BernsteinMarkovSparsity.lean` ‚ÄĒ `eta_norm_le_card` (`‚ÄĖő∑_b‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ |G|`, the ‚Ąď¬Ļ ceiling),
`eta_zero_eq_card`/`eta_zero_norm_eq_card` (ceiling attained: `ő∑_0 = |G|`), `no_subunital_sparsity_ceiling`
(no `őł<1` improvement holds for all configs). Signed DSAR-BMT. (The honest content of the sparsity route is
the ‚Ąď¬Ļ ceiling; the escape is the BGK arithmetic, kept as the named Wick/Paley wall ‚ÄĒ no shortcut.)


IDEA: M(őľ_n)/‚ąön is the PAPR (peak-to-average power ratio) of the coset spectrum b‚Ü¶ő∑_b
(ő∑_b=ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n}e_p(bx), constant on cosets of F_p*/őľ_n, an m-vector, m=(p-1)/n). Its "dual" is the
m-power-residue (Sidelnikov/generalized Legendre) sequence whose merit factor is KNOWN bounded
őė(1) (Schmidt/Jedwab, Legendre MF‚Čą6). The proposed NEW LEMMA: a PAPR‚ÜĒmerit (L^‚ąě‚ÜĒL‚Āī) duality plus
the bounded dual merit factor gives M/‚ąön = O(‚ąö(log m)) ‚ÄĒ an L‚Āī phase-sensitive object the L¬≤
conservation law misses.

PROBES (prize-faithful: őľ_n = 2-power subgroup n=2^őľ, n|p‚ąí1, p PRIME, p‚Čęn¬≥ (ő≤‚Čą3.0‚Äď3.1), proper
m=(p‚ąí1)/n>1, NEVER n=p‚ąí1), n‚ąą{8,‚Ä¶,256}, scripts/probes/probe_papr_merit_duality_I027.py,
probe_papr_merit_directionality_I027b.py, probe_papr_merit_l4linf_gap_I027c.py:

THE LITERATURE IDENTITY (Jedwab merit-factor survey; Borwein‚ÄďLockhart): for the Littlewood
polynomial p(z)=ő£ s_j z^j of a length-N sequence, ‚ÄĖp‚ÄĖ‚āĄ‚Āī = N¬≤(1 + 1/F), F = merit factor. So
"bounded merit factor F=őė(1)" is EXACTLY "bounded L‚Āī/L¬≤ ratio of the spectrum" ‚ÄĒ an L‚Āī flatness
statement, nothing more. (Also note the cited őė(1) Legendre value ‚Čą6 is the APERIODIC merit factor;
the object Fourier-dual to M is the PERIODIC power spectrum / 4th moment ‚ÄĒ a conflation, but
immaterial to the verdict below.)

(MEASUREMENT ‚ÄĒ the premise is TRUE, the conclusion is not deducible.) The coset spectrum IS
L‚Āī-flat: L4/L2 = 1.267, 1.291, 1.303, 1.310, 1.313, 1.325 at n=8‚Üí256 (4th-moment ratio
mean|ő∑|‚Āī/(mean|ő∑|¬≤)¬≤ ‚Üí ~3, i.e. periodic merit factor F=1/(ŌĀ‚āĄ‚ąí1)‚Üí1/2, BOUNDED őė(1) ‚ÄĒ the idea's
premise holds numerically). L2(ő∑)=‚ąö(p‚ąín)/‚ąöm ‚Čą ‚ąön exactly (Parseval floor, as known). But Linf(ő∑)=M
is NOT controlled: M/‚ąön = 2.43, 2.73, 3.40, 3.98, 3.77, 5.15 ‚ÄĒ it GROWS, and (M/‚ąön)/‚ąö(log m) DRIFTS
UP 1.17,1.15,1.28,1.37,1.21,1.54 (does not stabilize at a constant). The only inequality the merit
factor (L‚Āī flatness) actually licenses is H√∂lder in the WRONG direction: M ‚Č§ m^{1/4}¬∑L4 ‚Čą ‚ąön¬∑m^{1/4}
‚Čą ‚ąön¬∑n^{(ő≤‚ąí1)/4} ‚Čą n^{1.75} at ő≤=4 ‚ÄĒ WORSE than the trivial M‚Č§n. The "Holder m^.25*L4" column is
10.7, 22.2, 45.6, 93, 190 (‚Čę the actual M of 6.9‚Äď46): vacuous.

(THE STRUCTURAL KILL ‚ÄĒ parameter-free, probe_‚Ä¶I027c.py.) Hold the spectrum at the SAME bounded
merit factor the real ő∑ has (ŌĀ‚āĄ=3, F=1/2, L4/L2=3^{1/4}=1.316) and ask the LARGEST L^‚ąě that bounded
merit ALLOWS over m entries with mean|¬∑|¬≤=n fixed: solve the spike-plus-flat extremal. Answer:
max Linf ‚Čą (ŌĀ‚āĄ‚ąí1)^{1/4}(mn)^{1/4} ‚Čą n¬∑m^{1/4} ‚Čą n^{1.75}. The ratio (allowed Linf)/(target
‚ąö(n log m)) GROWS as a power of n: 2.30, 3.32, 4.97, 7.62, 11.9, 18.7, ‚Ä¶, 6043 at n=2^20. So a
bounded merit factor is CONSISTENT with M as large as ‚Čąn^{1.75} ‚ÄĒ it does NOT imply, and cannot
imply, M=O(‚ąö(n log m)). The single worst frequency (the spike = the open problem's argmax) is
invisible to the 4th moment: you can move all the mass into one spike of height n^{1.75} without
changing L‚Āī/L¬≤ by more than O(1).

WHY (the crisp reason): merit factor ‚ÜĒ L‚Āī flatness ‚ÜĒ 4th moment; M = L^‚ąě. Bounding L^‚ąě ABOVE from L‚Āī
requires sub-Gaussian/L^‚ąě‚ÜĒL‚Āī-equivalence of the spectrum (Salem‚ÄďZygmund flatness), which is an
ADDITIONAL hypothesis equivalent to the conjecture itself, NOT a free consequence of bounded merit.
The ‚ąö(log m) loss the idea wants is exactly the sub-Gaussian-tail bound that L‚Āī cannot certify. The
real ő∑ does happen to be far flatter in L^‚ąě than the worst L‚Āī-flat vector ‚ÄĒ but that flatness is a
fact about Gauss periods we are trying to PROVE; the duality merely re-states it. This is the SAME
class-4-average / L^‚ąě-from-moments wall as [I025] (Levenshtein weighted moment) and the campaign's
exhausted-moment finding: even moments (the merit factor is the 2nd even moment of the spectrum)
cannot pin a sup. The PAPR‚ÜĒmerit relationship runs L^‚ąě ‚Č• (L‚Āī/m^{1/4}) ‚ÄĒ a LOWER bound on PAPR from
merit, the OPPOSITE direction from the prize.

VERDICT: NO-GAIN. The merit-factor premise (L‚Āī/L¬≤ = őė(1), bounded dual merit) is genuinely TRUE for
őľ_n and the cited literature transfers correctly, but it is an L‚Āī statement that is structurally the
WRONG DIRECTION for an upper bound on the L^‚ąě quantity M: bounded merit factor is provably (probe
I027c) consistent with M up to ‚Čąn^{1.75}, so it gives no handle toward ‚ąön¬∑polylog. CORE stays OPEN.
No Lean brick (the only provable statement that emerges is the H√∂lder inequality M ‚Č§ m^{1/4}¬∑L4,
vacuous; and the merit-factor identity ‚ÄĖ¬∑‚ÄĖ‚āĄ‚Āī=N¬≤(1+1/F), which is the additive-4th-moment object
already in the energy substrate).

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ IDEA [I008] / C51 "Walsh/Haar-packet DYADIC-TOWER Stepanov auxiliary" NO-GAIN (probe_i008_walsh_dyadic_stepanov.py)

IDEA [I008] (stepanov-2adic, novelty 9): coordinatize x in mu_n (cyclic of order n=2^mu) by its
2-adic digit vector and build a Walsh-packet / dyadic-tower auxiliary
`Q(X) = prod_{i=1}^{mu} (X^{2^{i-1}} - s_i)` so that "vanishing to high order on mu_n is achieved by
TOWER TELESCOPING ‚ÄĒ each factor kills one dyadic level ‚ÄĒ order ~ mu at LINEAR degree, not n/r."
CLAIMED NEW LEMMA: exists Q, deg Q = O(n/log n), vanishing to ORDER >= log n on mu_n, giving via
Stepanov `|mu_n cap (shift)| <= O(n/(log n)^2)`, then summing cosets => `M(mu_n) <= sqrt(n log n)`
(past Johnson). Borrowed where-it-shows-up: Daubechies maximal-vanishing-moment two-scale filter
identities + Carlitz-Wan permutation polynomials.

VERDICT: **NO-GAIN.** The lemma is FALSE for the same reason the in-tree `mu_n_roots_simple`
(StepanovStructuredVacuous.lean) and C27 (Hasse-Davenport telescope) failed: the dyadic tower
TELESCOPES IN THE NUMBER OF ROOTS (across nested dyadic levels = DIFFERENT points), it does NOT
concentrate into VANISHING ORDER (multiplicity) at the points Stepanov must count. Stepanov consumes
`|Z| * M <= deg Q`, where M = the COMMON multiplicity (= min over the counted root set Z), NOT the
max at one designated point.

EXACT, CLOSED-FORM MECHANISM (verified vs sympy poly-division, `_sanity` True):
For x0 in mu_n (so x0 != 0) and p odd, every factor `(X^{2^{i-1}} - s_i)` has derivative
`2^{i-1} X^{2^{i-1}-1}` which is a UNIT at x0 (`2^{i-1}` is a unit mod p, x0 != 0). Hence x0 is a
SIMPLE root of factor i where it vanishes (`x0^{2^{i-1}} = s_i`) and not a root otherwise. Therefore
`mult_Q(x0) = #{ i : s_i = x0^{2^{i-1}} }` ‚ÄĒ a COUNT of factors, each contributing order EXACTLY 1.
To get common multiplicity m over Z roots you need each of the Z roots hit by >= m of the mu factors:
`m * Z <= sum_i (#roots of factor i in mu_n) = sum_i gcd(2^{i-1}, n) = sum_{i=1}^{mu} 2^{i-1} = n-1
= deg Q`. So `m * Z <= deg Q = n-1` is EXACTLY the trivial Stepanov inequality, with no saving.
The product buys NOTHING past the trivial degree bound.

DECISIVE PROBE (proper mu_n, p prime, p > n^3, p-1 div by n=2^mu, NEVER n=p-1; mu=3..7, beta=4):
- Forcing ALL mu factors to vanish at ONE point x0 (shifts `s_i = x0^{2^{i-1}}`) DOES give
  `mult(x0) = mu = log2 n` (the telescoping the idea wanted) ‚ÄĒ BUT this concentrates at a SINGLE
  point; the n/2 OTHER mu_n roots created are each SIMPLE (min-mult-over-roots = 1). Measured for
  mu=3..7: `mult(x0)=mu`, `#roots_on_mu_n=n/2`, `min-mult-over-roots=1`, `deg Q=n-1` uniformly.
- EXHAUSTIVE search over ALL shift vectors `s_i in mu_n` (mu=3: 8^3, mu=4: 16^4) via the closed-form
  multiplicity: BEST achievable COMMON multiplicity = **1** for every choice (lemma WANTS >= log n).
  Stepanov bound stays `|Z| <= deg Q / 1 = n-1` ‚ÄĒ the trivial bound, > n^{1/2}, never past Johnson.
- True `M(mu_n)` sanity (mu=3,4): exponent `log M / log n = 0.97, 0.95` (sitting at n^{1-o(1)}, the
  BGK SOTA the idea claimed to beat). The dyadic-tower auxiliary supplies no descent below it.

WHY THE WAVELET ANALOGY MISLEADS: Daubechies "vanishing moments" are vanishing of INTEGRALS
(sum_x x^k phi(x) = 0), an L^1/orthogonality condition ‚ÄĒ NOT vanishing to high ORDER (multiplicity)
of a polynomial at a point. The two-scale relation `phi(x)=sum c_k phi(2x-k)` is a self-similarity of
the FUNCTION, not a high-order root. Transporting "maximal vanishing moments" to "maximal root
multiplicity on mu_n" is the category error: separability of `X^n-1` (char p, p nmid n=2^mu) forbids
ANY polynomial from having order-2 contact on mu_n at sub-(M*n) degree. The 2-power / 2-adic
structure does not help ‚ÄĒ `gcd(2^{i-1}, 2^mu) = 2^{i-1}` makes the dyadic factors maximally
ROOT-RICH (good for spreading roots, the opposite of multiplicity).

MECHANISM CLASS: same horn as the entire Stepanov family on mu_n (C36 HBK REFUTED-FALSE;
StepanovStructuredVacuous `stepanov_collapses_to_degree`): mu_n manufactures NO multiplicity because
`X^n-1` is separable, so Stepanov collapses to the trivial `deg`-bound. A product/tower is structurally
blind in multiplicity exactly as it is blind in the conjugate-house (C27): it computes an AGGREGATE
(here, total incidence n-1 spread over roots), never a CONCENTRATION (multiplicity at one root) usable
by the inequality. NOT a new lever toward sqrt(n).

Probe committed: scripts/probes/probe_i008_walsh_dyadic_stepanov.py.

# Disproof Log ‚ÄĒ ABF26 Proximity Prize Grand Challenge 1 (Issue #232)

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ ANGLE A7 "Croot-Sisask almost-periodicity -> Bohr-set forcing of the worst u0" REFUTED (probe_a7_croot_sisask_bohr.py)

ANGLE (manifesto #7, route 30, relocation (C)/almost-periodicity): the far-line incidence
N(u0)=|Ball|+S(u0), S(u0)=Sum_{xi in D} K_w(wt xi) e_p(xi.u0), is the FT of a weighted measure on
the dual support D; Croot-Sisask almost-periodicity of the convolution 1_C * 1_Ball would give a
Bohr set B of offsets on which N(u0) is almost-constant. NEW LEMMA (invented, tested): if B is
large enough it forces the worst-case N(u0*) <= 2*avg = the floor, past Johnson. Faithful reduction
to the prize core: the period f(b)=Sum_{x in mu_n} e_p(bx)=FT(1_{mu_n}) is the simplest faithful
realization of S (worst u0 <-> worst b=argmax|f(b)|), so the Croot-Sisask program was tested ON f.

PROBE (prize-faithful: p PRIME, p=1 mod n, mu_n PROPER (n|p-1, (p-1)/n>1), p>>n^3 i.e. beta in {4,5,6},
NEVER n=p-1), n in {8,16,32,64}:
  PART 1 (the almost-period set). With eps=1 in ||f||_2=sqrt(np) units, the L2-almost-period set is
    EMPTY: |T_{1.0}|/p = 0.0000 at every n. The favorable (L2, not sup) reading of Croot-Sisask gives
    NO nontrivial almost-periods at the floor scale. The worst peak is DILATION-FIXED (|f(h b*)|=M),
    i.e. it sits in a rigid multiplicative orbit, not a translation-Bohr structure. M/floor (floor =
    sqrt(2 n ln(p/n))) = 0.756, 0.848, 0.891 -> 1 (the floor is real and approached, NOT 2*avg).
  PART 2 (peak vs Bohr set). On the Bohr-set translates b*+B (B from the large Fourier spectrum of
    1_{mu_n}), mean|f| is 1.235 (n=8, BELOW the bulk avg 2.005), 6.803 (n=16), 4.409 (n=32), 4.305
    (n=64) -- vs random+B = 2.005, 3.245, 4.672, 5.506. The peak is either ISOLATED (n=8: translates
    collapse below average -> the worst u0 ESCAPES the Bohr set) or a partial PLATEAU (n=16: b*+B ~2x
    random+B -> almost-periodicity PRESERVES the peak, the opposite of forcing it to the average).
    Neither produces N(u0*) <= 2*avg.
  PART 3 (THE KILL). The Croot-Sisask forcing target is M <= 2*avg. But M/(2*avg) GROWS MONOTONICALLY:
    1.653, 2.150, 2.538, 3.012 for n=8,16,32,64 (beta=4); and at FIXED n=16 it rises 2.150 -> 2.256
    as beta goes 4 -> 5 (p: 65537 -> 1048609). The ratio tracks sqrt(2 ln(p/n)) = the floor's excess
    factor exactly. Meanwhile avg|f|/sqrt(n) is FLAT at ~0.80 (Johnson scale), while M/sqrt(n) grows
    as sqrt(2 ln(p/n)). So the "2*avg" target is (i) only Johnson-scale even if achieved, and (ii)
    plainly FALSE here -- M exceeds 2*avg by precisely the sqrt(log) excess that separates the floor
    from Johnson.

VERDICT = REFUTED (the forcing fails on both horns; the surviving content reduces-to-wall, with a
Johnson-scale ceiling even in the best case). WHY (the conservation law, restated): Croot-Sisask
almost-periodicity is a SECOND-MOMENT / L2 statement -- the Bohr set is built from the large Fourier
spectrum and controls ||tau_t f - f||_2. Per the manifesto's Sec. I, the sqrt(log) excess is a
rare-event/tail phenomenon INVISIBLE to second moments. Concretely: (a) the L2 almost-period set is
empty at the floor scale (Part 1); (b) almost-periodicity, where it holds, makes the worst peak a
ROBUST PLATEAU (b*+B elevated, Part 2) -- it certifies the peak's stability, it does NOT push it down;
(c) the only bound the program could yield, 2*avg ~ 2 sqrt(n), is Johnson-scale, and the actual M
exceeds it by sqrt(2 ln(p/n)) (Part 3). The relocation to almost-periodicity is real but STERILE for
the sup: it reads the bulk (avg ~ sqrt(n) = Johnson) and is blind to the rare worst-b tail. Distinct
horn from A6 (which capped exactly at Johnson via trace structure): here the target is not merely
Johnson-capped but actively VIOLATED, and the violation IS the prize gap. Reduces ONLY to the
magnitude/sup question (the BGK/Paley wall); the secondary 2*avg ceiling is Johnson-scale. Probe
(committed): scripts/probes/probe_a7_croot_sisask_bohr.py.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ ANGLE A6 "Schur-Siegel-Smyth trace problem on the Gaussian period polynomial" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_a6_*.py)

Angle A6 (issue #444, manifesto angle 6, "truly novel, 0 in-tree"): relocate the cancellation
locus to the ALGEBRAIC-INTEGER TRACE. The `m=(p-1)/n` Gauss periods `eta_i` are the roots of the
integer period polynomial `Psi_p(T) in ZZ[T]`, deg `m`. NEW LEMMA tried: bound the largest period
`|eta_max|` via Schur-Siegel-Smyth trace-problem machinery (absolute trace + Newton/Schur power-sum
inequalities on `Psi_p`) to get `|eta_max| <= C sqrt(n)` past the Mann boundary, using ONLY the
integer structure of `Psi_p`, not the domain arithmetic.

**Exact identity established (load-bearing, verified):** `M(mu_n)=max_{b!=0}|Sum_{x in mu_n}e_p(bx)|
= max_i |eta_i|` ‚ÄĒ the prize sup-norm IS the largest absolute value of a Gaussian period of degree
`m`. (As `b` ranges over coset reps the period sum takes exactly the `m` values `eta_i`.) For `n`
even, `-1 in mu_n`, so the `eta_i` are REAL and `Psi_p` has `m` real roots ‚ÄĒ the clean
totally-real Schur-Siegel-Smyth setting. Parseval pins `Sum_i |eta_i|^2 = p-n`, so the RMS period
`= sqrt((p-n)n/(p-1)) -> sqrt(n)`: the second moment is RIGIDLY FIXED and gives only the trivial
`sqrt(n)` scale (no information), exactly the manifesto's "energy is blind" warning.

Verdict: **reduces-to-johnson** (periods too spread ‚ÄĒ the `‚ėÖ`-flagged A6 failure mode). Three
independent refutations, all on proper subgroups `n=2^mu`, `n|p-1`, `p` PRIME, `p>>n^3`,
`NEVER n=p-1`:

1. **Low-order integer-trace invariants are VACUOUS.** The only cheap integer invariants are
   `P_1=Sum eta_i=-1` (the trace) and `P_2=Sum eta_i^2=p-n`. The maximum root consistent with
   just `P_1,P_2` and `m` real roots is `~sqrt((m-1)/m)*sqrt(P_2) ~ sqrt(p)` ‚ÄĒ ENORMOUSLY larger
   than the true `M~sqrt(n log)`. Measured `M/permitted ~ 0.05->0.01` and shrinking with `n`
   (PROBE `probe_a6_powersum_diagnostic.py`). The absolute-trace structure does NOT cap the max
   anywhere near `sqrt(n)`. Higher power sums are rigid `p,n`-determined Jacobi-sum/cyclotomic
   quantities: `P_3=-n^2`, `P_5=-n^3` (tiny odd sums), even sums `~p^{k/...}` ‚ÄĒ computing them is
   recomputing the domain.

2. **The truncated moment-LP (= best-possible SSS bound from `P_1..P_K`, any finite `K`) is loose
   at every `K`.** The largest support point of any measure matching the first `K` integer power
   sums (Hankel pencil upper edge) grows with `K`: `K=2 -> sqrt(n)`, `K=4 -> 1.68 sqrt(n)`,
   `K=6 -> 2.2 sqrt(n)`, ... reaching the true `M~4.6 sqrt(n)` only as `K->m`. So the
   Schur-auxiliary-polynomial machinery needs ALL `~m` power sums = the full polynomial = the
   domain content again ‚ÄĒ the moment-ladder horn (PROBE `probe_a6_sss_applicability.py`). SSS
   proper, moreover, LOWER-bounds the AVERAGE trace of TOTALLY-POSITIVE integers; it cannot
   UPPER-bound an `L^infinity` max, and the periods aren't totally positive (trace `-1`).

3. **The decisive growth law: the saturated normalized edge `E_n := lim_{p->inf} M(mu_n)/sqrt(n)`
   EXISTS but grows like `sqrt(log n)`.** At fixed `n` the normalized period measure converges in
   moments to a `p`-independent limit (`L^{2k}` norm columns flat in `p` across decades ‚ÄĒ PROBE
   `probe_a6_even_moment_growth.py`), and `E_n(p)` saturates (n=8: `2.766->2.827`, increments
   `+.033,+.019,+.005,+.003->0`; PROBE `probe_a6_fixedn_limit.py`). But the saturated `E_n` GROWS
   with `n`: at fixed honest ratio `p/n^3=300`, `E_n = 2.00,2.80,3.74,4.29,5.11,5.11` for
   `n=4,8,16,32,64,128`; **const fit `R^2=0.00`, `sqrt(log n)` fit `R^2=0.98` (slope `3.26`)**
   (PROBE `probe_a6_horn_confirm.py`; large-`n` rows undersaturated => true growth `>=` measured,
   so robust). Hence in the prize regime where `n,p` co-grow (`log(p/n) ~ log n`), the limiting
   edge `E_n ~ sqrt(log(p/n))` gives `M ~ sqrt(n log(p/n))` = the Johnson scale.

**Constraint lemma (why the locus does not move):** the integer structure of `Psi_p` exposes only
the symmetric functions of the periods (power sums = traces). The first two are Parseval-rigid
(`= -1`, `= p-n`) and give exactly `sqrt(n)`; every higher one is a `p,n`-determined cyclotomic
number whose computation reconstructs the domain. The genuinely new fact ‚ÄĒ that `lim_p M/sqrt(n)`
exists as a fixed limiting-measure edge `E_n` ‚ÄĒ does not crack the wall because `E_n` itself
carries the `sqrt(log)` excess (the periods ARE spread on the Johnson scale). The trace problem is
an averaging machine; `M` is an `L^infinity` tail event, invisible to any bounded set of traces.
This is DISTINCT from the moment ladder (#W?) and Mann/antipodal only in framing ‚ÄĒ mathematically
it lands on the same Johnson boundary.

Probes: `scripts/probes/probe_a6_schur_siegel_smyth.py`, `probe_a6_powersum_diagnostic.py`,
`probe_a6_sss_applicability.py`, `probe_a6_even_moment_growth.py`, `probe_a6_edge_growth_verdict.py`,
`probe_a6_fixedn_limit.py`, `probe_a6_horn_confirm.py` (committed on branch main).


## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C13 "Vertical Sato-Tate Sup-Control via Katz Uniform Equidistribution Rate" SECRETLY-OPEN / REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c13_vertical_satotate_torus_dim.py)

Conjecture C13 (issue #444, algebraic-geometry, feasibility 1): claim the effective discrepancy of
the Gauss-sum-family equidistribution, against trig-polynomial test functions of degree
`‚Č§ log(p/n)` and RESTRICTED to a `őľ`-dimensional dyadic sub-torus, is `D = őľ¬∑2^őľ/‚ąöq ‚Č™ 1`, forcing
`max_b |ő∑_b| ‚Č§ ‚ąö(2n log(p/n))` past Johnson. **THE EXPLICIT BET: "the relevant torus has dimension
`őľ` rather than `f`."** REDUCES-TO: Katz Sato‚ÄďTate (large monodromy ‚üĻ equidistribution) + Rojas-Le√≥n
Cor 7 (only Hasse‚ÄďDavenport ‚üĻ `GL(1)^f`) + Deligne Weil II effective form + in-tree
`GeneralizedPaleyRamanujan` reduction.

Verdict: **secretly-open** (degenerating to **reduces-to-johnson** on the only effective reading).
The conjecture's `D ‚Č™ 1` premise ‚ÄĒ the sole thing that derives the `‚ąö(2n log(p/n))` bound ‚ÄĒ rests
entirely on the FALSE dimension bet. Probe `scripts/probes/probe_c13_vertical_satotate_torus_dim.py`,
commit `2690fef9cc80cc17f3be5a314a3aaeeec5e187a4`. All measured prize-faithfully (proper `őľ_n`,
`n=2^őľ`, `p` PRIME, `n|p‚ąí1`, `p‚Čęn¬≥`, `m=(p‚ąí1)/n>1`, NEVER `n=p‚ąí1`).

HORN-DIM (the load-bearing horn ‚ÄĒ the torus dimension is `f=(p‚ąí1)/n`, not `őľ`). The Rojas-Le√≥n
equidistribution monodromy is `GL(1)^f` where `f` = the number of monomial Gauss sums in the family
= the INDEX `m=(p‚ąí1)/n ~ p/n`. The DFT identity `ő∑_c = ‚ąí1/m + (1/m)ő£_{j=1}^{m‚ąí1} ŌĄ(Ōá^j) e(‚ąíjc/m)`
writes the period as a length-`(m‚ąí1)` LINEAR FORM in the `f=m‚ąí1` INDEPENDENT Gauss sums `ŌĄ(Ōá^j)`, so
the equidistribution lives on a torus of dimension `f=m‚ąí1 ~ p/n`, and the honest discrepancy is
`f/‚ąöq ~ ‚ąöp/n`. At PRIZE scale (`q=p~n¬∑2^128`, `n<p^{1/4}`):

| őľ | n=2^őľ | log‚āā p | ő≤=log_n p | `D_őľ=őľ¬∑2^őľ/‚ąöq` (C13's bet) | `D_f=f/‚ąöq` (TRUTH) |
|---|-------|--------|-----------|----------------------------|---------------------|
| 16 | 2^16 | 144 | 9.00 | 2.2e‚ąí16 | 2^56 |
| 20 | 2^20 | 148 | 7.40 | 1.1e‚ąí15 | 2^54 |
| 30 | 2^30 | 158 | 5.27 | 5.3e‚ąí14 | 2^49 |
| 35 | 2^35 | 163 | 4.66 | 3.5e‚ąí13 | 2^46.5 |

`D_őľ ‚Č™ 1` ONLY because of the bet; with the correct `f`, `D_f = 2^46..2^56 ‚Čę 1` ‚ÄĒ CATASTROPHICALLY
VACUOUS. A `őľ`-dimensional "sub-torus" is just `őľ` of the `m‚ąí1` Gauss-sum coordinates; restricting
the test functions to it simply DROPS the other `m‚ąí1‚ąíőľ` coordinates and proves nothing about the
full period `ő∑_c`. This is precisely the recorded large-sieve / Katz dimension obstruction
(`deltastar-407-large-sieve-dimension-obstruction-2026-06-13.md`, `‚Ä¶katz-monodromy-research‚Ä¶`,
in-tree `MonodromyConductorScaffold.lean` lines 64‚Äď73): equidistribution is effective only when
`f ‚Č§ ‚ąöq ‚üļ n ‚Č≥ ‚ąöp`; the prize has `n ‚Č™ p^{1/4} ‚Č™ ‚ąöp` by construction.

HORN-EFF (even granting dimension `f`, equidistribution is `q‚Üí‚ąě`, not the deterministic max). Katz
Sato‚ÄďTate / Deligne Weil II is a distributional limit as `q‚Üí‚ąě` for a FIXED sheaf; it gives the
TYPICAL (average) value, never the deterministic `max_b` over a thin DESIGNED subgroup at fixed
large `q`. Measured `M(n)=max_b|ő∑_b|` tracks the random/BGK value `‚ąö(n log(p/n))` within O(1) and
DRIFTS (no fixed effective constant), the random signature ‚ÄĒ the same effectivity barrier already
recorded for C14, C29, and the Katz/Rojas-Le√≥n route:

| n | p | p/n¬≥ | m=(p‚ąí1)/n | M(n) | ‚ąö(2n log(p/n)) | M/that | ‚ąö(n log(p/n)) | M/that |
|---|---|------|-----------|------|----------------|--------|---------------|--------|
| 8  | 11593    | 22.6 | 1449   | 7.676  | 10.792 | 0.711 | 7.631  | 1.006 |
| 16 | 262193   | 64.0 | 16387  | 14.215 | 17.622 | 0.807 | 12.461 | 1.141 |
| 32 | 1048609  | 32.0 | 32769  | 22.983 | 25.796 | 0.891 | 18.240 | 1.260 |
| 64 | 16777601 | 64.0 | 262150 | 38.529 | 39.963 | 0.964 | 28.258 | 1.363 |

`M(n)/‚ąön` GROWS `2.71 ‚Üí 3.55 ‚Üí 4.06 ‚Üí 4.82` (n=8‚Üí64), tracking `‚ąö(log(p/n))` ‚ÄĒ the random Gaussian
large-deviation law, NOT a fixed Sato‚ÄďTate semicircle edge (which would force `M/‚ąön ‚Üí 2`).

HORN-TARGET (moot but decisive on closure). The claimed conclusion `‚ąö(2n log(p/n))` IS the open
BGK/Paley target itself (`M(n)` tracks `‚ąö(n log(p/n))` to O(1)). So even if the dimension bet were
granted, C13 would assert the open core AS its conclusion ‚ÄĒ it does not reduce to proven math; it
reduces to the recognized-open thin-subgroup sup-norm bound. Hence **secretly-open**: pinning őī*
from C13 requires exactly the open BGK sup-norm. The in-tree `GeneralizedPaleyRamanujan`/
`MonodromyConductorScaffold` it cites are NAMED-OPEN hypotheses (`ConductorGeometricBound`,
`DeligneEffectiveEquidistribution`), not theorems ‚ÄĒ so "reduces-to in-tree reduction" is reducing to
another open residual, not closing anything.

Sibling to the REFUTED C14 (Kloosterman-purity, same object-mismatch/dimension family) and C29
(Kowalski‚ÄďSawin path CLT, same effectivity + growing-dimension horns). The genuine torus dimension
is `f=(p‚ąí1)/n`, fixed forever by Rojas-Le√≥n Cor 7 ‚ÄĒ the bet `dim=őľ` contradicts the very Corollary
the conjecture cites.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c13_vertical_satotate_torus_dim.py`, commit
`2690fef9cc80cc17f3be5a314a3aaeeec5e187a4`.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C48 "Effective Sauermann-Wigderson Polynomial-Method Rank Bound for the Agreement System" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c48_sauermann_wigderson_rank.py)

Conjecture C48 (issue #444, algebraic-geometry, feasibility 2): claim the Sauermann‚ÄďWigderson
polynomial-method rank bound, applied to the simultaneous far-line agreement system over `F_p`,
gives a list bound below budget `n` past Johnson, exploiting the bounded degree of the agreement
variety and the proven rigidity transfer (in-tree `RigidityReductionPrizeScale`). "A modern
polynomial-method lever distinct from CLP." REDUCES-TO: Sauermann‚ÄďWigderson polynomial-method rank
bounds + in-tree `RigidityReductionPrizeScale` + bounded-degree agreement variety.

**Verdict: reduces-to-johnson** (with a dimensional category error in even importing the SW
formula). Probe `scripts/probes/probe_c48_sauermann_wigderson_rank.py`. Probed over proper `őľ_n`,
`p` prime `p‚Čąn^4` (`p‚Čęn¬≥`, never `n=p‚ąí1`), `n‚ąą{16,32,64}` and the prize-band cap arithmetic out to
`n=1024`. Three horns, each independently fatal.

**What Sauermann‚ÄďWigderson actually is (arXiv:2010.00077, JLMS 2022).** For fixed `k‚Č•2` and `n`
large, the *minimum possible degree* of a polynomial `P‚ąą‚ĄĚ[x_1,‚Ä¶,x_n]` with `P(0)‚Č†0` vanishing to
multiplicity `‚Č•k` at every point of the **Boolean hypercube** `{0,1}^n \ {0}` equals `n+2k-3`
(`k=1` is Alon‚ÄďF√ľredi: degree `n`). It is an **extremal degree LOWER bound** on multiplicity-`k`
vanishing over the **`n`-dimensional Boolean grid** `{0,1}^n`, over `‚ĄĚ` (char 0). It is *not* a
list-size / incidence upper bound and *not* an algebraic-variety rank statement.

**(H1) Dimensional category error (the C39 H2 mechanism again).** SW lives in `{0,1}^n`, an
`n`-fold product; its content `n+2k-3` is a phenomenon of the GROWING number of grid coordinates
(`n‚Üí‚ąě`). The prize agreement "variety" `B = {őĪ‚ąąF_p : x^a+őĪx^b is őī-close to RS[k]}` is a
**1-parameter family in a cyclic group** ‚ÄĒ the eval domain `őľ_n` is a single-coordinate point set
on a line (dimension 1), with NO `n`-fold Boolean product to host the SW formula. This is exactly
the category error already recorded for C39 ("cap-bound-in-`F_q^N` vs cap-bound-in-`‚Ą§/p`"): the
polynomial-method dimensional saving lives in the ambient product dimension and is vacuous in a
1-dimensional cyclic ambient. Importing `n+2k-3` here is meaningless.

**(H2) Wrong direction / wrong quantity; it only re-derives the `k‚ąí1` ceiling.** SW is a degree
LOWER bound ("to vanish to order `k` off the origin you NEED degree `‚Č•n+2k-3`"). To convert a
vanishing-degree fact into a list-SIZE UPPER bound you run it through Alon‚ÄďF√ľredi /
Schwartz‚ÄďZippel / Vandermonde dimension counting. Probed on explicit `RS[F_p,őľ_n,k]`
(`n‚ąą{16,32}`, `k‚ąą{n/8,n/4}`, 780 polynomial pairs each): the maximum pairwise agreement of
distinct deg-`<k` codewords on `őľ_n` is `‚Č§ k‚ąí1` in EVERY case (measured `maxAgree=0` for random
pairs; the structural ceiling is `k‚ąí1`). That ceiling is *exactly* the in-tree Vandermonde rank
obstruction `rank_collapse_on_kset` / `poly_method_subset_incidence_bound` (`ListIncidencePolyMethod.lean`)
and the multiplicity double-count `card_le_choose_div_choose_of_pairwise_inter` (`FisherPastJohnsonCap.lean`).
SW's multiplicity refinement RE-DERIVES, it does not lower, `agree(c,c')‚Č§k‚ąí1`.

**(H3) The optimal poly-method cap is field-blind and super-polynomial past Johnson; rigidity
transfer does not help.** The in-tree axiom-clean cap `|L|¬∑C(a,k) ‚Č§ C(n,k)` IS the best output of
any polynomial method that uses only the `k‚ąí1` agreement ceiling (it is precisely the
Sauermann‚ÄďWigderson-style multiplicity double count). Probed at prize-band rates `ŌĀ‚ąą{1/4,1/8}`
just past the Johnson agreement radius `a‚Čą‚Ćą‚ąö(nk)‚ĆČ+1`: the cap `C(n,k)/C(a,k)` is `432` at `n=32`,
`4.0¬∑10¬≤¬≥` at `n=256`, `3.7¬∑10‚ĀĻ‚ĀĶ` at `n=1024` ‚ÄĒ astronomically above budget `n` at every interior
point. By `abstract_incidence_bound` the cap is FIELD-BLIND (identical for a generic injective
domain), so it cannot exploit `őľ_n`'s multiplicative smoothness. And `RigidityReductionPrizeScale`
only makes `őī*` **char-INDEPENDENT** (`I_p = I_0`, char-`p` incidence equals char-`0` incidence at
prize scale) ‚ÄĒ it does NOT shrink the char-`0` cap. So feeding SW the proven rigidity transfer
buys nothing past Johnson: SW + rigidity still lands exactly on the field-blind `k‚ąí1` Johnson wall.

**Conclusion.** C48 is the Sauermann‚ÄďWigderson sibling of C39 (CLP/EG). SW is a Boolean-hypercube
minimum-degree theorem with no cyclic analogue (H1), it produces a degree lower bound that only
re-derives the proven Vandermonde `k‚ąí1` ceiling (H2), and the resulting cap is the in-tree
field-blind super-polynomial incidence bound that is the converged Johnson wall (H3). The
past-Johnson lever is the open BGK / smooth-domain incidence (multiplicity) count, which SW does
not supply. The "bounded degree of the agreement variety" is real but is precisely what gives the
`k‚ąí1` ceiling and nothing past it. Reduces-to-johnson; confidence high.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C46 "Mahler-Measure / Lehmer Structure-Aware Norm on the Period Polynomial" SECRETLY-OPEN (probe_conjecture_refute_c46.py, commit ad99a21774)

Conjecture C46 (issue #444, gauss-period-exact, feasibility 2): a "structure-aware Mahler measure"
of the period polynomial `ő®_p(T)=‚ąŹ_{cosets}(T‚ąíő∑_c)` (weighting by the antipodal cyclotomic
structure) gives a SHARPER house bound than Cauchy, reaching `house=M(őľ_n) ‚Č§ ‚ąö(2n log p)` PAST
Johnson via the proven Myerson coefficient structure + in-tree antipodal pair `z+(‚ąíz)=0`.
REDUCES-TO: Mahler measure / Lehmer house inequality + Myerson coefficients + antipodal structure.
**Verdict: secretly-open** ‚ÄĒ it hides the open BGK/Paley sup-norm. The fourth distinct probe to
hit the **house‚Č†symmetric-functional archimedean gap** (same wall as C08-Myerson, C26-Stickelberger,
C27-HasseDavenport, C9). Already pre-flagged as a wall-risk in `docs/kb/deltastar-100-attacks/README.md`
lines 281/529-533 ("Mahler-measure 'house' framing is elegant but the m periods are NOT equimodular
... real content = |conjugate| equidistribution = the Sato-Tate/Paley wall; reduces to the open core").

LOAD-BEARING FACT (in-tree): `house(ő∑)=max_c|ő∑_c|=max_{a‚Č†0}|ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n}e_p(ax)|=M(őľ_n)` = THE OPEN
OBJECT (single-conjugate L‚ąě). Mahler measure `Mah(ő®)=‚ąŹ_{|ő∑_c|>1}|ő∑_c|` is a SYMMETRIC PRODUCT over
the `m=(p‚ąí1)/n` conjugates. The ONLY honest Mahler/Lehmer house inequalities are (a) `house ‚Č§ Mah(ő®)`
(trivial, = product, exponentially-in-m too weak) and (b) `house ‚Č• Mah^{1/#big}` (geometric mean of
the big roots, a LOWER bound). C46 needs a sharp UPPER bound = the wrong direction for a product.

DECISIVE PROBE (proper őľ_n, n=2^őľ őľ=3..5, p prime, p~n‚Āī‚Čęn¬≥, NEVER n=p‚ąí1, 4 primes/n):
- **T1 target shape REAL:** house=M(őľ_n)=7.56, 13.84, 22.98 (n=8,16,32) ‚Čą ‚ąö(n log p) below trivial n.
- **T2/T5 Mahler is exponentially-in-m too weak as an upper bound:** `log10(Mah)=158, 1710, 17683`
  (grows LINEARLY in m=516, 4096, 32769) ‚Üí `log10(Mah/house)=157, 1708, 17682`. "house ‚Č§ Mah(ő®)" is
  astronomically weaker than the target `‚ąö(2n log p)=11.5, 18.8, 29.8`. The Myerson/Cauchy coefficient
  route is the SAME symmetric functions, already shown 10¬≤‚Äď10¬≥ too weak in C08 (rerun confirmed).
- **T3 antipodal weighting halves the exponent AT MOST:** ő∑_{‚ąíc}=conj(ő∑_c) (‚ąí1‚ąąőľ_n) gives equal-
  modulus pairs, so a fully antipodal-aware Mahler is `Mah^{1/2}` = a product over m/2 roots, still
  `log10‚Čą79, 855, 8841` ‚Čę target. Collapsing product‚Üímax is NOT what the antipodal relation does.
- **T4 DECISIVE ‚ÄĒ Mahler does NOT determine house:** at FIXED n, house spread = 0.10, 0.64, 1.14 is
  DECOUPLED from log10(Mah) spread = 2.65, 3.60, 91.55. A symmetric product cannot pin the single
  largest conjugate. `Mah^{1/#big}=2.6, 3.3, 4.2 ‚Čą ‚ąön` (the geometric mean) = the L¬≤/JOHNSON scale.

The `‚ąö(log p)` prize factor IS the conjugate-argument SPREAD `{arg ő∑_c}` = the open BGK/Paley sup-
norm (best PROVEN `n^{1‚ąío(1)}` BGK ineffective; di Benedetto `n^{0.989}` only `n>p^{1/4}`, outside the
prize). The in-tree `L2MahlerNormBound.lean` already proves the strongest unconditional Mahler/AM-GM
norm bound, and its `l2_dominates_house` theorem makes the direction explicit: L¬≤/Mahler is a LOWER
bound on the house. No coefficient weighting converts the symmetric product into the L‚ąě house.
Probe: scripts/probes/probe_conjecture_refute_c46.py, commit ad99a21774. Same wall as C08/C26/C27/C9.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C47 "Cocycle Phase-Alignment Multi-Level for the Gauss-Sum Arguments" SECRETLY-OPEN / REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_conjecture_refute_C47_cocycle_phase.py, commit abec15e6e)

Conjecture C47 (issue #444, gauss-period-exact, feasibility 2): a multi-level cocycle/coboundary
decomposition of the Gauss-sum ARGUMENTS `arg ŌĄ(Ōá_j)` across the dyadic Hasse‚ÄďDavenport tower forces
PHASE NON-ALIGNMENT, bounding the period sup-norm `M(őľ_n) = max_{b‚Č†0}|ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n} e_p(bx)|` by
`‚ąö(n log p)` PAST Johnson; it "attacks the archimedean phase distribution directly." REDUCES-TO:
cocycle/coboundary phase decomposition + Hasse‚ÄďDavenport tower + in-tree antipodal structure.

**Verdict: secretly-open** (the only past-Johnson lever is the open archimedean argument
distribution), **degenerating to reduces-to-johnson** (the cocycle/coboundary algebra is exact but
pins only the symmetric/L¬≤ = geometric-mean = Johnson `‚ąön` scale). C47 is the ADDITIVE/argument-side
twin of C27 (multiplicative HD/Jacobi telescope, refuted above) and C28 (Gross‚ÄďKoblitz period);
all three are governed by the same in-tree no-go bricks `_DyadicJacobiCocycleNonContraction.lean`
(unimodular cocycle ‚üĻ no cancellation), `_GrossKoblitzPhaseNoGo.lean` (the `őď_p` unit part / any
reflection relation transmits at most a discrete sign, pinning a phase only at index 2), and
`_PhaseAlignmentReality.lean` (the only "alignment" the dyadic structure forces is reality of `ő∑_b`,
which holds for ALL `b` in thick AND thin regimes ‚ÄĒ not thinness-essential). Probe over PROPER `őľ_n`
(`n=2^őľ`), `p` PRIME, `p>n¬≥`, NEVER `n=p‚ąí1`, with EXACT Gauss sums for the cocycle test.

**Horn 1 ‚ÄĒ the cocycle "decomposition" is EXACTLY the Jacobi relation taking arguments; it holds
trivially and is vacuous (existence ‚Č† bound).** The named object is precise: taking arguments in the
Jacobi relation `ŌĄ(Ōá_j)ŌĄ(Ōá_k) = J(Ōá_j,Ōá_k)¬∑ŌĄ(Ōá_{j+k})` gives, with `a_j := arg ŌĄ(Ōá_j)`,
`c_{j,k} := arg J(Ōá_j,Ōá_k)`, the identity `a_j + a_k ‚ąí a_{j+k} = c_{j,k}` (mod 2ŌÄ) ‚ÄĒ i.e. the
argument family `(a_j)` is a 1-cochain on the cyclic dual `‚Ą§/m` (`m=(p‚ąí1)/n`) whose coboundary
`őīa` equals the Jacobi-argument 2-cochain `c`. The probe verifies this **EXACTLY**: `cocycle_err =
0.00e+00` at `őľ=2` (`p=73,m=18`) and `őľ=3` (`p=521,m=65`). So the "multi-level cocycle/coboundary
decomposition" is just the Jacobi relation and always exists. It is a tautology, not a bound. (This
is the in-tree `_DyadicJacobiCocycleNonContraction` fact: the cocycle factor `c_{j,k}=J_{j,k}/‚ąöp` is
**unimodular** `|c|=1`, so it imposes NO magnitude constraint beyond unit modulus and is consistent
with a fully-aligned `‚ÄĖP‚ÄĖ=m` saturating configuration ‚ÄĒ `exists_unimodular_cocycle_saturating_triangle`.)

**Horn 2 ‚ÄĒ DECISIVE: the residual phase is SPREAD, not LINEAR. C47 needs "non-alignment ‚üĻ a single
spike"; the data gives the opposite ‚ÄĒ full equidistribution = the random ‚ąö(n log p) value.** C47's
hope is that `c=arg J` being a coboundary on cyclic `‚Ą§/m` makes `arg ŌĄ(Ōá_j) =` (linear in `j`) +
(coboundary), a RIGID phase law whose DFT is one spike (height `m` at one frequency, near-zero
elsewhere ‚üĻ sup-cancellation past Johnson). The probe measures the DFT of the unit-phase family
`e^{i a_j}` directly (= the period itself): the off-spike-to-spike ratio is `dft_spread = 0.7735`
(`őľ=2`) and `0.9507` (`őľ=3`) ‚ÄĒ the phases are spread across essentially ALL frequencies, NOT a single
linear spike. The Ōá¬≤ uniformity statistic (`0.22 < 9.49` crit; `16.48 < 24.0` crit) confirms the
`arg ŌĄ` family is equidistributed on the circle. So the cocycle/coboundary solve recovers the
EQUIDISTRIBUTED argument distribution, whose sup over `b` is the random extreme-value `‚ąö(n log p)` ‚ÄĒ
which is the OPEN bound itself, not a proof of it. (This matches Heath-Brown‚ÄďPatterson cubic/Kummer
Gauss-sum argument equidistribution and the in-tree `_GrossKoblitzPhaseNoGo` finding that for `m‚Č•3`
the phase is "the wild Kummer quantity, not a residue formula" and equidistributes.)

**Horn 3 ‚ÄĒ the cocycle transfers NO cancellation: the floor sits at/above the random-phase value.**
The reconstructed sup `M=house` tracks `‚ąö(n log p)` to within 7%: `M/‚ąö(n log p) ‚ąą [0.93, 0.99]` over
12 prize-thin primes (`p/n¬≥‚ąą[1.00,1.16]`), with one row at `1.301` (n=32, p=32993) where the REAL
floor EXCEEDS the random value (100th percentile of the null). A coboundary is a per-frequency phase
shift; phase shifts cannot lower an L^‚ąě sup. The cocycle is consistent with zero cancellation
(`flat_target_below_cocycle_saturation`): the only bound the unimodular cocycle forces is the trivial
triangle bound `‚Č§ m`, and the flat target `‚ąö(n log p) ‚Č™ m` is strictly stronger and NOT implied.

**Why secretly-open (the hidden open step).** Solving the coboundary equation `őīa = arg J` for the
1-cochain `a` does NOT pin `a`: the solution is unique only up to a HOMOMORPHISM `‚Ą§/m ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ/2ŌÄ‚Ą§` (the
linear phase `őĽj` = the cohomology/homomorphism freedom). That freedom ‚ÄĒ the genuine archimedean
argument of `ŌĄ` modulo the magnitude ‚ÄĒ IS the open object: its distribution is the Kummer/cubic-and-up
Gauss-sum argument equidistribution, and the sup over `b` of the resulting period is the BGK/Paley
sup-norm `M(őľ_n) ‚Č§ C‚ąö(n log(p/n))`, the recognized ~25-year-open analytic-NT core. The cocycle fixes
the SYMMETRIC part (`a_j + a_k` data ‚ÜĒ the magnitude `|ŌĄ|=‚ąöq` ‚ÜĒ the norm `N=‚ąŹő∑_c` ‚ÜĒ the L¬≤
geometric-mean `‚Čą‚ąön` = the JOHNSON scale, exactly C27's P1) and leaves the antisymmetric/homomorphism
phase (the conjugate spread = the `‚ąö(log p)` prize factor) UNDETERMINED.

**Why not refuted-false:** the cocycle identity is genuinely true (it is the Jacobi relation), and the
true `M(őľ_n) ‚Čą ‚ąö(n log p)` IS the conjectured bound ‚ÄĒ C47 states the right value but the MECHANISM
(cocycle algebra) cannot prove it; it is exact-but-empty for the sup. Hence secretly-open, not false.

**Why not survives:** the past-Johnson content is entirely in the homomorphism-freedom/argument
distribution, which is the open BGK core under the route-elimination meta-theorem (no closed/
p-independent/cocycle object reaches past Johnson; the cocycle is p-independent algebra and caps at
the symmetric/L¬≤/Johnson scale).

**Mechanism class.** Archimedean-argument sibling of the C27 multiplicative-telescope horn: a product
(C27) computes a symmetric function (norm) blind to which conjugate is largest; a cocycle/coboundary
(C47) computes the symmetric argument relations blind to the homomorphism phase = which frequency
aligns. Both reach the `‚ąön` geometric-mean/Johnson scale; both leave the archimedean conjugate-spread
`‚ąö(log p)` = BGK open. Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_conjecture_refute_C47_cocycle_phase.py`, commit
`abec15e6e36a47da95a4125863bd5c14f6def9ac`. In-tree no-go bricks (axiom-clean): `_DyadicJacobiCocycleNonContraction.lean`, `_GrossKoblitzPhaseNoGo.lean`, `_PhaseAlignmentReality.lean`. Confidence high.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C40 "BCH/RS Weight-Distribution List Bound via MacWilliams Duality" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c40_macwilliams_bch_weightdist.py)

Conjecture C40 (issue #444, gmmds-homds, feasibility 2): the dual (BCH) weight distribution of
RS[k] over őľ_n, computed via MacWilliams + the proven antipodal/cyclotomic-coset structure, bounds
the number of low-weight dual words and hence the far-line list size below budget PAST Johnson.
REDUCES-TO: MacWilliams identity + BCH weight-distribution moments + in-tree cyclotomic-coset
structure + L3 orbit-count.

**Verdict: reduces-to-johnson** (with a secretly-open undertone on the only past-Johnson lever).
The MacWilliams / weight-distribution object is exactly the second-moment functional already in the
tree ‚ÄĒ domain-INDEPENDENT, its list bound is the Johnson/annulus bound, and it is provably blind to
the structure őī* depends on. Probe `scripts/probes/probe_c40_macwilliams_bch_weightdist.py` (exact
integer arithmetic mod prime p, proper subgroup őľ_n, p > n¬≥, NEVER the full group): all four
predictions pass.

**Horn 1 ‚ÄĒ the weight distribution is a DOMAIN-INDEPENDENT functional (it forgets őľ_n).** A weight
distribution `A_w = #{c : wt(c)=w}` is a permutation-invariant functional of the code; MacWilliams is
the exact Krawtchouk transform `A^‚ä• = (1/|C|)¬∑K¬∑A` between `C` and `C^‚ä•`. RS[k] over ANY n-subset of
F_p is MDS (in-tree `reedSolomonFrame_isMDS`; O109 CENSUS 1 confirms the empirical weight dist matches
the classical MDS closed form `A_w = C(n,w)¬∑ő£_j(‚ąí1)^j C(w,j)(q^{w‚ąíd+1‚ąíj}‚ąí1)` at every tested instance).
- **P1 (probe, empirical at small q):** `A_w(őľ_n) == A_w(random subset) == A_w(arithmetic-progression
  domain) == MDS formula` ‚ÄĒ IDENTICAL at every tested (n,k,q) (n=4,6; q=13,17; proper subgroups).
- **P2 (probe):** the MacWilliams transform of A[RS[k]] equals A[RS[n‚ąík]] (the dual GRS is again MDS),
  i.e. the "dual BCH weight distribution" is itself the SAME fixed MDS form ‚ÄĒ domain-blind. The
  antipodal/cyclotomic-coset structure lives in the DOMAIN, which the weight distribution has already
  forgotten; nothing about őľ_n survives the transform that was not already in (n,k,q). Verified
  n‚ąą{8,12,16}, k=4, p>n¬≥.

‚üĻ The entire MacWilliams/BCH-weight-distribution object depends only on (n,k,q). But őī* is provably
DOMAIN-DEPENDENT past Johnson (őľ_n is the worst case; BKR10 / BCHKS Thm 1.13). A functional that is
identical for őľ_n and for a random domain cannot pin the worst-case-over-őľ_n threshold.

**Horn 2 ‚ÄĒ its list bound IS the second-moment / Johnson bound, vacuous past Johnson.** The only list
bound a symmetric weight functional yields is the "many low-weight codewords in a ball" / annulus
count = the second moment. This is exactly the in-tree object: `AgreementMomentTwo.sum_agreement_
spectrum_sq` proves M2 "enters through the weight enumerator alone, which MDS pins" ‚ÄĒ the variance is
weight-distribution-determined and domain-independent. And `CandidateBridgeLoop16` proves its wall:
`second_moment_fails_in_band` / `sq_gt_iff_large_gap` ‚ÄĒ the second-moment denominator `a¬≤‚ąín¬∑b`
(`a=(1‚ąíőī)n`, `b=ŌĀn`) is `> 0` iff `(1‚ąíőī)¬≤>ŌĀ` iff `őī < 1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ` = Johnson, and `‚Č§ 0` (no bound) strictly
past Johnson.
- **P3 (probe):** for n=16, k‚ąą{2,4,8}, the denominator is `>0` at `őī_J‚ąí0.01` and `‚Č§0` at `őī_J+0.01`
  ‚ÄĒ the bound is silent over the ENTIRE window interior `(1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ, 1‚ąíŌĀ)` it is supposed to control.

**Horn 3 ‚ÄĒ the real far-line list is domain-DEPENDENT, invisible to the functional (SECRETLY-OPEN
undertone).** The far-line incidence `I(őī)` (the prize object) is NOT a permutation-invariant
functional of the weight distribution: a far line `x^a+őĪ x^b` close to RS[k] is a low-degree-pencil
object whose őĪ-count obeys the orbit-count law L3 (`I = N¬∑n/gcd(b‚ąía,n)`), depending on WHICH points the
domain uses.
- **P4 (probe, genuinely past Johnson):** over the actual subgroup őľ_n the structured bad-scalar set
  (the KK/O11‚Ä≥ mechanism, `bad őĪ = ‚ąíe‚āĀ(S)` over r-subsets S) DIFFERS from the same combinatorial count
  over a random domain (m=8,r=3: 40 vs 56; m=16,r=4: 1185 vs 1507; m=32,r=5: 32672 vs 32707; all at
  radii őī strictly past the effective Johnson radius). The far-line list distinguishes őľ_n from a
  random domain; the weight distribution (P1/P2: identical for both) provably cannot.

So to reach past Johnson C40 must inject a domain-DEPENDENT input ‚ÄĒ which is precisely the open
p-dependent BGK sup-norm `M(őľ_n) ‚Č§ C‚ąö(n¬∑log(p/n))` (faces 3‚ÜĒ4 of the open core). MacWilliams duality,
being a domain-blind symmetric transform, cannot supply it.

**Why not refuted-false:** MacWilliams duality and the MDS weight distribution are genuinely true and
formalized (in-tree O109/M2). C40 is not false; it is a TRUE-but-Johnson-only object whose past-Johnson
rider ("bounds the far-line list past Johnson") is unsubstantiated and would require the open BGK core.
Same horn-family as C41 (GS/MDS, reduces-to-johnson), C32/C21 (the past-Johnson lever is the open BGK
bound renamed).

**Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_c40_macwilliams_bch_weightdist.py`, commit pinned below. Python-only
(exact arithmetic mod prime); no Lean changed.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C41 "Guruswami-Sudan / Johnson is Tight for Explicit őľ_n + potential-function őė(1/log n) strip" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (degenerates to secretly-open on the past-Johnson lever)

Conjecture C41 (issue #444, gmmds-homds, feasibility 2): the GS list-decoding analysis combined
with proven MDS structure (`reedSolomonFrame_isMDS`) bounds the worst-case list at Johnson, and a
"refined potential-function argument" extends the bound a őė(1/log n) strip PAST Johnson for explicit
őľ_n. REDUCES-TO: Guruswami-Sudan + Johnson bound + `reedSolomonFrame_isMDS` + potential-function
refinement.

**Verdict: reduces-to-johnson** (the GS/MDS half is closed and TRUE but caps exactly at Johnson),
**degenerating to secretly-open** on the only past-Johnson lever (the unspecified
"potential-function refinement" IS the open BGK/line-ball core renamed). No probe needed ‚ÄĒ the
refutation is a logical reduction to ALREADY-FORMALIZED in-tree no-go theorems.

**Horn 1 ‚ÄĒ the GS half is machine-checked to cap at Johnson (CLOSED but Johnson-only).** ArkLib
already contains the EXACT two-sided characterization of the Guruswami-Sudan certificate system:
- `GSJohnson.gs_johnson_wall` (`GSJohnsonWall.lean:241`): GS feasibility forces `n(k‚ąí1) < t¬≤`, i.e.
  the certified agreement threshold `t > ‚ąö(n(k‚ąí1))` = the Johnson radius, **for EVERY multiplicity m
  and EVERY weighted degree D**. No GS parameter choice certifies agreement at or below Johnson.
- `GSExactWall.exact_wall` (`GSExactCountWall.lean:139`): even the EXACT lattice-point monomial count
  (the sharpest possible "refined" interpolation-space dimension ‚ÄĒ precisely what a "refined GS
  analysis" would use) keeps the same `‚ąö(c¬∑n)` wall: it reaches `t=60` vs Johnson's `50` at the
  witnessed instance, still STRICTLY above Johnson (`wall_consistency`, line 202), never below.
- `ArkLib.CodingTheory.GSReachesJohnson.t_close_to_johnson` (`GSReachesJohnson.lean`): the matching
  "reaches" direction ‚ÄĒ GS IS feasible at `t ‚Č§ ‚ąö(c¬∑n(1+1/m)) + 2`, converging to Johnson FROM ABOVE
  as `m‚Üí‚ąě`. So GS is `‚ąö(c¬∑n) < t_GS(m) ‚Č§ ‚ąö(c¬∑n(1+1/m))+2`: the machine is *CHARACTERIZED*, not merely
  bounded. The file's own docstring states it verbatim: "any certificate past Johnson for the prize
  must be a genuinely different system." `reedSolomonFrame_isMDS` enters only via the pairwise-agreement
  ‚Č§ n‚ąíd input to the second-moment Johnson list bound (`JohnsonList.johnson_list_bound`,
  `JohnsonListBound.lean:38`) ‚ÄĒ which is the canonical Johnson-capped object (`L¬∑(a¬≤‚ąínb) ‚Č§ n¬≤`). MDS
  sharpens the constant `b=n‚ąíd`, not the EXPONENT; it gives Johnson, not past-Johnson.

‚üĻ The "GS + Johnson bound + MDS" component pins őī* at exactly `1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ` (Johnson), not past it. This is
the standard list-decoding analysis, and it is closed ‚ÄĒ but it is the boundary, not the interior.

**Horn 2 ‚ÄĒ the only past-Johnson lever is the open core under a new name (SECRETLY-OPEN).** The
entire past-Johnson content of C41 is the single phrase "a refined potential-function argument
extends the bound a őė(1/log n) strip past Johnson." This object:
- Does NOT exist in-tree. `grep -rn "potential" ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/ProximityGap/*.lean`
  returns ZERO potential-function GS machinery; the only hit (`KKH26DimGeneralSharpPin.lean:28`)
  explicitly flags the `r ‚Č≤ ‚ąö(n¬∑ln n)` asymptotic potential of the sharp count as "a heuristic
  estimate, *not*" a proof. There is no `reducesTo` named brick C41 can cite.
- Is exactly the open object. The őė(1/log n) strip past Johnson is the window interior
  `(1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ, 1‚ąíŌĀ‚ąíőė(1/log n))` ‚ÄĒ the literal prize. Extending the worst-case far-line list bound from
  Johnson into that strip is the statement "the RS[k+1] candidate list concentrates to its first
  moment" (`MonomialLineListBridge.lean:37-38`, `LineListBound.lean:28-29`), which the route-elimination
  meta-theorem (L8) and the in-tree characterization both certify requires the **open p-dependent BGK
  sup-norm moment** `M(őľ_n) ‚Č§ C‚ąö(n log(p/n))`. A "potential function" that yields a őė(1/log n) strip
  is just the open BGK moment renamed ‚ÄĒ same wall as C32 (BSG-antipodal "sharpening" = the original
  open BGK bound under a new name) and C21 (the genpos lever is vacuous on the affinely-DEPENDENT
  worst-case far-line list, which IS the open BGK/line-ball core).

**Why not refuted-false:** the GS/MDS/Johnson half is genuinely true and formalized; C41 is not a
false claim, it is a TRUE-but-Johnson-only claim with an unsubstantiated past-Johnson rider. The
rider is not refuted (it is the open prize), it is simply not closed ‚ÄĒ so the honest horn is
reduces-to-johnson (closed core reaches only Johnson) with the past-Johnson lever secretly-open.
This is the single most common horn (matches C21's "secretly-open masquerading as closed via the
beyond-Johnson lever being vacuous/open on the worst-case object").

**Mechanism class.** Same family as every GS/algebraic-certificate attempt: the GS parameter
optimization has a HARD `‚ąö((k‚ąí1)n)` barrier proven in-tree at every multiplicity and even at the
exact-count refinement (`gs_johnson_wall`, `exact_wall`, `GSReachesJohnson`); the "potential-function
refinement" is the unproven past-Johnson lever = the open BGK line-ball core. No probe required ‚ÄĒ the
no-go is already machine-checked and axiom-clean (`#print axioms GSJohnson.gs_johnson_wall`,
`GSExactWall.exact_wall`, `GSReachesJohnson.t_close_to_johnson` all `[propext, Classical.choice,
Quot.sound]`).

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C39 "Tao-Croot-Lev-Pach for the Bad-Scalar Set as a Cap Configuration in ‚Ą§/p" REFUTED-FALSE / REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c39_clp_cap_badset.py, commit c3b9cd438)

Conjecture C39 (issue #444, additive-combinatorics, feasibility 2): claim the bad-scalar set
`B_w` is 3-AP-free / a cap configuration in `(‚Ą§/p,+)`, so the polynomial-method cap bound
(Croot‚ÄďLev‚ÄďPach / Ellenberg‚ÄďGijswijt) forces `|B_w| ‚Č§ p^{1‚ąíc}` for fixed `c>0`, which with the
gcd-coset law L3 caps the per-direction list below budget `n` past Johnson. REDUCES-TO: CLP/EG
cap bound + in-tree orbit-count law L3 + FactorizationRigidity.

**Verdict: refuted-false** (the cap bound is a category error / dimensionally vacuous in cyclic
`‚Ą§/p`), and the salvageable core is **reduces-to-johnson/circular** (it pins only the already-known
coset size `S`, never the open multiplicity `N`). Probe over proper `őľ_n`, `p` prime `p‚Čąn^4` (`p‚Čęn¬≥`,
never `n=p‚ąí1`), `n‚ąą{16,32,64,128}`. Three independent horns:

**(H1) The cap hypothesis is actually TRUE for the L3 object ‚ÄĒ and that is the trap, not the win.**
The single-codeword bad-őĪ set is, by orbit-count law L3, `B = {‚ąíz^{j(a‚ąíb)} : j} ‚äā F_p`, a GEOMETRIC
progression (multiplicative coset of `‚ü®z^{a‚ąíb}‚ü©` scaled by `‚ąí1`). The probe counts 3-APs `x+z=2y`
inside `B` and finds **zero nontrivial 3-APs in every tested direction** (all `(a,b)`, all `n`): a
geometric progression of roots of unity is generically 3-AP-free. So C39's hypothesis holds ‚ÄĒ but
the object it holds for is exactly the L3 coset, whose size is already known.

**(H2) The cap BOUND does not exist in cyclic `‚Ą§/p`.** CLP (`‚Ą§/4^N`) and Ellenberg‚ÄďGijswijt (`F_q^N`)
give an EXPONENTIAL-IN-AMBIENT-DIMENSION saving: a 3-AP-free subset of `F_q^N` has size `‚Č§ c_q^N`
with `c_q<q`, the saving living in `N‚Üí‚ąě`. `‚Ą§/p` is the `N=1` (one-dimensional) cyclic group ‚ÄĒ there
the polynomial/slice-rank method gives NOTHING beyond trivial. The only proven 3-AP-free bound in
`‚Ą§/p` is Behrend/Bloom‚ÄďSisask `r_3(p)=p/exp(c(log p)^t)=p^{1‚ąío(1)}`, which is **NOT** `p^{1‚ąíc}` for any
FIXED `c>0`. Importing the EG saving exponent into a cyclic group is the same category error as C05
(additive vs multiplicative): there it was "AP-in-exponent vs AP-in-`F_p`"; here it is "cap-bound-in-
`F_q^N` vs cap-bound-in-`‚Ą§/p`".

**(H3) Even granting the FALSE `p^{1‚ąíc}`, it is strictly WEAKER than the trivial in-tree L3 bound, and
bounds the wrong quantity.** With `n‚Čąp^{1/4}` the budget is `O(n)=O(p^{1/4})`; `p^{1‚ąíc}‚Čęn` for every
`c<3/4`. But L3 already gives `|B| = n/gcd(b‚ąía,n) ‚Č§ n` EXACTLY, for free. A cap bound of size `~p^{1‚ąíc}`
(‚Čę `n`) therefore caps nothing L3 does not already cap better. More decisively, a SIZE bound on the
cap `B` controls only the coset size `S`; the orbit-count law is `I_pencil = N_pencil ¬∑ S` and
`_B2DoorAOrbitCount.lean` flags `S` as **circular** ("#bad=O(n) is the prize floor itself"). The open
quantity is the MULTIPLICITY `N_pencil` (how many `deg<k` codewords realize the same bad őĪ / the full
gate over all codewords), which a cap/size bound on `B` cannot reach. So even a perfect cap bound
pins `S` (known) and leaves `N` (open BGK character-sum count) untouched ‚ÄĒ same horn as C05.

**Conclusion.** C39 is the cap-bound sibling of C05's Freiman-doubling claim. C05 conflated additive
vs multiplicative structure; C39 conflates the high-dimensional `F_q^N` polynomial method with cyclic
`‚Ą§/p` (no fixed-exponent cap bound exists there) AND, even granting the bound, only pins the already-
known coset size `S`, never the open multiplicity `N`. Refuted-false on the cap-bound step;
reduces-to-johnson/circular on whatever survives. Confidence high.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C14 "Kloosterman-Sheaf Purity on the Antipodal-Paired Dyadic Period (Katz Kl_{n/2}, Sp/SL)" REFUTED-FALSE (probe_c14_kloosterman_purity.py)

Conjecture C14 (issue #444, algebraic-geometry, feasibility 2): Mann's antipodal decomposition
`ő∑_b = 2 ő£ cos(2ŌÄ b x / p)` is a real cosine sum; claim it is the Frobenius trace of a
hyper-Kloosterman sheaf `Kl_{n/2}` with monodromy `Sp/SL`, so Deligne purity gives
`|ő∑_b| ‚Č§ 2‚ąö(n‚ąí1)` ‚ÄĒ stronger than the prize target, pinning `őī*` past Johnson. REDUCES-TO:
Katz GKM Ch. 11 (Kl_m monodromy Sp_m/SL_m) + Deligne Weil II + Mann/Conway‚ÄďJones antipodal
(in-tree) + in-tree `AntipodalEvenOddDescent`.

Verdict: **refuted-false** ‚ÄĒ an explicit, reproducible counterexample at EVERY prize `n`. Probe
`scripts/probes/probe_c14_kloosterman_purity.py`, commit `1af1e83a64ee408ac16368ff4e33fded4492c952`.
All rows measured prize-faithfully: PROPER `őľ_n` (`n = 2^őľ`), `p` PRIME, `n | p‚ąí1`, `p ‚Čę n¬≥`,
`m = (p‚ąí1)/n ‚Čę 1`, NEVER `n = p‚ąí1`.

| n | p | p/n¬≥ | m=(p‚ąí1)/n | M=max\|ő∑_b\| | C14 bound 2‚ąö(n‚ąí1) | M/bound |
|---|---|------|-----------|-------------|-------------------|---------|
| 8   | 4129     | 8.1  | 516    | 7.558  | 5.292  | 1.43√ó |
| 16  | 65537    | 16.0 | 4096   | 13.838 | 7.746  | 1.79√ó |
| 32  | 1048609  | 32.0 | 32769  | 22.983 | 11.136 | 2.06√ó |
| 64  | 16777601 | 64.0 | 262150 | 38.529 | 15.875 | 2.43√ó |
| 128 | 14605697 | 7.0  | 114107 | 49.461 | 22.539 | 2.19√ó |

Cleanest witness: `n=16, p=65537` (Fermat prime, proper `őľ_16`, `m=4096`): `max|ő∑_b| = 13.84`
(real, `Im = ‚ąí2.2e‚ąí15`, so Mann's antipodal decomposition is CORRECT) at `b*=16`, versus the C14
bound `2‚ąö15 = 7.75` ‚ÄĒ a **1.79√ó violation**. The Mann/Conway‚ÄďJones real-valuedness premise holds;
the Kloosterman-purity *inference* from it is false.

HORN ‚ÄĒ OBJECT MISMATCH (the wrong sheaf; `ő∑_b` is NOT a hyper-Kloosterman trace).
- The hyper-Kloosterman sum is `Kl_m(a;p) = ő£_{x_1¬∑¬∑¬∑x_m = a, x_i ‚ąą F_p^*} e_p(x_1+¬∑¬∑¬∑+x_m)`. It is
  the trace of the Kloosterman sheaf `Kl_m`, **rank `m`**, pure of weight `m‚ąí1`, monodromy `Sp_m`
  (m even)/`SL_m` (m odd). Deligne purity gives `|Kl_m| ‚Č§ m ¬∑ p^{(m‚ąí1)/2}` ‚ÄĒ a **p-DEPENDENT** bound.
  For `m=2` (classical, Sp_2) that is `2‚ąöp`, NOT `2‚ąö(n‚ąí1)`. There is no instantiation of Kloosterman
  purity that yields a `p-independent` edge `2‚ąö(n‚ąí1)`: the `‚ąö` in Deligne purity is `‚ąöp`, the field
  size, not `‚ąö(rank)` or `‚ąö(# terms)`.
- `ő∑_b = ő£_{y‚ąąőľ_n} Ōą(by)` is a **cyclotomic Gauss period** (a partial additive-character sum over a
  *multiplicative subgroup*), a fundamentally different object. Its correct sheaf-theoretic home is the
  Kummer/hypergeometric Gauss-sum FAMILY whose geometric monodromy is **`GL(1)^f`** (Rojas-Le√≥n
  arXiv:2207.12439, Katz [Kat88]; in-tree `KatzEffectiveGaussSum.lean`, `MonodromyConductorScaffold.lean`,
  `docs/kb/deltastar-407-katz-monodromy-research-2026-06-13.md`), giving an **asymptotically-independent
  GAUSSIAN (UNBOUNDED) law** ‚ÄĒ `M(n) ‚Čą ‚ąö(n log(p/n))` with NO hard `‚ąö`-edge. The conjecture's premise
  that there is a single Sp/SL Kloosterman sheaf with a bounded edge contradicts the established
  GL(1)^f monodromy of the actual family.

HORN ‚ÄĒ THE MEASURED LAW HAS NO EDGE. `M/‚ąön` GROWS monotonically `2.67 ‚Üí 3.46 ‚Üí 4.06 ‚Üí 4.82` (n=8‚Üí64),
the signature of a `‚ąölog`-type Gaussian large deviation (Kluyver/random-walk; in-tree
`deltastar-407-new-ideas-katz-monodromy-framing-2026-06-13.md` line 9‚Äď11), NOT a fixed semicircle/
Sato‚ÄďTate edge `2‚ąön` (which would force `M/‚ąön ‚Üí 2`). A bounded-`2‚ąö(n‚ąí1)` edge is empirically absent.

Even granting (counterfactually) the bound, `2‚ąö(n‚ąí1) = őė(‚ąön)` would sit at the additive-energy/Johnson
scale `‚ąöS` (meta-theorem L8: 2nd-order/‚ąöS objects cap at Johnson) ‚ÄĒ so on its own arithmetic it could
not pin `őī*` PAST Johnson regardless; the route-elimination meta-theorem already excludes any closed
`p-independent ‚ąön` edge from reaching past Johnson. But that is moot: the bound is simply FALSE.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c14_kloosterman_purity.py` (self-contained, sympy GF(p);
coset-reduced exact sup-norm sweep). Commit `1af1e83a64ee408ac16368ff4e33fded4492c952`. Sibling to the
C29 Kowalski‚ÄďSawin Kloosterman-path entry below (same object-mismatch family: the prize period is a
multiplicative-subgroup Gauss period, not any Kloosterman-sheaf object).

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C29 "Kowalski‚ÄďSawin Kloosterman-Path Functional CLT Sup-Bound" SECRETLY-OPEN / REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c29_kowalski_sawin_path_clt.py)

Conjecture C29 (issue #444, algebraic-geometry, feasibility 2): apply the Kowalski‚ÄďSawin
functional CLT for partial-sum *paths* to the period family and claim the sub-Gaussian sup of the
limiting path process bounds `max_c |ő∑_c| ‚Č§ ‚ąö(2 n log p)` past Johnson, via the proven
large-monodromy support theorem (Deligne Weil II + functional CLT). REDUCES-TO: Kowalski‚ÄďSawin
Kloosterman-paths + Deligne Weil II + large-monodromy support theorem.

Verdict: **secretly-open** (degenerating to **reduces-to-johnson** on the only effective reading).
The import fails on three independent, mutually reinforcing horns, all measured prize-faithfully
(PROPER `őľ_n`, `n = 2^őľ`, `p` PRIME, `p ‚Čę n¬≥`, NEVER `n = p‚ąí1`). Probe
`scripts/probes/probe_c29_kowalski_sawin_path_clt.py`, commit `702858d5d35cddf2447a0283c9e886efb082f070`.
Sibling to the REFUTED Brick-Ledger #1 ("Conjecture (G) periods jointly sub-Gaussian var n") and the
Salem‚ÄďZygmund / Lamzouri-CLT route (`deltastar-salem-zygmund-gausssum-chaining-2026-06-13.md`).

HORN A ‚ÄĒ OBJECT MISMATCH (the named theorem governs a *different* random object). The
Kowalski‚ÄďSawin path process is `K_p(t) = (1/‚ąöp) ő£_{0‚Č§a‚Č§tp} Kl(a;1,1;p) ‚ąą C([0,1])` ‚ÄĒ the
**partial-sum path of ONE Kloosterman sum indexed by the summation variable `a`**, normalized by
`‚ąöp`. Its functional CLT (Deligne equidistribution as `p‚Üí‚ąě` for the FIXED Kloosterman sheaf) gives a
sub-Gaussian sup `sup_t |K_p(t)|`, which is the maximal *partial sum*, an O(1)-scale fluctuation of a
length-`p` walk. The prize object is `max_{c‚Č†0} |ő∑_c|` where `ő∑_c = ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n} e_p(g^c x)` is the
**COMPLETE (full) period sum** and the max runs over the **dual frequency index `c ‚ąą ‚Ą§/m`**. Measured:
path-sup = 0.74‚Äď0.88 (Weil O(1) scale) versus period-sup = 7.4‚Äď15.8 (the n-term object) at the SAME
`(p,n)`. They are governed by different processes; the path CLT does not bound `max_c|ő∑_c|`.

| p | n | m | period max\|ő∑_c\| | ‚ąö(n log p) | ratio | KS path-sup \|K_p\| |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 193 | 16 | 12 | 7.432 | 9.176 | 0.810 | 0.876 |
| 769 | 16 | 48 | 9.100 | 10.311 | 0.883 | 0.741 |
| 3329 | 16 | 208 | 11.085 | 11.392 | 0.973 | 0.828 |

HORN B ‚ÄĒ EFFECTIVITY GAP (the decisive horn; the CLT is asymptotic-in-`p`, not effective at fixed
prize `q`). The Kowalski‚ÄďSawin CLT is a distributional limit as `p‚Üí‚ąě` at FIXED conductor/sheaf; the
sub-Gaussian sup of the LIMIT process bounds the LIMIT, not the fixed-`q` tail with an explicit
constant. The sub-Gaussian variance proxy `c(p,n) = 2 ln MGF(t*) / t*¬≤` does NOT lock to `n`: over a
ő≤-sweep at `n=8` it sits at proxy/n = 0.77 ‚Üí 0.76 ‚Üí 0.72 and **drifts** as ő≤ = log_n p grows. A CLT
that had landed effectively would force proxy/n ‚Üí 1 with a `p`-independent constant; the observed drift
is exactly the signature that the limit is approached only as `p‚Üí‚ąě`, i.e. **ineffective at the prize's
fixed (large) `q`** ‚ÄĒ the same effectivity barrier already recorded for the Katz/Rojas-Le√≥n Gauss-sum
equidistribution route (`deltastar-407-katz-monodromy-research-2026-06-13.md`).

| p | n | ő≤ | m | max\|ő∑\| | ‚ąö(n ln p) | ratio | proxy/n |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2081 | 8 | 3.67 | 260 | 7.277 | 7.818 | 0.931 | 0.766 |
| 4129 | 8 | 4.00 | 516 | 7.558 | 8.161 | 0.926 | 0.755 |
| 32801 | 8 | 5.00 | 4100 | 7.843 | 9.121 | 0.860 | 0.717 |

HORN C ‚ÄĒ GROWING-DIMENSION OBSTRUCTION (single fixed sheaf vs growing family). Via the DFT identity
`ő∑_c = ‚ąí1/m + (1/m) ő£_{j=1}^{m‚ąí1} ŌĄ(Ōá_j) e(‚ąíjc/m)`, the period is a sum of `m‚ąí1 ‚Čą p/n` Gauss sums ‚ÄĒ
a family whose **dimension / conductor grows with `p`** (the same large-sieve dimension obstruction
noted for the FKM/Bombieri large-values route, `deltastar-407-large-sieve-dimension-obstruction-2026-06-13.md`).
The large-monodromy support theorem is invoked for ONE fixed sheaf; it does not deliver a uniform
sub-Gaussian sup over a `(p/n)`-growing family. Measured `max_c|ő∑_c|/‚ąö(n log p) ‚ąą [0.86, 0.99]` ‚ÄĒ the
period-sup tracks the **random / Salem‚ÄďZygmund value** `‚ąö(n log p)`. That scale IS the open BGK core:
the target the CLT wants to deliver is precisely the random-model derandomization that is open
everywhere (`deltastar-gate-is-sqrt-cancellation-BGK-fullpower-short-2026-06-13.md`).

The deeper structural point. C29 names real theorems (Kowalski‚ÄďSawin Kloosterman-paths, Adv. Math.
2016; Deligne Weil II) but applies them off-object and off-regime. The genuine probabilistic content
the prize needs ‚ÄĒ a sub-Gaussian tail with proxy `O(n)` for the GROWING `(m‚ąí1)`-term Gauss-sum DFT,
uniform and EFFECTIVE at fixed `n ‚Čą p^{1/8}` ‚ÄĒ is exactly the residual already isolated as
"Lamzouri's CLT one regime further" (extend the 2-D Gaussian value-distribution CLT from length
`p^{o(1)}` to length `p^{ő≤}` at fixed `ő≤`), which is the Bourgain barrier = the open BGK ‚ąö-cancellation.
The Kloosterman-path CLT supplies neither the right object (HORN A), the effectivity (HORN B), nor the
growing-family uniformity (HORN C). Sub-Gaussian sup-control of a single-sheaf path process caps at the
random-model `‚ąö(n log p)` value asymptotically ‚ÄĒ and that asymptotic value, made worst-case at fixed
`q`, is the open core that itself sits at the Johnson‚ÜĒcapacity boundary the meta-theorem reserves for
the open p-dependent regime. No new non-BGK lever is delivered.

Probe committed: `scripts/probes/probe_c29_kowalski_sawin_path_clt.py` (self-contained, sympy/numpy
GF(p); three horns: object mismatch, ő≤-sweep proxy drift, random-scale tracking; proper `őľ_n`,
`p` prime, `p ‚Čę n¬≥`, never `n=p‚ąí1`).

---

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C24 "Relaxed-rMDS additive-inflation" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c24_relaxed_rmds_additive.py)

Conjecture C24 (issue #444, gmmds-homds): use BGM's relaxed `MDS(L+1) ‚üĻ list ‚Č§ L` and claim the
in-tree corank deficiency `őė(a)` only ADDITIVELY inflates the list (`L_relaxed = L_generic + őė(a)`),
with `őė(a¬≤)` slack `<` budget at prize scale, pinning `őī* ‚Č• 1‚ąíŌĀ‚ąíc'/log n` past Johnson.
Verdict: **reduces-to-johnson** (with a secretly-open undertone). Two independent defects, both
measured prize-faithfully (PROPER őľ_n, p PRIME, p ‚Čą 8n¬≥ ‚Čę n¬≥, n < p‚ąí1). Probe
`scripts/probes/probe_c24_relaxed_rmds_additive.py`, commit `9daa05149`. Sibling to C23/C43/C44.

DEFECT 1 ‚ÄĒ "additive inflation" MIS-READS BGM's relaxed theorem (the fatal semantics error).
BGM's relaxed bound is `list ‚Č§ (relaxation order) ‚ąí 1`: the list size IS the order at which the code
is still MDS, minus one. The CORANK is precisely the deficiency in the order at which MDS HOLDS ‚ÄĒ
`MDS(‚Ąď)` holds ‚üļ every order-‚Č§‚Ąď generalized-Vandermonde minor is full rank ‚üļ corank 0 at all
orders ‚Č§ ‚Ąď. A corank-`d` deficiency at order `a` means `MDS(a)` FAILS, so the smallest `L` for which
the relaxed hypothesis `MDS(L+1)` holds forces `L+1 ‚Č• a`, giving `list ‚Č§ a‚ąí1 = őė(a) = őė(n)`. The
corank does NOT add on top of a generic `O(1/ő∑)` list; it SETS the relaxation order, collapsing the
bound to the TRIVIAL `őė(n)` counting list (= the whole far-coset orbit; orbit-count L3: `I ‚Č§ n`).
`L_relaxed = L_generic + őė(a)` is a category error.

DEFECT 2 ‚ÄĒ the corank IS `őė(a) = őė(n)`, confirmed exactly (so even C24's own premise gives `őė(n)`).
The in-tree law `corank_Fp(őľ_a;E) = a ‚ąí #distinct(e mod a)` reproduced **5400/5400 EXACT, char-faithful**
over proper őľ_n at p ‚Čą 8n¬≥. On the binding interior coset `a = n/2` the max list-relevant corank is
`d = n/4 = őė(n)` (measured: 2, 4, 8, 16 for n = 8, 16, 32, 64). So the relaxation order needed is
`őė(n)` ‚üĻ `list ‚Č§ őė(n)`. `őė(a¬≤) ~ n¬≤` "slack" is moot: the OBJECT being bounded is already `őė(n)`
= the whole orbit = the trivial counting bound = the open BGK/counting regime, NOT a poly,
below-orbit, between-Johnson-and-capacity pin.

NET. The only regime where relaxed-MDS yields a USEFUL (poly, sub-orbit) list is order ‚Č§ the generic
MDS order = Johnson. Past Johnson the structural corank of őľ_n is `őė(a)` (the antipodal/HOMDS horn of
the route-elimination meta-theorem), so the honest relaxed-MDS bound is the trivial `őė(n)`. Beating it
needs the open p-dependent count of the actual far-coset list (= the BGK/Paley wall). C24 relocates,
does not remove, the wall. DO NOT re-claim "relaxed rMDS_d additively inflates the őľ_n list by `őė(a)`
with `őė(a¬≤)` slack ‚üĻ őī* past Johnson" ‚ÄĒ the corank sets the order, not the slack, and the bound is
trivial. Same wall as P7 (DISPROOF_LOG line ~99), C23, C43, C44.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C26 "Stickelberger-House" SECRETLY-OPEN (probe_c26_stickelberger_house.py)

Conjecture C26 (issue #444): the dyadic Gauss-period house (= max archimedean conjugate
modulus = M(őľ_n), the open object) is ‚Č§ C‚ąö(n log p) via the EXACT Stickelberger ideal
factorization of ŌĄ(Ōá), which "forces the conjugates to be BALANCED without any moment/
equidistribution input" (Duke‚ÄďGarcia house framework). Verdict: **secretly-open** ‚ÄĒ it
hides the open BGK/Paley sup-norm step. Reconfirms two prior 2026-06-14 entries below
(Galois/house + p-adic/Stickelberger REFUTED; HANDLE A3 REFUTED) with a fresh prize-shaped probe.

DECISIVE OBSTRUCTION (two independent, both measured at p~n^4 ‚Čę n^3, n=2^őľ, NEVER n=p-1):
- **Stickelberger is Galois-INVARIANT on the conjugate orbit.** The periods {ő∑_c} are the full
  Galois orbit of one algebraic integer; Stickelberger gives each conjugate the SAME prime-ideal
  valuation profile (they are Galois-conjugate ideals; val = base-p digit-sum, Gross‚ÄďKoblitz exact).
  So the p-adic data carries ZERO information about WHICH conjugate is largest:
  corr(|ő∑_c|, p-adic valuation) = 0.020, 0.022, 0.019, ‚ąí0.004 (n=8,16,32,64). The archimedean
  size is the UNIT/root-of-unity (phase) factor of ŌĄ(Ōá) ‚ÄĒ explicitly NOT pinned by Stickelberger
  (sign/unit-root ambiguity open even for index-2,4 Gauss sums; Yang‚ÄďXia, Aoki).
- **The conjugates are NOT balanced** (the claim's load-bearing step is false). house/geomMean =
  5.0, 6.4, 6.2, 7.2 (GROWING with n); house/min ‚Čą 2785 .. 14498. Balance would give house/geom‚Čą1.
  The norm |N|^{1/m} (= geomMean, what Duke‚ÄďGarcia/Habegger compute) UNDER-estimates the house by
  the growing factor 5‚Äď7√ó. Norm ‚Üõ house unless conjugates are flat = the open equidistribution.
- **Object is real but bound undelivered.** house ‚Čą 3.4‚Äď3.8¬∑‚ąön ‚Čą ‚ąö(n log p) (below trivial n) ‚ÄĒ so
  the TARGET shape is correct, but Stickelberger delivers only the p-adic valuation, not the ‚ąön
  archimedean cancellation. Pinning house from norm = forcing balance = the open BGK/Paley sup-norm
  (best PROVEN n^{1‚ąío(1)} BGK; di Benedetto n^{0.989} only n>p^{1/4}, outside the prize).
Probe: scripts/probes/probe_c26_stickelberger_house.py, commit 16a4ab116. Same wall as A3/Galois.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C27 "Hasse‚ÄďDavenport Dyadic-Lifting Telescope" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON / SECRETLY-OPEN (probe_c27_hassedavenport_telescope.py)

Conjecture C27 (issue #444, gauss-period-exact): the 2-power Gauss-sum tower `őľ_2‚äā‚čĮ‚äāőľ_{2^őľ}`
linked by Hasse‚ÄďDavenport lifting telescopes the period sup-norm `M(őľ_n)` into a PRODUCT of
Jacobi sums (each `|J|=‚ąöp`), the `2^őľ` levels contributing `‚ąö(log p)`, giving
`M(őľ_n)‚Č§‚ąö(n log p)` PAST Johnson via a purely MULTIPLICATIVE recursion (claimed non-analytic,
hence outside the moment-wall impossibility map). Reduces-to: HD product/lifting (exact) +
`ŌĄ(Ōá)ŌĄ(Ōá')=J(Ōá,Ōá')ŌĄ(ŌáŌá')`, `|J|=‚ąöp` (proven). Verdict: **reduces-to-johnson (= secretly-open)**.

This is the MULTIPLICATIVE-telescope twin of C26 (Stickelberger, above) and was already
promoted-then-refuted as **C9** by the algebraic-route loop
(`docs/kb/deltastar-conjecture-loop-algebraic-route-2026-06-13.md`, lines 80‚Äď89). Same fatal
fact: `M(H)=max_{a‚Č†0}|ő∑_a|=HOUSE(ő∑)` (max conjugate modulus), and any product/telescope (HD,
Jacobi, norm) computes only a SYMMETRIC function of the `m=(p‚ąí1)/n` conjugates `{ő∑_c}` ‚ÄĒ
chiefly the NORM `N=‚ąŹ_c ő∑_c`. A product is structurally BLIND to which conjugate is largest. By
AM‚ÄďGM the norm gives only `|N|^{1/m}‚Čą‚ąön` = the geometric mean = the L¬≤-average = the JOHNSON
scale, i.e. a LOWER bound `M(H)‚Č•‚ąön`, useless as the claimed `‚ąö(n log p)` UPPER bound. The
`‚ąö(log p)` prize factor IS the conjugate spread = the archimedean argument distribution
`{arg ŌĄ(Ōá_j)}` = the BGK/Paley open core, which no multiplicative recursion supplies.

DECISIVE PROBE (all distinct conjugate periods, proper `őľ_n`, `n=2^őľ`, őľ=3..6, `p` prime,
`p>n¬≥`, NEVER `n=p‚ąí1`, 5 primes/n):
- **P1 geometric mean = ‚ąön (Johnson scale):** `gm/‚ąön ‚Čą 0.50‚Äď0.59` uniformly for all n ‚ÄĒ the norm
  (everything a telescope yields) sits at the ‚ąön L¬≤-average.
- **P2 prize factor = conjugate spread, invisible to products:** `house/gm` GROWS 4.2‚Üí5.3‚Üí6.3‚Üí7.5
  (n=8‚Üí16‚Üí32‚Üí64), tracking `‚ąö(log(p/n))`. The ‚ąö(log p) gap is real and the product misses it.
- **P3 DECISIVE ‚ÄĒ norm does NOT determine house:** at FIXED n, `gm_spread ‚Č§ 0.22` across primes
  while `house_spread = 0.34, 0.70, 6.78, 7.24` (n=8,16,32,64). Two fields with essentially equal
  `|N|` have `M(H)` differing by 6‚Äď7. A product/telescope CANNOT pin the sup.

So the HD/Jacobi telescope is exact and proven but reaches only the ‚ąön geometric-mean = Johnson
scale (reduces-to-johnson); the past-Johnson upper bound `‚ąö(n log p)` (the TRUE value) needs the
archimedean conjugate-spread = BGK sup-norm open core (secretly-open). "Non-analytic ‚üĻ off the
moment wall" is correct but irrelevant: the bound never leaves the L¬≤/Johnson scale ‚ÄĒ it lands on
a DIFFERENT wall (the house-vs-norm archimedean gap). NOT refuted-false (the true M(őľ_n)‚Čą‚ąö(n log p));
the MECHANISM cannot prove it. Probe: scripts/probes/probe_c27_hassedavenport_telescope.py,
commit aa781ec66. Same wall as C26/C9/A3 (the house‚Č†norm archimedean gap).

Goal: keep trying to **disprove** the ABF26 Grand-Challenge-1 conjecture, then
**disprove the disproof**. Record every attempt so we don't repeat ourselves and
so we zero in. Keep lemmas that *constrain* even if they don't fully disprove.
Default assumption: my disproof is wrong ‚ÄĒ find the precise reason it fails and
make that reason a sorry-free Lean lemma.
