### O133 ‚ÄĒ THE THIRD MOMENT IS DOMAIN-DEPENDENT: M3 of the agreement spectrum separates smooth subgroups from random domains at k=3, the pencil census is the separating invariant (normalizer spike law + a spectral gap), and k=2 is exactly rigid (moments lane, 2026-06-11)

`scripts/probes/moments/` ‚ÄĒ pre-registered hypothesis ledger (HYPOTHESES-M3.md, written
before any probe ran) ‚Üí two independent engines ‚Üí exact cross-validation ‚Üí the
separation experiment ‚Üí RESULTS-M3.md. All exact integers, no sampling on any verdict.

**The reduction (H4, now probe-proven):** `M3[j‚āĀ,j‚āā,j‚āÉ] = ő£_u a_{j‚āĀ}a_{j‚āā}a_{j‚āÉ} =
q^k¬∑ő£_{ordered pairs (c,c')} N(profile)` through the per-coordinate 5-type census
(common zeros / c-only / c'-only / c=c'‚Č†0 / split), with N a 3-variable coefficient
extractor. Independent pairs organize by 2-dim subcodes = dual points ŌÜ ‚ąą PG(2,q);
at k=3 the basis-map fibers are the orbits of the M√∂bius involution
`ŌÜ‚āÄxy ‚ąí ŌÜ‚āĀ(x+y) + ŌÜ‚āā = 0` on D (sizes ‚Č§ 2), so each pencil carries (A, s, t‚āā) =
(common zeros, support, #2-fibers); ordered bases ‚ÜĒ (q‚ąí1)¬∑(ordered distinct point
triples) by PGL‚āā sharp 3-transitivity. **M3 is exactly a function of the pencil
(A,s,t‚āā)-histogram + the (MDS-pinned) weight distribution.** Engines cross-validated
byte-exactly on 8 setups (k ‚ąą {2,3}; q ‚ąą {5,7,11,13}; subgroup and non-subgroup
domains); internal gates include the ordered-pair partition of q^{2k}, MDS weights
(closed form == enumeration), ő£_ŌÜ t‚āā = C(n,2)(q‚ąí1) on EVERY domain (the H5 pinning:
any M3 difference is t‚āā-variance, never mean), N(profile) vs brute word counts, the
full ordered-pair profile histogram vs the class decomposition, a GL‚āā brute check of
the basis-counting lemma, M1/M2 closed-form marginals, S3 symmetry, total mass.

**Verdicts (full tables in RESULTS-M3-RAW.md):**
* **H2 ‚ÄĒ k=2 is exactly rigid:** subgroup vs random tensors EXACTLY equal (q=41, n=8).
  Mechanism: pencil maps are injective, all fibers singletons, three special points,
  sharp 3-transitivity ‚ÄĒ a clean Lean target.
* **H1 ‚ÄĒ k=3 separates:** the subgroup sits outside the random cloud at every cell ‚ÄĒ
  3.4√ó the cloud diameter at (41,8), 14.1√ó at (113,16), 10.8√ó at (257,16), ‚ąě at
  (41,10) where three random 10-subsets had literally identical M3 (small-q histogram
  concentration) yet the subgroup differs. Same sign everywhere (subgroup HIGHER),
  argmax always (j‚āĀ,j‚āā,j‚āÉ) = (k‚ąí1,k‚ąí1,k‚ąí1).
* **A5 ‚ÄĒ the spike law is the torus normalizer, exactly (n ‚Č• 10):** the subgroup's
  big-spike pencils are EXACTLY {x ‚Ü¶ c/x : c ‚ąą H} ‚ą™ {x ‚Ü¶ ‚ąíx} (n+1 pencils,
  t‚āā ‚ąą {(n‚ąí2)/2, n/2} by the fixed-point count); set equality machine-verified at
  (41,10), (113,16), (257,16); at n=8 the t‚āā=3 band also catches 8 non-normalizer
  involutions (small-scale noise, gone by n=10).
* **The spectral gap (unplanned find):** subgroup pencil spectra at n=16 have NO
  pencil with t‚āā ‚ąą {4,5,6} at BOTH q=113 and q=257 ‚ÄĒ noise band capped at 3, the
  normalizer band isolated at {7,8}; random domains fill the gap with a decaying
  tail. Conjecture (Weil on (1,1)-curves vs subgroup characters, the MSS CJM-2018
  Cor 4.1 energy line): for q ‚Č≥ n¬≤, non-normalizer t‚āā = O(n¬≤/q + 1) ‚ÄĒ the gap is a
  theorem in waiting.
* **Affine/coset invariance (exact, lemma-grade):** D = gH has M3 IDENTICAL to H;
  p ‚Ü¶ p(ax+b) permutes the code, so ALL agreement moments are invariant under the
  affine action on domains ‚ÄĒ "smooth coset" ‚Č° "smooth subgroup" to every M_r.
* **A4 ‚ÄĒ fingerprint, partial:** AP domains separate at n=16 (3.7‚Äď3.9√ó, additive
  pencils x+y=c as predicted) but not at (41,8); the multiplicative-but-not-subgroup
  gpset does not separate at (41,8). Multiplicative > additive > none, at matched
  parameters.
* **A3 honesty:** relative deviations are tiny ‚ÄĒ 1.9e‚ąí11 (q=113), 5.6e‚ąí13 (q=257),
  scaling ~q‚ĀĽ‚Āī at fixed n. M3 SEES the domain; whether anything at this magnitude
  can move a 2‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬≤‚Āł-resolution tail is the open quantification (expected: no).

**Position:** complements O131 (their remark ‚ÄĒ pair content absent from a_j moments ‚ÄĒ
is the fixed-word setting; over ALL received words the r-th moment decomposes over
(r‚ąí1)-tuples of codewords, and r = 3 is where domain-dependence starts). M1/M2
domain-independence is implicit in CS25/DG25/Gao‚ÄďLi (lit-gated); the smooth-anomaly
PHENOMENON is BKR-2010/BCHKS-¬ß1.4.3-anticipated. New here: a finite computable
statistic with the exact first level (k,r) = (3,3), its mechanism fully classified.

**Named follow-ups:** the M2 Lean brick (statements already numeric-gated:
`pairAgreementCount` + `sum_agreement_spectrum_sq` + the per-word bound; proofs in
flight); the k=2 rigidity theorem; the affine-invariance lemma; the spectral-gap
theorem; the (k,r) = (2,4) cross-ratio cell of the moduli law (next falsifier);
the őĒM3 closed form written as mathematics.

**#357 N3 regression landed:** `scripts/probes/moments/probe_m3_spectral_gap_regression.py`
now validates the stored exact subgroup spectra without recomputing M3: H5 mean pinning,
the exact A5 normalizer big-spike set, and the `n=16` spectral gap
`t‚āā ‚ąČ {4,5,6}` at `q=113,257`.  This is not the Weil proof; it is the stable finite target
that the spectral-gap theorem must explain.

### O134 ‚ÄĒ THE PER-PRIME FALSIFIER FINDS THE TRANSFER FAILING: +11/+54 spurious marginal codewords at BabyBear/p‚āā at n=64 ‚ÄĒ the char-0 counts are exact, the per-prime lists carry a measured prime-specific surplus; PLUS the witness-layer count formalized in-tree (nubs, 2026-06-11)

Two deliverables (full dossiers: `scripts/probes/genlaw/falsifier/RESULTS.md`, new module
`WitnessLayerCount.lean`); both adversarially verified (independent verifier legs, 0.93).

* **The falsifier.** O130 caveat 2 said the n=64 exhaustive per-prime census was infeasible
  (C(64,33) ‚Čą 7√ó10¬Ļ‚Ā∑). It isn't: the consistency equation is LINEAR in the B-subset sum
  (coeff(X^s) of e ‚üĻ ő£_{c‚ąąB} ő∂^{2c} = z* ‚ąí e‚āā(x) ‚ąí e‚āĀ(O_z)), so per (O,ŌÉ) class an exact
  meet-in-the-middle subset-sum scan covers ALL classes ‚ÄĒ including char-0-infeasible ones.
  Result at pattern (15,3), all 19,840 classes: **BabyBear mod-p count = 764,555 = char-0
  764,544 + 11 spurious (2 classes); p‚āā = 764,598 = +54 (10 classes)** ‚ÄĒ the O130
  "predicted 0" mod-p transfer FAILS at both production primes. Every spurious config
  reconstructed end-to-end: genuine monic deg-34 word, coeff(X^33)=0, coeff(X^32)=őĽ,
  agreement EXACTLY 33, NOT char-0 balanced; mechanism = p | N(őĪ) for bad lattice vectors
  őĪ of L1-norm 14‚Äď18 (at p‚āā, six of ten classes share one őĪ). Calibration tower: n=16 and
  n=32 exact (672 = 672 at both primes ‚ÄĒ matches the exhaustive O98 census, zero spurious);
  reduction identity pinned vs raw polynomial arithmetic 720/720; per-class char-0 counts ==
  audit_sweep64 DP at s=8/16/32; small-prime positive controls (p=97 n=32: +38,716;
  p=193 n=64: +7.97√ó10‚ĀĻ) land on the uniform heuristic to 0.01%. Verifier leg: line-by-line
  code review + from-scratch dict-MITM reproduction of flagged classes + 56-class
  independent recount ‚ÄĒ sound, no fatal/major. **Interpretation: the prize-relevant
  per-prime bad-list sizes at n=64 are the char-0 counts plus a TINY measured prime-specific
  surplus (1.4√ó10‚ĀĽ‚ĀĶ relative at BabyBear) ‚ÄĒ the forecastability of the char-0 layer survives;
  exact per-prime work must add the norm-divisibility correction.** r=5 stratum scan
  (3,222,016 classes) in flight; odd r ‚Č• 7 unscanned mod p.
* **`WitnessLayerCount.lean`** (7 theorems, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`; `balanced_iff` leaner `[propext, Quot.sound]`): the witness-layer counting
  law formalized ‚ÄĒ `balanced_card` (#{(2k+1)-subsets of range(2h) with S ‚äé {q+h} antipodally
  balanced} = C(h‚ąí1,k), explicit `card_bij'`), composed with the in-tree criterion to
  `witness_layer_card`/`witness_e1_card`: at scale s = 2^(m+2), #{(s/2+1)-element exponent
  sets with ő£ ő∂^e = z*} = C(s/2‚ąí1, s/4) ‚ÄĒ O130's layer-dichotomy counting half, in-tree.
  Kernel gates: s=8 full `decide` enumeration = 3 (C19's rung); 35/6435/C(31,16) instantiate.
  Even-r death primitives included (`sum_ne_zero_of_support_lower_half`, geometric form
  cross-referenced as the exponent-coordinate contrapositive of
  `LamLeungUnconditionalGeneral.antipodal_unconditional`). HONEST SCOPE: counts solutions of
  the balance equation; the reduction from RS agree-(s+2) list elements to the equation (the
  e‚āā‚ąíe‚āĀ¬≤ algebra + agreement-cap + even-r elimination at codeword level) is the
  not-yet-formalized analytic half ‚ÄĒ the named follow-up.

**Where the open core moves:** per-prime exactness at n ‚Č• 64 is now an EMPIRICALLY
CALIBRATED correction theory (char-0 count + p|N(őĪ) surplus), not a conjecture; the surplus
őĪ-spectrum is the new finite object to classify. The witness count is formal; the analytic
reduction and the marginal-layer (r ‚Č• 3) counting laws are the remaining formalization
targets on this front.

### R3 (#357 campaign) ‚ÄĒ halving-map renormalization bands REFUTED at kill-check
Idea: iterate 2026/858's threshold-halving `T : őī ‚Ü¶ őī/2` on the window `(1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ, 1‚ąíŌĀ)`;
hoped fixpoint bands force őī* to a band edge.
**Refuted (one inequality):** `(1‚ąíŌĀ)/2 ‚Č§ 1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ` (it is `(1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ)¬≤ ‚Č• 0`), so the FIRST
iterate from anywhere below capacity already lands strictly below Johnson; the orbit
never returns; the unique fixpoint is 0; the band partition is trivial. Any
renormalization analysis of the window needs a map that re-enters it ‚ÄĒ halving never
does (which is precisely why 858 works as a protocol trick and says nothing about
`őĶ_mca` in the window). ‚Üí verified as `halving_exits_window` /
`halving_orbit_never_returns` (`HalvingWindowExit.lean`, axiom-clean).

### S3/N1 (#357 campaign) ‚ÄĒ the naive AFFINE orbit-count law REFUTED; the maximizer set is ONE PROJECTIVE orbit (exhaustive at RS[F‚āÖ,‚ü®2‚ü©,2]); the projective symmetry layer formalized

The S3 dossier conjectured the őĶ_mca-maximizer set is a single orbit of the affine symmetry
group (translation √ó rotation √ó scaling √ó shear, now formal in `MCAEquivariance.lean`).
**Refuted, exhaustively**: at RS[F‚āÖ,‚ü®2‚ü©,2], őī = 1/4, the maximizer set has 100,000 stacks but
the affine orbit of the probe stack has exactly 50,000. The row swap is NOT a repair: it is
not even bad-count-preserving (4 ‚Üí 3 on the seed; the affine ő≥-line is not swap-invariant).

**The true law (verified, then formalized):** the maximizer set is exactly TWO disjoint
affine orbits of 50,000, fused into ONE orbit by the non-affine GL‚āā element
`(u‚āÄ,u‚āĀ) ‚Ü¶ (u‚āĀ, u‚āÄ+u‚āĀ)` ‚ÄĒ i.e. one *projective* orbit. Mechanism: the MCA pencil is a
projective line with `|F|+1` slots; the affine ő≥-census misses the `[0:1]` (‚ąě) slot; the
affine group is precisely the stabilizer of ‚ąě, so one projective orbit splits into affine
orbits indexed by the ‚ąě-slot position. Constraint lemmas (axiom-clean,
`MCAProjectiveEquivariance.lean`): `mcaEventProj` (homogeneous event),
`pairJointAgreesOn_row_mix_iff` (the no-explanation clause is GL‚āā-invariant),
`mcaEventProj_row_mix` (GL‚āā equivariance), `mcaEventProj_smul` (projective well-definedness),
`badSlotCount_eq_affine_add_infty` (affine census = projective census ‚ąí ‚ąě indicator).

Consequences: (a) any orbit-count/flat-numerator law must be stated on `|F|+1` projective
slots ‚ÄĒ affine counts drift by ¬Ī1 inside one structure class, which is now explained, not
anomalous; (b) N1's structured-extremality conjecture survives its first decisive audit in
projective form: at the R1 rung there are NO unstructured maximizers ‚ÄĒ the maximizer set is
one projective orbit exactly.

### O135 ‚ÄĒ G5 REFUTED: the union bound is measure-tight; the weight filter is the whole game (nubs, 2026-06-11)

Fourth verdict of the pre-registered incidence batch (rungs/HYPOTHESES.md), computed
inline exactly (M√∂bius over the full 2¬Ļ‚Ā∂ locus universe, partition check ő£ = q¬Ļ‚Ā∂ passed,
no truncation): the EXACT union of the per-locus spaces over the 4,072 measured
cross-pair loci agrees with the union-bound SUM to 9 significant digits ‚ÄĒ slack
1 + O(1/q). The pre-registered falsifier ("Bonferroni fails to separate") fired.
Mechanism: V_Z ‚ą© V_Z‚Ä≤ = V_{Z‚ą™Z‚Ä≤} has measure ‚Č§ (1/q)¬∑min(|V_Z|,|V_Z‚Ä≤|) ‚ÄĒ over
|F| ‚Čą 2√ó10‚ĀĻ, overlap corrections are negligible BY CONSTRUCTION; the measured
locus-sharing (mean multiplicity 11.55, O129) is combinatorially real but
measure-irrelevant. Structure: a 31-locus antichain (7 singletons + 24 pairs) carries
the entire union; the union exceeds the 47,040 actual differences by 2.7√ó10¬Ļ¬≤‚Ā∂.

**Where the lane's counting target moves (the redirect):** level-1 list counting loses
nothing to locus overlap ‚ÄĒ the ENTIRE gap is the weight filter: bound
#{f ‚ąą V_Z : wt_D(f) ‚Č§ w} against |V_Z|¬∑(volume fraction), where smooth-domain structure
must enter (a random subspace of the same dimension would meet the weight ball at the
generic rate; the measured configuration's differences are EXACTLY the low-weight
elements ‚ÄĒ their excess over generic is the true smooth-domain signal). Incidence stays
decisive at the level of EXACT anatomy (G1 menu law, the S‚ą©B lattice) ‚ÄĒ but as input to
weight-filter arguments, not unions. Batch: G1 ‚úď A1 ‚úď A2 ‚úď G5 ‚úó(refuted-informative);
G2+G3, A3 pending capacity.

### O135 ‚ÄĒ THE r=5 FALSIFIER FINALS: the mod-p surplus SCALES with pattern complexity ‚ÄĒ +33,453 (33.6%) at BabyBear, +16,941 (17.0%) at p‚āā, vs r=3's 1.4√ó10‚ĀĽ‚ĀĶ; first observed feasible-class inflation (nubs, 2026-06-11)

O134's r=5 scan completed (full dossier `scripts/probes/genlaw/falsifier/RESULTS.md`):
exhaustive per-class MITM over ALL 3,222,016 pattern-(14,5) (O,mask) classes at both
production primes. Crossfoots exact (char-0 ő£ = 99,512; 11,808 feasible classes; per-class
char-0 == audit DP at both primes).

* **BabyBear: mod-p marginal r=5 count = 132,965 = char-0 + 33,453** (4,242 spurious
  classes, 33.6% relative). **p‚āā: 116,453 = +16,941** (2,409 classes, 17.0%). Compare r=3:
  +11/+54 (~10‚ĀĽ‚ĀĶ). The (14,5) őĪ-lattice is rich enough that p | N(őĪ) is statistically
  GENERIC at ~2¬≥¬Ļ-size primes ‚ÄĒ the surplus sits at the uniform-heuristic scale. The
  forecastable char-0 core survives unchanged; the per-prime halo is now the dominant
  correction at r ‚Č• 5 and grows with r.
* **Locality law (new):** at BabyBear, ALL 33,453 spurious solutions land on
  char-0-INFEASIBLE classes ‚ÄĒ the 11,808 feasible class counts transfer EXACTLY. At p‚āā,
  exactly ONE feasible class inflates (+1) ‚ÄĒ the first observed mod-p inflation of a
  feasible class, so feasible-class exactness is itself prime-specific, not a law.
* **Verification:** 25-class full-brute samples per prime, 50/50 mitm == brute and
  genuine_bal == char0; 447 explicit spurious configs (238 BB + 209 p‚āā) all reconstructed
  by raw polynomial arithmetic (monic deg-34, coeff(X¬≥¬≥) = 0, coeff(X¬≥¬≤) = őĽ, agreement
  EXACTLY 33); őĪ-spectrum: unique őĪ per sampled class (no cross-class sharing, unlike r=3
  at p‚āā), L1 norms 12‚Äď20, őĪ(ő∂) ‚Č° 0 mod p verified for all. őĺ ‚ąą őľ‚āÜ‚āĄ never occurs mod p
  (both strata, both primes).
* **Consequence for the per-prime bad list at n=64, BabyBear:** witness 6,435 (exact mod p
  for the in-S/out-S anatomy... per the O129 incidence caveat) + marginal ‚Č• r=3 764,555 +
  r=5 132,965; odd r ‚Č• 7 mod-p strata UNKNOWN (char-0 zero no longer implies mod-p zero ‚ÄĒ
  at this surplus scale the r=7 pattern (13,7) plausibly carries a pure-spurious mod-p
  stratum; named open).

**Where the open core moves:** per-prime census theory at n ‚Č• 64 = char-0 structure + a
quantitatively measured generic halo (uniform-heuristic scale, locality on infeasible
classes at large primes, breaking at smaller ones). The named opens: the halo's exact
first moment (is the uniform heuristic a theorem on average over őĪ?), the r=7 mod-p
stratum, and the witness-layer surplus.

### O137 ‚ÄĒ KKH26 IS EXTREMAL at the exact-pin instance: the worst-case stacks are exactly the twisted-monomial orbit of the KKH26 stack (2026-06-11, #357 ‚ÄĒ the exact-point and ceiling campaigns meet)

Probe `probe_o137_kkh26_extremal.py` (exact): at RS[F‚āÖ,(1,2,4,3),2], őī = 1/4, the KKH26
monomial-pair stack `(X¬≥, X¬≤)` (the r = 3 shape: code degree r‚ąí2 = 1) has bad-ő≥ set exactly
`{1,2,3,4}` ‚ÄĒ count 4 = the proven worst case (`DeltaStarExactPinF5.lean`), and exactly the
census law's prediction `‚ąí{3-subset sums of (1,2,4,3)}` (`KKH26CensusLaw.lean`). Since the
extremal stacks form a SINGLE orbit of the twisted-monomial group (O135), the worst case
*is* the orbit of the KKH26 stack:

  **őĶ_mca(C, 1/4) is ATTAINED by the KKH26 family ‚ÄĒ the ceiling family is extremal, not
  just a lower-bound family ‚ÄĒ at the first exactly-solved instance.**

Care: the r = 2 shape `(X¬≤, X)` fires ZERO bad ő≥ here because its direction row `X` is a
codeword (deg < k) ‚ÄĒ when `u‚āĀ ‚ąą C` every closeness witness extends to a joint pair, so
`mcaEvent` never fires. The KKH26 validity constraint (direction strictly outside the code)
is what instance-matching must respect.

**Conjecture (extremality of the monomial orbit):** at every smooth instance and matching
radius, the sup in `őĶ_mca` is attained on the twisted-monomial orbit of the KKH26 stack ‚ÄĒ
equivalently `őĶ_mca(C, 1 ‚ąí r¬∑m/n) = (#distinct fiber-subset sums)/|F|` exactly. If true,
the upper-bracket question for this radius family is *computable* from the subset-sum
census alone. Next falsifiers: (i) the n = 8 rung (orbit-reduced decide via the landed
descent engine); (ii) the (12,6) flat numerator 12 across p ‚ąą {13,37,61} vs the census of
the matching (r, m) shape.
### S2(b) (#357 campaign) ‚ÄĒ universal MissingLine/ObstructionBound REFUTED at the Johnson radius; phase transition of the obstruction-hitting number
Idea: every 2-column stack misses a line (`MissingLine C őī G U` for all U), so the
covering lemma kills the Jo26 A(q,s) factor for every generator unconditionally.
**Probe arc (all exhaustive mod exact symmetries ‚ÄĒ per-row codeword translation,
per-row scaling, row swap; engines cross-validated):**
* F‚āÖ rungs (`probe_missing_line_f5_rungs.py`): n=3 k‚ąą{1,2} and the smooth n=4
  domain ‚ü®2‚ü©=F‚āÖ* k‚ąą{2,3} ‚ÄĒ H(U) ‚Č§ 2 everywhere; the F‚āÉ sharper law `H ‚Č§ l` survives
  the field axis and the length axis.
* l=3 rungs (`probe_missing_line_l3.py`): 3-row stacks at r = n‚ąík = 2 still have
  H = 2 ‚ÄĒ the ROW law `H ‚Č§ l` is not the binding quantity; the syndrome law `H ‚Č§ r`
  takes over.
* Decisive rungs (`probe_missing_line_heavy_fast.py`, memoized engine, 7.6M stacks
  in ~10s): at n=4, k=1 (r=3): őī=1/4 (sub-Johnson, 2 witness levels) ‚Üí H = 2; at
  őī=1/2 = 1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ (Johnson, 3 levels) ‚Üí **H = 4**, killing `H ‚Č§ l` (2), `H ‚Č§ r` (3) and
  `H ‚Č§ #levels` (3) simultaneously. Over small fields the q-bound itself falls:
  F‚āā n=4 őī=1/2: H = 3 > q = 2; F‚āÉ n=4 őī=1/2: H = 4 > q = 3
  (`verify_missing_line_defeater.py`: independent tuple-semantics engine, MATCH).
**Refuted in Lean (`MissingLineDefeater.lean`, axiom-clean):** the F‚āā defeater is
fully rigid ‚ÄĒ stack rows (e‚āÄ,e‚āĀ),(e‚āĀ,e‚āÄ+e‚āā) over the 4-coordinate repetition code at
its Johnson radius őī=1/2 has three bad seeds (0,1),(1,0),(1,1) whose UNIQUE witnesses
{0,2},{2,3},{0,1} pin the three distinct lines of F‚āā¬≤ as obstructions
(kernel-checked cores), overflowing every ‚Č§ q=2 dominating family:
`missingLine_defeated`, `not_forall_missingLine`, `obstructionBound_defeated`.
**What survives:** every sub-Johnson rung still has H ‚Č§ 2 ‚ÄĒ the re-aimed S2(b) is
"MissingLine below Johnson", with a measured phase transition AT Johnson as the
obstruction-side mirror of the global őī* wall; the sibling S2(b') one-row reduction
(`Jo26ObstructionRowCount.lean`) localizes exactly this gating one dimension down,
and these defeaters certify its `‚Č§ q‚ąí1` direction-count hypothesis is TIGHT (it
genuinely fails at Johnson over small fields). Exactness itself is NOT refuted (the
route is sufficient, not necessary); whether epsMCAG interleaving exactness fails at
the defeater configs is the named follow-up probe.
### S1 (#357 campaign) ‚ÄĒ common sum-polynomial API landed; unification bridge, not yet a őī* pin

`SumPolynomialAPI.lean` now names the finite-window coefficient surface shared by three
previously separate proof lanes:

* `coeffPoly n c = ő£_{i<n} C(c_i) X^i` and `rootSum x n c = ő£_{i<n} c_i x^i`
  with coefficient/evaluation/map lemmas.
* KKH26 signed data are this API with `{‚ąí1,0,1}` coefficients:
  `sumPoly_eq_coeffPoly`, `sVal_eq_rootSum`.
* Witness-layer multiplicities are this API with nonnegative integer coefficients:
  `layer_rootSum_vanishing_iff` packages the Lam-Leung antipodal-balance criterion through
  `rootSum`.
* de Bruijn prime-power indicators are this API with `{0,1}` rational coefficients:
  `indicatorPoly_eq_coeffPoly`.

Interpretation for the őī* campaign: S1's "same mathematics" claim is now theorem-shaped
in-tree at the coefficient-surface level.  What remains open is the hard analytic transfer:
the RS agreement-to-layer reduction, the marginal-layer laws beyond the balanced witness
layer, and the per-prime norm-divisibility surplus correction.

**O137 addendum ‚ÄĒ the conjecture survives (13,4,2) and (17,4,2), and predicts the FULL
profile:** exact ladder rerun: max-bad profile `{m=3: 4, m=4: 1}` at BOTH p = 13 (domain
(1,8,12,5)) and p = 17 (domain (1,13,16,4)) ‚ÄĒ equal to the census profile
`{#3-subset sums = 4, #4-subset sums = 1}` of the respective őľ‚āĄ domains. Three fields
(5, 13, 17), one census. The field-independent "flat numerator" phenomenon is *explained*
at these rungs: the numerator is the subset-sum census, which is field-independent as long
as the sums stay distinct (no collisions at these primes). Probe additions in
`probe_o137_kkh26_extremal.py`-adjacent rerun of the exact ladder.

### O138 ‚ÄĒ THE (12,6) FLAT NUMERATOR SOLVED: the extremal stack is the m = 1 pair (X‚ĀĻ, X‚Āł) and the numerator is the constrained subset-sum census, field-independent (2026-06-11, #357)

The dossier's open phenomenon ‚ÄĒ max bad-ő≥ count exactly 12 at (n,k) = (12,6), őī = 1/4, at
EVERY field p ‚ąą {13,37,61} ‚ÄĒ is fully explained
(`probe_o138_flat_numerator_solved.py`, exact):

* **Monomial-stack scan at p = 13** (all `(X^s, X^t)`, 6 ‚Č§ t < s ‚Č§ 11, agreement ‚Č• 9):
  unique maximum **(X‚ĀĻ, X‚Āł) with badcount 12**; the KKH26 fiber stack (X‚ĀĻ, X‚Ā∂) gives only 4;
  (X¬Ļ‚Āį,X‚Ā∑) and (X¬Ļ¬Ļ,X‚Āł) give 4; everything else 0. The extremal monomial pair is the
  **adjacent-exponent (m = 1) pair**, same as the n = 4 extremal (X¬≥, X¬≤) ‚ÄĒ NOT the
  m = 3 fiber shape.
* **The census explains 12 exactly:** the general m = 1 law (monic-root forcing at degree
  a, coefficient matching) says őĽ is bad for `(X^a, X^{a‚ąí1})` against degree-< k codes at
  agreement ‚Č• a iff `‚ąÉ A ‚ąą C(H, a)` with `e‚āā(A) = ‚Ä¶ = e_{a‚ąík}(A) = 0` and `őĽ = ‚ąíe‚āĀ(A)`.
  At (12,6), a = 9: `{‚ąíe‚āĀ(A) : A ‚ąą C(őľ‚āĀ‚āā,9), e‚āā(A) = e‚āÉ(A) = 0}` has EXACTLY 12 elements
  (12 qualifying subsets, all sums distinct) at p = 13, 37, and 61 ‚ÄĒ field-independent,
  matching the flat numerator. (12 = n suggests the qualifying subsets are one rotation
  orbit ‚ÄĒ the orbit law again.)

**Corrected extremality conjecture:** the sup in `őĶ_mca(C, 1 ‚ąí a/n)` is attained on the
twisted-monomial orbit of the **adjacent pair** `(X^a, X^{a‚ąí1})`, and equals
`#{‚ąíe‚āĀ(A) : A ‚ąą C(H,a), e‚āā(A) = ‚Ä¶ = e_{a‚ąík}(A) = 0} / |F|`. Confirmed exactly at every rung
where exact computation exists: (5,4,2), (13,4,2), (17,4,2) (where the constraint set is
empty and this reduces to the proven `badScalar_iff_subsetSum`), and (12,6) √ó three fields.
Formalization target: `badScalar_iff_constrainedSubsetSum` (same monic-root-forcing proof,
esymm coefficients via Vieta); the KKH26CensusLaw file's law is the k = a‚ąí1 special case.

### O139 ‚ÄĒ first exact census data INSIDE the window: the adjacent-pair family saturates small fields above Johnson, goes field-dependent mid-window, and DIES at őī = 0.5625 for p ‚Č• 97 (2026-06-11, #357)

`probe_o139_window_interior_census.py` (exact): rate 1/4 (n = 16, k = 4), window
(0.5, 0.75); the O138 constrained census `{‚ąíe‚āĀ(A) : A ‚ąą C(őľ‚āĀ‚āÜ,a), e‚āā = ‚Ä¶ = e_{a‚ąí4} = 0}`
at the window radii őī = 1 ‚ąí a/16:

* **a = 5 (őī = 0.6875):** a = k+1 ‚üĻ NO constraints ‚ÄĒ every 5-subset qualifies and the
  census **saturates the field** (= p) at p ‚ąą {17, 97, 113, 193}: for the explicit stack
  `(X‚ĀĶ, X‚Āī)`, EVERY scalar is bad above Johnson at small fields (and the direction row is
  never explainable, so this is genuine mcaEvent badness): an explicit, machine-checked
  witness for why the prize must fix |F| large. At huge p the census is ‚Č§ C(16,5) = 4368 ‚ÄĒ
  and the unconstrained a = k+1 census is exactly the in-tree t = 1 sliver object
  (`ListInteriorUnconditionalT1`): the two lanes meet.
* **a = 6 (őī = 0.625):** one constraint (e‚āā = 0) ‚ÄĒ the census goes **field-dependent and
  non-monotone**: (p, #qualifying, census) = (17, 480, 17), (97, 80, 32), (113, 48, 48),
  (193, 16, 16). The qualifying count decays toward ~n at large p (Weil-type fluctuation
  in between ‚ÄĒ the e‚āā = 0 condition is a curve count over subsets).
* **a = 7 (őī = 0.5625):** two constraints (e‚āā = e‚āÉ = 0) ‚ÄĒ the census is **EMPTY at every
  p ‚Č• 97** (only p = 17 retains 32 qualifying subsets). The adjacent-pair family
  contributes NOTHING below őī = 0.625 at large fields: the first measured **family death
  radius inside the window**, őī_death(16, 4) ‚ąą (0.5625, 0.625].

Reading: along the adjacent-pair family the census interpolates from the exponential t = 1
sliver at capacity down to EMPTY mid-window ‚ÄĒ the constraints `e‚āā = ‚Ä¶ = e_{a‚ąík} = 0` over
subgroup subsets are a *vanishing-power-sum* system whose solvability threshold IS this
family's contribution to őī*. Whether OTHER stacks (higher monomials with cofactor freedom,
rational/DEEP shapes) take over below the death radius is now THE precise extremality
question; the (12,6) scan pattern (adjacent pair uniquely maximal, higher shapes strictly
smaller) suggests not. Next: the (16,4,a=7) higher-monomial scan; death-radius scaling in n
at fixed rate (does őī_death ‚Üí Johnson, capacity, or an interior limit? ‚ÄĒ this is a direct
empirical probe OF őī* itself for the conjecturally-extremal family).

### O140 ‚ÄĒ the adjacent-pair death radius is rate-UNIVERSAL at constraint depth 2: rate 1/2 dies at the same two vanishing power sums (2026-06-11, #357)

`probe_o140_death_radius_rate_half.py` (exact, asserts green): rate 1/2 (n = 16, k = 8),

  (17, 432, 17), (97, 32, 16), (113, 64, 32), (193, 32, 32).
* **a = 11 (őī = 0.3125, e‚āā = e‚āÉ = 0):** **EMPTY at every p ‚ÄĒ including p = 17** (which
  retained 32 qualifying subsets at rate 1/4). őī_death(16, 8) ‚ąą (0.3125, 0.375].

Reading: at BOTH measured rates the family dies at exactly TWO vanishing power sums:

`capacity ‚ąí őė(1/n)`, *narrower at toy scale* than the KKH26 `őė(1/log n)` strip. If the
O138 adjacent-pair extremality conjecture holds at small n while the KKH26 m > 1 fiber
shapes dominate asymptotically, the two families must CROSS OVER in n; locating that

### O141 ‚ÄĒ the COMPLETE prime spectrum of mid-window badness via cyclotomic norm divisibility: the (16,8) adjacent-pair family is mid-window-bad at EXACTLY 17 primes, and clean at every other field FOREVER (2026-06-11, #357)

`probe_o141_norm_divisibility_spectrum.py` (exact, asserts green). Three verdicts that
together close the (16,8) window profile of the candidate-extremal family for ALL p:

1. **The char-0 layer is EMPTY at depth 1**: no 10-subset of őľ‚āĀ‚āÜ has `e‚āā(A) = 0` in
   `‚Ą§[ő∂‚āĀ‚āÜ]` (exact arithmetic mod `ő¶‚āĀ‚āÜ`, all 8008 subsets). Every per-prime qualifying
   subset of O140's erratic depth-1 row is a pure characteristic-p surplus ‚ÄĒ the O134
   `p | N(őĪ)` mechanism is not a small correction here, it is the ENTIRE mid-window story.
2. **The finite spectrum**: p qualifies at depth 1 only if `p | N(e‚āā(A))` for some A; the
   complete set of such primes ‚Č° 1 (mod 16) is
   `S(16,8) = {17, 97, 113, 193, 257, 337, 433, 449, 881, 1217, 1249, 1553, 2113, 2161,
   3121, 7489, 18433}` (largest norm 18433). Validated: depth-1 census nonzero ‚üļ p ‚ąą S for
   all 25 primes ‚Č§ 1297; for every p > 18433 the row is empty ‚ÄĒ no scan needed, ever.
3. **Depth ‚Č• 2 dead at every prime**: a = 11, 12 census = 0 at all lucky primes
   (257‚Ä¶18433), completing O140's universal death.

**The first exact, all-fields, őī-resolved window profile of any candidate-extremal family:**
- `őī ‚ąą [cap‚ąí1/n, cap) = [.4375, .5)`: bad at every p (saturation / t=1 sliver);
- `őī ‚ąą [cap‚ąí2/n, cap‚ąí1/n) = [.375, .4375)`: bad at exactly the 17 primes of `S(16,8)`;
- `őī ‚ąą (Johnson, cap‚ąí2/n)`: empty at EVERY prime.

**Consequences for őī\*.** (a) If the O138 adjacent-pair extremality conjecture holds at
(16,8), then for all p ‚ąČ S(16,8) the true őī\* at this instance is `‚Č• cap ‚ąí 2/n` ‚ÄĒ the
window interior is CLEAN and őī\* is pinned within `2/n` of capacity at toy scale; the
entire őī\*-relevant question collapses to (i) extremality and (ii) the norm spectrum.

### O141 ‚ÄĒ THE FAKE-POINT REFORMULATION + exact n = 32 death table: c*(n) = log‚āān ‚ąí 3 at the clean prime, and the fiber mechanism identified as STRUCTURAL moment vanishing (2026-06-11, #357)

`probe_o141_mitm_fakepoint_census.py` (MITM, exact; gates: a = 10, 11 reproduce the O140
exhaustive counts byte-exactly):

**The fake-point lens.** Newton-reducing the constrained band: `e‚āā(A) = ‚Ä¶ = e_c(A) = 0`
‚üļ `p_j(A) = p‚āĀ(A)^j` for `j = 2..c` ‚ÄĒ a qualifying `a`-subset *masquerades as the single
field point `t = p‚āĀ(A)` through its first `c` moments*, and the bad scalar is `őĽ = ‚ąít`.
The census is the set of **fake points** the domain supports at level `(a, c)`. (This is
what makes MITM counting trivial: match half-subset moment vectors against `(t, t¬≤, ‚Ä¶)`.)

**Exact death table, n = 32, rate 1/4 (constraints e‚āā..e_{a‚ąí8}):**
| a | constraints | p = 97 | p = 193 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 12 | 3 | **384 (census 96 ‚ÄĒ saturates!)** | 0 |
| 13 | 4 | 0 | 0 |

* At the cleaner prime (193): death at 2‚Üí3 constraints for n = 32 vs 1‚Üí2 for n = 16:
  **c*(n) = log‚āā n ‚ąí 3 at both scales** ‚üĻ the m = 1 adjacent-pair family dies at
  `őī_death = 1 ‚ąí ŌĀ ‚ąí őė(log n / n)` ‚ÄĒ a *capacity-adjacent sliver*, far shallower than the
  KKH26 fiber family's `capacity ‚ąí őė(1/log n)`.
* At p = 97 an arithmetic halo keeps a = 12 alive (384 subsets, fake points saturating
  nearly all of F‚āČ‚āá) ‚ÄĒ small-prime accidents, gone at 193. Death is field-dependent at
  fixed n; the **structural (field-independent) core** at these (n, a) is empty.
* **The fiber mechanism, explained:** a union of `r` full fibers of `x ‚Ü¶ x^m` on őľ_n has
  `p_j = 0` for every `j` with `m ‚ą§ j` ‚ÄĒ the KKH26 fiber construction satisfies the moment
  constraints *structurally* (for all fields at once), which is exactly why m-structured
  families reach deep radii at production-size fields while the m = 1 family dies just
  below capacity. Small-field extremality of the adjacent pair (O137/O138) and
  large-field KKH26 reach are two regimes of ONE census.

**The reduced production-scale question (the new apex):** classify the field-independent
solutions of the moment-vanishing system `p_j(A) = t^j (j ‚Č§ c)` over subsets of őľ_n. If
fiber unions (and their coset/orbit images) are the only structural solutions, then the
extremal-family census at production fields IS the KKH26 fiber census, the in-tree ceiling
is family-optimal, and the upper-bracket side of őī* equals the fiber-census threshold.
This is the N1 structure conjecture made exact ‚ÄĒ and it is now a finite, probeable,
formalizable classification problem at each (n, a, c).

### O143 ‚ÄĒ THE TWO-LAYER LAW of the window census: char-0 vanishing sums + finite-spectrum char-p surplus; the #232 Lam‚ÄďLeung lane and the O134 surplus mechanism are the two halves of one decomposition (2026-06-11, #357)

`probe_o143_two_layer_law.py` (exact, asserts green). The unification of
O134 + O138‚ÄďO142:

  `census_p(n, k, row) = (char-0 layer mod p) + (surplus layer)`,

where the **char-0 layer** is the classical vanishing-power-sum locus over `őľ_n`
(Lam‚ÄďLeung / de Bruijn ‚ÄĒ the #232 machinery, now appearing *inside* the window census) and
the **surplus layer** is nonzero only for `p` in the finite norm spectrum `S(n,k)` (O141).

* `(8,2)`: pure char-0 layer ‚ÄĒ 10 subsets (two `őľ‚āĄ`-cosets with `e‚āĀ = 0` + eight
  near-antipodal configs), 9 distinct `e‚āĀ` values; `S = ‚ąÖ` (max norm 16 = 2‚Āī has no
  prime ‚Č° 1 mod 8). Verified `#qual = 10`, `census = 9` at all of
  p ‚ąą {17, 41, 73, 89, 97, 113, 233, 1009} ‚ÄĒ field-independent, exactly the char-0 layer;
* `(16,4)`, `(16,8)`: char-0 EMPTY ‚ÄĒ pure surplus layer on 17 explicit primes each
  (O141/O142).

**Why this matters for őī\*.** The window-interior badness of the candidate-extremal family
is now a sum of two *computable, scan-free* objects: a field-independent census governed by
vanishing-sums classification (nonzero only when `n/k` is large relative to constraint
depth ‚ÄĒ present at rate 1/4, n = 8; absent at every n = 16 row), and a field-exceptional
finite-prime layer priced by cyclotomic norm growth. The S1 unification thesis (KKH26
census ‚Č° de Bruijn vanishing sums) is no longer a conjecture about analogy ‚ÄĒ the window
census literally *contains* the vanishing-sums locus as its field-independent layer.

### Red team (#357 campaign) ‚ÄĒ CensusUpperExtremal (the O138 extremality formalization) FALSE as stated at empty-census rungs; floor repair landed

floor őĶ_mca ‚Č• 1/|F| at every below-capacity radius (`epsMCA_ge_inv_card_of_finrank_lt`).
**Machine-checked:** `censusUpperExtremal_false_of_empty` (`CensusExtremalFloor.lean`,

F‚āÖ instantiation still recovers őī* = 1/4 (`mcaDeltaStar_F5_via_censusFloor`) ‚ÄĒ repair

### O145 ‚ÄĒ the classification HOLDS at three instances (after proper multi-prime intersection) + THE ONE-ORBIT HALO LAW + a methodological trap caught (2026-06-11, #357)

`probe_o145_classification_instances.py` + follow-ups (exact):

* **Instance A (n = 16, stack (X¬Ļ¬≤, X‚Āł), k = 5 ‚ÄĒ the m = 4, r = 3 shape):** solutions = 4 at
  every prime ‚ąą {97, 113, 193, 257}, equal to the 4 quartic-fiber unions. **No halo at all.**
* **Instance B (n = 32, stack (X‚Āł, X‚Ā∂), k = 5 ‚ÄĒ the m = 2, r = 4 shape):** per-prime
  solutions = **1852 at p = 193, 257, AND 449** (flat count!), of which the three-prime
  intersection is **exactly the 1820 antipodal-fiber unions**. The classification holds.
* **The one-orbit halo law:** at each prime the 32 extra solutions are exactly ONE rotation
  orbit (size n) of a prime-specific exotic 8-subset (e.g. (0,1,3,8,11,18,20,21) at 193 ‚ÄĒ
  verified dead at 257..1153 and char-0-nonzero |ő£ő∂^i| ‚Čą 0.66). Halo membership is
  field-specific; halo SIZE is field-independent (32 = n at every prime tested) ‚ÄĒ the
  flat-count phenomenon at yet another level.
* **METHODOLOGICAL TRAP (caught):** intersecting over TWO primes is NOT a valid
  structurality test ‚ÄĒ a char-0-nonzero sum can vanish at several primes simultaneously
  (its norm has many prime divisors; the initial 2-prime run made the 193-halo look
  structural because 257 happened to carry an equally-sized halo). Char-0 Lam‚ÄďLeung
  (no non-antipodal-closed vanishing sums of 2-power roots of unity) is the theoretical
  anchor: any apparent non-fiber structural solution MUST die at large/many primes.
  Protocol fixed: ‚Č• 3 primes + char-0 numeric check.

**Classification scoreboard:** fiber unions are exactly the field-independent gap-band
solutions at ALL three instances tested ‚ÄĒ (16, m=2, r=4), (16, m=4, r=3), (32, m=2, r=4) ‚ÄĒ
strengthening `CensusUpperExtremal`'s structural half. The remaining open legs of the
ceiling chain stay: the classification as a THEOREM (the Lam‚ÄďLeung bridge ‚ÄĒ at 2-power n,
char-0 vanishing of e‚āĀ forces antipodal-closure, which plus the band forces fiber towers),
the per-prime halo quantification (one orbit ‚ÄĒ provable?), and beyond-two-monomial
extremality.

### O145 ‚ÄĒ the a = 4 char-0 census closed form: N‚āĄ(n) = n(n‚ąí3)/4, all solutions antipodal-structured (blind n=64 forecast confirmed); the a = 8 layer decomposes 70 = 64 + 6 (2026-06-11, #357)

two-layer law's field-independent layer:

  `st = x¬≤` (cross terms vanish on the antipodal pair); count `(n/2)¬∑(n‚ąí2)/2` minus the

  n = 8, 16, 32 (10/52/232) and the **blind n = 64 forecast (976)** verified by exhaustive

Combined with O144's parity law, the field-independent layer of the depth-1 window census

`a = 4` ‚ÄĒ the first two rungs of the vanishing-locus classification the K4/Lam‚ÄďLeung lane
predicted would govern the window.

### Red team round 2 (#357 campaign) ‚ÄĒ CensusUpperExtremalFloor FALSIFIED by take-over at the death radius; the extremality conjecture corrected to the EXCESS census

field-independently (p = 97 and 193). At a=8 (Johnson agreement) it beats the adjacent
pair 16-to-1.**

őľ‚āą-cosets, so x‚ĀĻ + őĽx‚Āł = ¬Ī(x+őĽ) is piecewise linear; witnesses with six points on one
coset plus the crossing point are explained by ¬Ī(X+őĽ); every őĽ ‚ąą őľ‚āĀ‚āÜ is bad. The
CS25/KK25 splitting mechanism IS the take-over family below the adjacent death radius.

### O146 ‚ÄĒ RED TEAM: CensusUpperExtremal REFUTED AS STATED (double-spike witness at a = n‚ąí1) and CORRECTED: the profile is the MAX of two proven families (sparse-spike staircase ‚ą® census); the corrected form survives every exact data point (2026-06-11, #357 red-team cycle 1)

Red-team attack on the census-crossing pin at the non-2-power ladder instances:

* **The refutation (exact):** at (7,6,3) AND (13,6,3), agreement a = 5 (= n‚ąí1) has
  adjacent-pair census **0** but exact max-bad over ALL stacks **2** ‚ÄĒ the census is not
  extremal at this rung. Witness extracted (syndrome-reduced exact scan): the **double
  spike** u‚āÄ = 1_{x‚āÖ}, u‚āĀ = 1_{x‚āĄ,x‚āÖ}: bad ő≥ ‚ąą {0, ‚ąí1}, each line a 1-sparse word vanishing
  on n‚ąí1 points. This is precisely the mechanism of the just-landed **universal second
  band** (`őĶ_mca = 2/|F|` exactly on `1 ‚Č§ őī¬∑n < 2` for EVERY linear code of distance ‚Č• 4) ‚ÄĒ
  a field-independent staircase near őī = 0, orthogonal to the census mechanism.
* **The correction:** the extremality conjecture becomes
  **`őĶ_mca(C, 1 ‚ąí a/n) ¬∑ q = max(staircase band value at a, adjacent-pair census at a)`** ‚ÄĒ
  the sparse-spike staircase dominates at a ‚ąą {n, n‚ąí1, ‚Ä¶} (őī¬∑n small), the census takes over
  at the crossing scale. Both families are PROVEN lower bounds (universal bands; census law
  + `census_le_epsMCA`); only the upper half ("nothing beats the max") remains the named
  surface.
* **The corrected form survives every exactly-computed instance:**
  (5,4,2): {a=4: max(1,1)=1 ‚úď, a=3: max(¬∑,4)=4 ‚úď};
  (13,4,2)/(17,4,2): {1,4} ‚úď; (12,6) @ 13/37/61: a=9: max(¬∑,12)=12 ‚úď;
  (7,6,3): {a=6: max(1,1)=1 ‚úď, a=5: max(2,0)=2 ‚úď, a=4: max(¬∑,7)=7 ‚úď};
  (13,6,3): {1, 2, 13} ‚úď. No exact data point deviates from the two-family max.
* Bonus: the small-field saturations (census = p at (7,6,3) a=4 and (13,6,3) a=4) again
  match max-bad exactly ‚ÄĒ saturation is census-driven, not spike-driven.

**Consequence for the conditional pin:** `mcaDeltaStar_eq_of_censusCrossing'` remains valid
verbatim wherever the crossing agreement sits BELOW the staircase rungs (a_c ‚Č§ n ‚ąí 2 and
the staircase value at a > a_c also ‚Č§ őĶ*¬∑q ‚ÄĒ at production parameters őĶ*¬∑q ‚Čą 2^128 the
first staircase bands are astronomically below threshold, so the staircase NEVER decides
the crossing there; the correction matters only at toy scales/tiny őĶ*). The named surface
should still be restated as the two-family max for honesty. Red-team cycle 1 verdict:
**the candidate pin survives, with a corrected and sharper conjecture.**

## Fable N1 (pencil-energy law) ‚ÄĒ REFUTED at scale (2026-06-11)
Hypothesis: őī*(H) = F(E‚āā(H)/n¬≤), E‚āā = ő£_b t‚āā(b)¬≤ (M√∂bius pencil energy).
Refutation: `scripts/probes/probe_n1_energy_vs_badcount.py` ‚ÄĒ exact őĶ_mca bad-count is
DOMAIN-INDEPENDENT (smooth=random at equal n,k,őī) across 8 feasible instances, while E‚āā varies
up to 10√ó. So őĶ_mca is not a function of E‚āā; the energy is moment-blind to the extremal bad count.
Constraint lemma target: ‚ąÉ two domains, same field, E‚āā differing, őĶ_mca equal. The proven
E‚āā=őė(n¬≥) smooth separation (MobiusPencilEnergy.lean) is a spectrum-moment fact, not őī*-controlling.
Refinement N1‚Ä≤: target the EXTREMAL pencil invariant (max t‚āā / M3 third moment), not the sum.

### O147 ‚ÄĒ the two-layer law EXTENDS to the excess census; the CA/MCA gap localizes on coset witnesses; the take-over flat-n numerator is one rotation orbit of char-0 pinned scalars (2026-06-11, #357)

`probe_o147_excess_census_two_layer.py` (exact char-0 in ‚Ąö(ő∂‚āĀ‚āÜ) + mod-97; asserts green).
The hand-off question from the take-over comment ("does the norm machinery extend to the
excess system?") ‚ÄĒ answered, with three structural verdicts at the take-over instance
((X‚ĀĻ, X‚Āł) over őľ‚āĀ‚āÜ, k = 4, a = 7, excess 2):

1. **Subset census is pure layer-1**: 464 qualifying 7-subsets in char 0 AND mod 97 ‚ÄĒ
   the two-layer law extends verbatim to the excess band system (surplus primes are again
   norm divisors of the band minors).
2. **The CA/MCA gap is exactly the coset-witness layer**: 16 of the 464 subsets carry a
   full ő≥-LINE (band system rank 1) ‚ÄĒ and they are precisely the sixteen 7-subsets of the
   two parity classes (the index-2 subgroup and its coset). On those witnesses `X‚Āł ‚Č° ¬Ī1`
   and `X‚ĀĻ ‚Č° ¬ĪX`, so both rows are codewords there ‚ÄĒ the stack is jointly explainable and
   `mcaEvent` cannot fire. The ő≥-lines are CA-bad but MCA-invisible: a fully worked,
   machine-checked instance of the CA ‚Č† MCA separation *inside* a census computation.
3. **The flat-n law of the take-over family is a one-orbit char-0 law**: the remaining
   448 subsets pin ő≥, with exactly 16 = n distinct pinned values ‚ÄĒ and the 16 values form
   a SINGLE rotation orbit `{‚ąíő∂^{4+j} : j}` (ő≥‚āÄ = ‚ąíő∂‚Āī). Field-independence of the measured
   16-at-every-prime is explained: the values are characteristic-zero constants reduced
   mod p.

**Consequence for the excess-census ledger:** census(MCA, excess row) = (pinned char-0
ő≥-census, one orbit here) + (finite-spectrum surplus); the band system alone OVERCOUNTS by
the explainable coset layer ‚ÄĒ any future excess-census ledger entry needs the
explainability filter, not just band solvability.

### S3 (#357 campaign) ‚ÄĒ pure-frequency extremality REFUTED at intermediate rungs; repaired by rotation powers; the flat numerator IS one orbit
Idea (v1): every rung of the exact bad-ő≥ profile is attained by a *pure-frequency*
eigenstack `(e_{j‚āÄ}, e_{j‚āĀ})` of the rotation `R` (diagonal on syndrome frequencies), making
every rung count `őĶ + j¬∑d` with `d = ord(h^{j‚āĀ‚ąíj‚āÄ}) | n`.

pure-frequency class caps at 1 < exact 2/2/3 (`probe_s3_eigenstack_orbit_law.py`, stages
B/C). The ŌÉ¬Ļ syndrome eigenspaces are 1-dimensional ‚ÄĒ too rigid for the count-2/3 rungs.
**Repaired (same probe, confirmed):** rotation *powers* ŌÉ^t have multi-dimensional syndrome
eigenspaces (frequency classes mod `n/gcd(t,n)`); ŌÉ^t-eigenstacks attain every missing rung
with the predicted orbit structure ‚ÄĒ count-2 rungs by antipodal pairs `{ő≥, ‚ąíő≥}` (ŌÉ¬≥ at n=6:
`[1,6],[2,5],[3,4]` at p=7; ŌÉ‚Ā∂ at n=12: `[1,12],[2,11],[3,10]` at p=13), count-3 by
ŌČ-triples (ŌÉ‚Āī: `[4,10,12]` = an ‚ü®h‚Āī‚ü©-orbit). The plateau rung m=9 count 12 = **one full
order-12 orbit** (attainer `(j‚āÄ,j‚āĀ) = (9,8)`, őĶ = 0), reproduced verbatim at p = 37, 61
where the orbit is a *proper* coset of `F*` ‚ÄĒ the field-independent flat numerator is an
orbit count, mechanism identified. Honest census caveat: at `(13,6,3)` m=5 only 6 of 300
maximizers are genuinely ŌÉ¬≥-eigen (the rest are eigen only for the vacuous t = n) ‚ÄĒ
eigenstacks *attain* the exact max at every tested rung but do not *exhaust* the maximizer
set at intermediate rungs; structured-extremality (N1) must be read as attainment.
‚Üí verified as `MCAEigenstackOrbitLaw.lean` (axiom-clean, layered on the same-hour sibling
engine `MCAEquivariance.lean` ‚ÄĒ the two S3 lanes landed within the hour; engine adopted,
orbit law layered on top): `mcaEvent_eigenstack_iff` (bad set invariant under
`T(ő≥) = a‚ĀĽ¬Ļb + ő≥¬∑a‚ĀĽ¬Ļc`), `orderOf_le/dvd_card_of_mul_mem` (orbit arithmetic for invariant
scalar sets), `orderOf_le/dvd_badScalarSet_card_of_eigenstack` (count = őĶ + #orbits¬∑d,
field-independent), and the F‚āÖ demo (one certificate + the orbit law re-derives
`őĶ_mca(C542, 1/4) ‚Č• 4/5`, replacing four hand certificates).
**Where the open core moves:** the rung profile is orbit arithmetic over divisors of `n`;
which radius activates which divisor reduces to a splitting-locus question for
`x^{j‚āÄ} + ő≥x^{j‚āĀ} ‚ąí ő≤` over the domain subgroup ‚ÄĒ a sharply-posed finite question. The
KKH26 ceiling stack is itself an eigenstack (eigenratio `g^{‚ąím}`, order s): the
near-capacity ceiling family and the toy plateau maximizers are one object class, and the
one-orbit halo law (O145) is this mechanism at the census level.

### O148 ‚ÄĒ G2-3: the production crossing, priced exactly ‚ÄĒ under PROVEN census counts the fiber census never reaches őĶ*¬∑q at |F| < 2^256; the entire numeric uncertainty of őī*(production) is the true subset-sum count at fold scales s ‚ąą [64, 256] (2026-06-11, #357)

Assembly of the profile law (O147) with the in-tree census-size certificates:

* **The crossing calculus.** Under the profile law, őī*(production) = 1 ‚ąí a_c/n where a_c is
  the largest agreement with census(a) > őĶ*¬∑q ‚Čą 2^128. The census at fold scale s = n/m,
  row r (radius őī = 1 ‚ąí r/s, validity r ‚Č§ ŌĀs + 2) has PROVEN size ‚Č• stratified
  `ő£_j 2^{r‚ąí2j}¬∑C(s/2, r‚ąí2j)` ‚ÄĒ but distinctness is certified only under the prime
  threshold: `p > s^{s/2}` (resultant route) or the Parseval-halved threshold (s = 64 rows
  open unconditionally at |F| < 2^256; s = 128 closed ‚ÄĒ the in-tree reach table).
* **The pricing.** At |F| < 2^256 the certified scales are s ‚Č§ 64 (unconditional) where the
  maximal certified census is ‚Čą 2^{s/2 + s/2} = 2^64 ‚Č™ 2^128: **the certified fiber census
  never crosses őĶ*¬∑q at production parameters.** The deep rows (s ‚Č• 128, census up to
  2^{s(H‚āā+ŌĀ)} ‚Č• 2^128 at s ‚Čą 256) need either the TZ external (polynomial-field route,
  `kkh26_mcaDeltaStar_le_of_TZ`, in-tree) or genuinely larger certified counts.
* **The localized uncertainty.** The production őī* under the profile law therefore sits in
  the bracket: floor = the proven Johnson-side MCA theorems (1 ‚ąí ‚ąöŌĀ, unconditional);
  ceiling = capacity ‚ąí 2/s* where s* is the largest fold scale whose TRUE distinct-sum
  count crosses 2^128 at |F| < 2^256. Everything in between is decided by ONE number per
  rate: **the true (not resultant-certified) distinct subset-sum count of őľ_{s} strata at
  s ‚ąą [64, 256]** ‚ÄĒ a finite question per scale, sharply localized, attackable by the
  lacunary-resultant / certified-computation routes already inventoried (#357 ¬ß5) and by
  the O134 per-prime correction theory.
* **Two regimes of the answer (conditional on the two named surfaces):** if the true counts
  match the char-0/stratified forecasts (no large-prime collapse ‚ÄĒ the genlaw/falsifier
  evidence supports this up to measured surpluses), then s* ‚Čą 256 and
  **őī*(ŌĀ, 2^{‚ąí128}) = 1 ‚ąí ŌĀ ‚ąí őė(1/s*) = capacity ‚ąí c(ŌĀ)** with c(ŌĀ) ‚Čą 2/s* a CONSTANT
  (not őė(1/log n)) for all n ‚Č• s*¬∑m ‚ÄĒ strictly stronger than the published KKH26 phrasing,
  whose 1/log n came from the prime-threshold coupling, not from the census itself. If the
  true counts collapse at large p below 2^128, őī* recedes toward the floor accordingly. The
  prize question, under the profile law, IS this count.

### O149 ‚ÄĒ the halo mechanism verified at the norm level: the exotic subset's norms are EXACTLY 2¬≤¬∑193¬≤ ‚ÄĒ monogamous halo membership; the halo bound becomes a divisor-counting question (2026-06-11, #357, surface (ii))

`probe_o149_halo_norm_mechanism.py` (exact ‚Ą§[ő∂‚āÉ‚āā] arithmetic mod ő¶‚āÉ‚āā): the p = 193 exotic
halo subset `A = (0,1,3,8,11,18,20,21)` (O145) has

  `N(őĪ_A) = N(ő≤_A) = 148996 = 2¬≤ ¬∑ 193¬≤`

(őĪ = ő£ő∂^i, ő≤ = ő£ő∂^{3i} ‚ÄĒ the two gap-band constraint sums). Consequences:

* **Monogamy:** 193 is the only odd prime dividing either norm ‚ÄĒ this subset can join the
  halo at p = 193 and at NO other prime. The O145 observation (each prime's halo is its
  own) has its mechanism: halo membership at p ‚üļ p | N(őĪ_S) AND p | N(ő≤_S).
* **Joint vanishing explained:** both constraint norms share the prime ‚ÄĒ the apparent
  coincidence that one subset satisfies BOTH band constraints mod 193 is a single
  algebraic event (likely one prime ideal of ‚Ą§[ő∂] above 193 dividing both elements).
* **Tiny norms:** 148996 ‚Čą 2^17.2 versus the worst-case bound a^ŌÜ(32) = 8^16 = 2^48 ‚ÄĒ
  the same real-vs-worst-case gap measured at the resultant level (O129/Parseval), now at
  the halo level. Per-subset halo-prime counts are ‚Č§ ő©(N) ‚Č§ ~17/log‚āā(p) ‚Čą 2 at p ‚Čą 200.
* **The halo surface, recast:** `halo(p) = #{S non-fiber : p | N(őĪ_S), p | N(ő≤_S)}` ‚ÄĒ the
  one-orbit law is a joint norm-divisibility multiplicity statement, attackable by the
  O134 őĪ-spectrum correction theory (which counts exactly such divisibilities) and by
  averaging: `ő£_p halo(p) ‚Č§ ő£_S ő©(N(őĪ_S))` gives a PROVABLE average-halo bound from the
  norm bound alone. Formalization route: Mathlib cyclotomic-field norms; the finite
  per-instance certificates (like this one) are exact-arithmetic checkable today.

## CORRECTION to the domain-blindness entry (2026-06-11)
The "őī* domain-independent" reading was over-stated: exact probes reach only the SATURATED band
[UD,~Johnson) where bad count = n (saturation ceiling) for all domains ‚ÄĒ the easy regime. The
INTERIOR (Johnson,capacity), where őī* lives and KKH26 is domain-specific, is exact-infeasible at
low rate. So: domain-blind in the saturated band (proved by enumeration of all 252/120 subsets);
interior separation OPEN, not killed. N1 (specific E‚āā law) stays refuted; N1' (extremal pencil /
M3 third moment) REVIVED as the live domain-aware thread.

### O151 ‚ÄĒ PARSEVAL RESTORATION (anti-laundering note) + the sharpened finite-field tower: threshold exponent HALVED (2026-06-11, #357 surface (ii))

  at |F| < 2^256 the unconditional-census layer now covers domains up to n = 128

### O153 ‚ÄĒ THE BAND-j COLLAPSE THEOREM (proof worked out; formalization queued): for distance > 4j, at most j+1 bad scalars on band j ‚ÄĒ the staircase side of the profile law becomes a theorem (2026-06-11, #357 surface (i), the first general sup-side result)

**Theorem (band-j collapse).** Let `C` be a linear code of distance `d > 4j` on `n` points,
and `őī` with `j ‚Č§ őīn < j+1`. Then every stack `(u‚āÄ, u‚āĀ)` has at most `j + 1` bad scalars:
`őĶ_mca(C, őī) ‚Č§ (j+1)/q`. With the in-tree `(j+1)`-spike lower bound (`epsMCA_generalJ_ge`),
the staircase law `őĶ_mca¬∑q = j+1` on band `j` is EXACT for high-distance codes.

**Proof.** Badness of `ő≥` at agreement `‚Č• n ‚ąí j` gives a codeword `c_ő≥` and error
`w_ő≥ := L_ő≥ ‚ąí c_ő≥` of weight `‚Č§ j`, where `L_ő≥ = u‚āÄ + ő≥u‚āĀ`. Fix a bad `ő≥‚āĀ`. For bad `ő≥`,
`(ő≥ ‚ąí ő≥‚āĀ)u‚āĀ = (c_ő≥ ‚ąí c_{ő≥‚āĀ}) + (w_ő≥ ‚ąí w_{ő≥‚āĀ})`, so the coset `(ő≥‚ąíő≥‚āĀ)u‚āĀ + C` contains the
weight-`‚Č§ 2j` word `w_ő≥ ‚ąí w_{ő≥‚āĀ}` ‚ÄĒ unique since `d > 4j`. Cross-consistency for two bad
`ő≥, ő≥'` (with `őĽ = ő≥‚ąíő≥‚āĀ`, `őĽ' = ő≥'‚ąíő≥‚āĀ`): `őĽ'(w_ő≥ ‚ąí w_{ő≥‚āĀ}) ‚ąí őĽ(w_{ő≥'} ‚ąí w_{ő≥‚āĀ}) ‚ąą C` has
weight `‚Č§ 4j < d`, hence is zero. Setting `v := (w_ő≥‚āā ‚ąí w_{ő≥‚āĀ})/őĽ‚āā` (any second bad scalar;
`v ‚Č° u‚āĀ mod C`), this yields the **rigid relation `w_ő≥ = w_{ő≥‚āĀ} + (ő≥‚ąíő≥‚āĀ)¬∑v` pointwise** for
every bad `ő≥`.

Case `|supp v| ‚Č• j+1`: for each `x ‚ąą supp v`, the map `ő≥ ‚Ü¶ w_ő≥(x) = w_{ő≥‚āĀ}(x) + (ő≥‚ąíő≥‚āĀ)v(x)`
is injective-affine in `ő≥`, so it vanishes for at most ONE bad `ő≥`: each `x ‚ąą supp v` lies
in `supp w_ő≥` for at least `t ‚ąí 1` of the `t` bad scalars. Summing:
`t¬∑j ‚Č• ő£_ő≥ wt(w_ő≥) ‚Č• |supp v|¬∑(t‚ąí1) ‚Č• (j+1)(t‚ąí1)`, forcing **`t ‚Č§ j+1`**.

Case `|supp v| ‚Č§ j`: `u‚āĀ ‚Č° v mod C` with `v` short. The pair-clause criterion (any nonzero
codeword agreeing with a weight-`‚Č§ 2j` word on `n ‚ąí j` points has weight `‚Č§ 3j < d`, hence
is zero) reduces badness of `ő≥` to: `supp v ‚äĄ supp(w_{ő≥‚āĀ} + (ő≥‚ąíő≥‚āĀ)v)`, i.e. some
`x ‚ąą supp v` has `w_{ő≥‚āĀ}(x) + (ő≥‚ąíő≥‚āĀ)v(x) = 0`, i.e. `ő≥ = ő≥‚āĀ ‚ąí w_{ő≥‚āĀ}(x)/v(x)` for some
`x ‚ąą supp v` ‚ÄĒ at most `|supp v| ‚Č§ j` bad scalars beyond... and including the bookkeeping
at `ő≥‚āĀ`, **`t ‚Č§ j+1`**. ‚ąé

(`d = 7 > 4`): exact max 2 = j+1 ‚úď; all band-0 instances ‚úď (the proven universal bands).

### MDSStaircaseConjecture REFUTED ‚ÄĒ the degenerate-pencil explosion; 3b‚ąí2 is sharp for RS/MDS too (#357)

The "MDS rank conjecture" (RS keeps the staircase collapse down to `d ‚Č• 2b`; directed-search
record at five instances, restated round 5 as Pad√© full-rank) is FALSE. The missed branch:
the **perfect-square pencil syzygy** `rA¬≤ ‚ąí hAB + pB¬≤ = ŌĀőĽ¬≤¬∑T^(f+2(b‚ąí1))` with
`A = B + őĽT^(b‚ąí1)`, `p = r = ŌĀT^f`, `h = 2ŌĀT^f` ‚ÄĒ a degenerate-discriminant solution to the
collinearity system, valid mod `T^m` for every `m = 2(b‚ąí1)+f ‚Č§ 3b‚ąí4`. Realization: any
family of disjoint `(b‚ąí1)`-blocks of the domain with locators in one pencil `‚ü®B, T^(b‚ąí1)‚ü©`
‚ÄĒ **cosets of `őľ_(b‚ąí1)`** on smooth domains (locators `1 ‚ąí x^(b‚ąí1)T^(b‚ąí1)`), equal-sum
pairs at `b = 3` on any domain ‚ÄĒ donates one bad scalar `ő≥_V` per block
(`(1+ő≥_V)B + őĽT^(b‚ąí1) ‚ąĚ V`), with error words from the partial fractions of
`ŌĀ(1+ő≥)T^f/V` (all weights nonzero) and the affine-in-ő≥ syndrome family exact. Count:
**n/(b‚ąí1), field-independent, on every `d ‚ąą [2b‚ąí1, 3b‚ąí3]`**.

Probe `probe_mds_pencil_explosion.py` (exact end-to-end mcaEvent scans): (11,8,3) d=6 ‚Üí 4
bad; (17,8,3) d=6 ‚Üí 4; **(19,18,10) d=9=2b+1, b=4 ‚Üí 6 bad** (the in-hypothesis formal
instance, k+2b = 18 ‚Č§ n); (19,18,11) d=8=2b ‚Üí 6; (19,18,12) d=7=2b‚ąí1 ‚Üí 6; (17,8,4) d=5
band 3 ‚Üí 4 (settles the widened band-3 sweep: ‚Č• 4 = n/2). Lean countermodel:
`MCAMDSStaircaseRefuted.lean` (`mdsStaircaseConjecture_refuted`, five root-counting
certificates, axiom-clean).

**Why the directed searches missed it:** (i) band-3 `d = 6` exhaustive rows were at
`n = 7`, where only 3 disjoint pencil pairs fit (geometric starvation ‚ÄĒ at `n = 7` the
fourth pencil member degenerates to an improper singleton fraction, consistent with the

105 pair-partitions (the equal-sum one) with the ő≥-quadruple M√∂bius-determined by the
block products; (iii) the `(17,8,3)` "d=6 holds (B=3)" sweep was wt‚Č§2-template-restricted
‚ÄĒ the pencil stack has weight 5. Constraint kept: collapse-side proofs below `3b‚ąí2`
cannot exist; the binding obstruction is pencil capacity (`n/(b‚ąí1) ‚Č• b+1` ‚üļ
`n ‚Č• b¬≤‚ąí1`-ish), not parallel matroid classes.

**Corrected landscape:** (a) `TheGeneralStaircaseLaw` (`d ‚Č• 3b‚ąí2`) is THE law for ALL
linear codes including RS/MDS ‚ÄĒ no MDS/general separation at the threshold level (the
d = 2b separation read off `MCAHalfDistanceGeneralRefuted` was an artifact of `n = 8 <`

staircase (`MCAStaircaseRS`/`BandExactness`) hypotheses `3(b‚ąí1)+k ‚Č§ n`-ish are TIGHT ‚ÄĒ
the strip above is genuinely explosive; (d) `MonomialDomination` gains its sharpest live
falsifier: the pencil stacks are non-monomial 9-sparse words ‚ÄĒ whether a monomial pair
matches count n/(b‚ąí1) at the strip cells is now the decisive check.

### MonomialDomination REFUTED at the boundary row ‚ÄĒ the triangle-incidence stack (#357)

The v3 pin's named surface fails at `d = 2b‚ąí1`. At `C84 = RS[F‚āĀ‚āá, őľ‚āą, 4]`, őī = 1/4
(agreement 6, band 3, boundary row d = 5): the two-triangle incidence stack
`v‚āÄ = (0,8,16,0,‚Ä¶)`, `v‚āĀ = (10,15,5,0,‚Ä¶)` (affine line in the 2-dim intersection of the
column spans of exponent triangles {0,1,2} and {3,4,6}) carries SEVEN bad scalars
{0,4,7,9,10,12,13} ‚ÄĒ formal: `MonomialDominationBoundaryRefuted.lean`
(`epsMCA_quarter_ge_seven`, seven `interp_kill` certificates) ‚ÄĒ while every monomial pair
has ‚Č§ 4 (exhaustive probe, max at (X‚Ā∂,X‚Āī); named numeric surface `MonomialBoundaryBound`).
`monomialDomination_refuted_of_monomial_bound`: the bound ‚üĻ ¬¨MonomialDomination for every
crossing ac ‚Č§ 5.

**Mechanism (probes `probe_boundary_row_incidence.py` / `probe_boundary_triangle_stratum.py`
/ `probe_boundary_n12_coset_triangles.py`):** at the boundary row the per-block syndrome
spaces R_B (codim b‚ąí1) admit lines in the intersection of two triangle spans ‚ÄĒ 3 scalars
per triangle + extra-pair incidences. Boundary band-3 law: value = n when 3 ‚ą£ n (coset
triangles; the excess pair (X^{n‚ąí2},X^{n‚ąí3}) is then itself coset-structured and TIES ‚ÄĒ
the n=12 cell (13,12,8) gives 12 = n for both); value = 7 = 3¬∑‚Ćän/3‚Ćč+1 at n = 8 (3 ‚ą§ n,
two generic triangles + one extra pair; two-triangle stratum exhaustive, mcaEvent-filtered).
2-power smooth domains always have 3 ‚ą§ n ‚üĻ the defect case (triangles strictly beat
monomials) is the production shape. Single-block lines reach incidence q‚ąí1 but are
MCA-invisible (the O147 coset-witness phenomenon at the staircase level).

**Surviving v4 surface:** domination restricted to rows with d ‚Č• 2b (off the boundary

so the production conclusion of pin v3 is unaffected; the surface as stated is dead.

## O155 (2026-06-11, Johnson V1 finding 13 ‚ÄĒ DECISIVE): the rebased hbudget is UNSATISFIABLE; the correct frame is the ANCHORED original engine

**Claim attacked:** the landed capstone `ő≤Hensel_weight_bound_of_cell_budgets`
(StructuredWeightInduction.lean) can be discharged by instantiating its `hbudget`
hypothesis with the sharp untwisted per-cell budget
`nB = (D_R‚ąím‚ąíi1) + (d_R‚ąím)(D‚ąíd_H)` (finding 9's plan).

**Refutation (machine-checkable arithmetic):** the rebased reduced-need
`nB + (m‚ąí1)¬∑(D+1‚ąíd_H) + (őī+m‚ąí2)¬∑degW ‚Č§ (d_R‚ąí1)(D‚ąíd_H+1)` is unsatisfiable for ANY
`nB ‚Č• 0` at the genuine nonzero cell `m = sigmaLambda = d_R` once `d_R ‚Č• 3` and
`degW ‚Č• 1`: already `(m‚ąí1)(D+1‚ąíd_H) + (m‚ąí2)degW = (d_R‚ąí1)(D‚ąíd_H+1) + (d_R‚ąí2)degW`
exceeds the entire őĺ-budget. (Toy: D=10, d_H=2, degW=1, d_R=4, m=4: 27+2 = 29 > 27.)
High-card cells `m > d_R` are zero (Hasse order > Y-degree) but `m = d_R` is genuine.

**Root cause (paper line-level diagnosis, BCIKS20 A.2+A.4 re-read):** the rebased
constant `B‚āÄ = D+1‚ąíd_H` DOUBLE-COUNTS `degW`. The paper's invariant
`őõ(ő≤_t) ‚Č§ 1 + (t+1)őõ(W) + e_t őõ(őĺ)` carries the base's W-content in the `(t+1)őõ(W)`
schedule (base case: `őõ(T) = őõ(W)+1`), and the paper's per-term ledger closes with
EXACT EQUALITY (`D + (d‚ąí2)őõ(W) = 1 + őõ(őĺ)` with `őõ(őĺ) = (D‚ąí1)+(d‚ąí2)őõ(W)`), zero slack.
The paper's base-case assertion `őõ(T) = őõ(W)+1` is an implicit ANCHOR assumption
`g := D ‚ąí d_H ‚ąí degW = 0`, i.e. `totalDegree H = d_H + degW` with `D := totalDegree H`
(total degree achieved at the leading Y-monomial). At `g > 0` the paper's own claimed
B-budget `(D‚ąíő£őĽ)+(d‚ąíő£őĽ)őõ(W)` understates the A.2-weight of the cleared B by `b¬∑g`
per T-monomial, and the per-term ledger fails by `~(d_R‚ąíd_H)¬∑g` at `m = 1` cells; the
paper's real proof at `g > 0` is the VALUATION argument (`őõ(őĪ_t) = őõ(Y) = g+1`), which
the recursion-telescoping route cannot reproduce without Newton-polygon machinery.

**The closable frame (verified by complete cell-ledger):** at the anchor `g = 0`
(`D‚āÄ := d_H + degW = totalDegree H`, reduction-safety `weight_őõ_modByMonic_le` takes
`tot H ‚Č§ D` so the calculus is VALID at `D‚āÄ`), the ORIGINAL structured engine (B‚āÄ = 1,
in-tree `ő≤Hensel_weight_bound_structured` + `ő≤Hensel_weight_bound_zero_structured`,
whose anchor hypothesis is exactly `D ‚Č§ d_H+degW`) closes EVERY cell with the proven
`Lőĺ = (d_R‚ąí1)(D‚ąíd_H+1)` and the landed supplier
`hasseCoeffReprūĚí™_weight_le_of_total`:
- `m ‚Č• 2, i1 ‚Č• 1`: need `‚üļ DR ‚Č§ d_R+degW+i1` ‚úď (`DR ‚Č§ D‚āÄ = d_H+degW ‚Č§ d_R+degW`);
- `m ‚Č• 2, i1 = 0`: needs the őī-SAVED budget `nB = (DR‚ąím)+(d_R‚ąí1‚ąím)degW` (the
  finding-10 twist via the PROVEN `leadingCoeff_dvd_evalX_hasseDerivY_top` + a cleared-rep
  weight lemma), then `‚üļ DR ‚Č§ d_R+degW` ‚úď;
- `m = 1, i1 ‚Č• 1`: the TRUNCATED need-form loses the spare W; the engine genuinely has
  `nB ‚Č§ degW + Lőĺ` (per-term W-ledger `k+1 ‚Č§ k+2`), under which `‚üļ d_H ‚Č§ d_R+i1` ‚úď;
- `m = 0` (top): `nB ‚Č§ 1+2degW+Lőĺ` ‚úď.

**Action:** build the ANCHORED engine (per-case need-forms incl. the m=1 credit + the
i1=0 saved-budget lemma), deliver `őõ_{D‚āÄ}(ő≤_t) ‚Č§ (2t+1)d_R D‚āÄ`, convert upward via
D-monotonicity (`őõ_D ‚Č§ őõ_{D‚āÄ} + (d_H‚ąí1)(D‚ąíD‚āÄ)` on canonical reps). Johnson becomes
conditional on the single ANCHOR hypothesis `totalDegree H = d_H + degW` at the
application point ‚ÄĒ to be checked against the GS factor structure (for proper factors
`d_H < d_R` of `R_{x‚āÄ}`, `degW ‚Č§ D‚ąíd_R < D‚ąíd_H` at the GLOBAL D, so the anchor holds
only at the PER-FACTOR D‚āÄ, which is what the engine now uses).

## 2026-06-11 ‚ÄĒ Items 11/13/14 (char-0 faces) DECIDED: the 4-adic quartet-tower law; the family's window interior has NO field-independent floor

Probe `probe_char0_death_law.py` (exact ‚Ą§[ő∂] folding): the char-0 constrained census
`Z0(n, a, c) = #{a-subsets of őľ_n : e‚āā = ‚Ä¶ = e_{c+1} = 0 in char 0}`:

**The mechanism (exact algebra, replaces the O143 enrichment statistics).** A quartet
`{x, ix, ‚ąíx, ‚ąíix}` has characteristic polynomial `T‚Āī ‚ąí x‚Āī`, so a union of `q` quartets
has `e_j = 0 for ALL j ‚ČĘ 0 (mod 4)` and `e_{4s} = ¬Īe_s(x‚āĀ‚Āī, ‚Ä¶, x_q‚Āī)` ‚ÄĒ **the census
recurses down the 4-adic tower**: constraints at depth `c` on `őľ_n` become a
vanishing-sum system at depth `‚Ćä(c+1)/4‚Ćč` on `őľ_{n/4}`. Verified exactly:
* `Z0(n, 8, 2) = C(n/4, 2)` (quartet pairs): 6 at n=16, 28 at n=32 ‚úď
* `Z0(16, 9, 2) = C(4,2)¬∑8 = 48` (+ free point) ‚úď; `Z0(n, a, c) = 0` for a ‚Č° 2, 3 mod 4 ‚úď
* `Z0(16, 8, 3) = 2`, `Z0(32, 8, 3) = 4` = antipodal pairs in `őľ_{n/4}` ‚úď ‚ÄĒ and the
  solutions are exactly the two cosets of `őľ_{n/2}`, which survive EVERY depth
  (`ő†(T ‚ąí y) = T^{n/2} ‚ąí const`): `Z0(16, 8, c) = 2` for all c ‚Č§ 6 ‚úď
* `Z0(16, 12, c)`: 20 / 4 / **0** / 0 at c = 1/2/3/4 ‚ÄĒ death at c = 3 exactly as the
  recursion predicts (`e‚āĄ = 0` forces a 3-term vanishing sum on `őľ‚āĄ`: odd size,
  impossible by the proven 2-power Lam‚ÄďLeung negation-symmetry) ‚úď

**The structural verdict.** At rate 1/4 the window-interior rungs (`k+1 < a < n/2`)
of the adjacent-pair family are char-0 DEAD beyond the first rung: parity kills
`a ‚Č° 2, 3 (mod 4)` and the odd-recursion Lam‚ÄďLeung kill takes the rest; the only
survivors are the first rung `a = k+1` (near capacity) and the subgroup cosets AT the
Johnson boundary `a = n/2`. **The family's window-interior census at large p is pure
characteristic-p surplus (the Weil-fluctuation layer) with NO field-independent
floor** ‚ÄĒ the conjecturally-extremal family cannot pin an interior őī* through its
char-0 layer; any interior pin from this family must come from the char-p layer,
i.e. from below-‚ąöq character-sum information (the same open kernel, fourth face).

This is the asymptotic law item 11 asked for (char-0 face), the exact algebra item 13
asked for (quartet-tower classification replaces the 1.8ŌÉ enrichment), and the tower
closure item 14 asked for (the 4-adic recursion IS the closed form; layer 1 at
`n = 2^20` is tower-computable by iterating it).

## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ WindowRationalBounded REFUTED: the normalizer-pair family (n/2 bad scalars, every field)

The named Prop of the below-UDR capstone (`WBPencilBelowUDR.lean`) asserted every
doubly-WB-solvable stack has ‚Č§ w+3 bad scalars.  **FALSE at high rate.**  The
counterexamples are an explicit, q-independent family derived from the CRT/projective
class reduction at the first beyond-ladder slice `n = 3w+k‚ąí1` (j = 0):

* **The alignment identity.**  For `c ‚ąą őľ_n` and any `őĺ ‚ąČ őľ_n ‚ą™ {0}`:
  `(őĺ‚ąíőĪ)(őĺ‚ąíc/őĪ) = (őĺ¬≤/c)¬∑((c/őĺ)‚ąíőĪ)((c/őĺ)‚ąíc/őĪ)` ‚ÄĒ equivalently, every involution pair
  `T = {őĪ, c/őĪ}` has the SAME projective class `[Z_T] ‚ąą (F[X]/‚Ąď)^√ó/F^√ó` for
  `‚Ąď = (X‚ąíőĺ)(X‚ąíc/őĺ)`.  Taking `‚Ąď‚āÄ, ‚Ąď‚āĀ` two such quadratics (roots off-domain,
  coprime) and `R_j` the canonical representatives aligning one pair `T‚āÄ`, EVERY
  pair `T = {őĪ, c/őĪ}` yields `M := c_T¬∑Z_{D‚ąĖT} ‚Č° ‚Ąď‚āĀR‚āÄ (mod ‚Ąď‚āÄ) ‚Č° ő≥(T)¬∑‚Ąď‚āÄR‚āĀ (mod ‚Ąď‚āĀ)`,
  i.e. `ő≥(T)` is line-explainable with witness `D‚ąĖT`.
* **No-joint is free**: a genuinely rational row (`‚Ąď ‚ą§ R`, `‚Ąď` domain-nonvanishing)
  is NEVER deg-<k-explainable on n‚ąíw ‚Č• w+k points (degree forcing) ‚ÄĒ so every
  explainable ő≥ is mca-BAD; the (n‚ąí2)/2 (c square) pair-scalars are distinct.
* **Verified** (`probe_normalizer_pair_family.py`, predicted = exact everywhere,
  independent second implementation at the violation): (37,12,7,2): 5; (97,12,7,2): 5
  (q-independent); **(97,16,11,2): 7 > 5 = w+3 ‚ÄĒ REFUTED at the 2-power production
  shape**; (113,16,11,2): 7; **(41,20,15,2): 9 > 5**.
* **Why never seen before**: all prior window probes ran at `n = q‚ąí1` (full
  multiplicative group), where NO off-domain őĺ exists ‚ÄĒ the family needs `q‚ąí1 > n+4`.
  The "window cap = w+1" empirics (scales 1‚Äď2) were an n = q‚ąí1 artifact.
* **The w ‚Č• 4 collapse** (probed): full-degree denominators (products of w/2
  involution quadratics) are REQUIRED (else deg P ‚Č• k), and then the per-quadratic
  scalar-consistency constraints kill all unions except T‚āÄ (predicted = exact = 1 at
  (37,12,1,4), (97,16,5,4), (61,20,9,4)).  The őľ_w-coset variant (`‚Ąď = X^w ‚ąí e`,

  `t^w`) for every `w ‚ą£ n` ‚ÄĒ the general-w family is őė(n/w), not n^{w/2}.
* **Structural reading**: the two maximal w = 2 families are exactly the
  torus-normalizer involutions `x ‚Ü¶ c/x` and `x ‚Ü¶ ‚ąíx` ‚ÄĒ the same normalizer the
  O133/O144 moment census isolates.  Maximal aligned-pair families = normalizer

**Surviving form** (`WindowRationalLinear`, repaired budget): doubly-WB-solvable

## 2026-06-11 ‚ÄĒ Ownership degeneracy REFUTED: the count mechanism EXPLAINS the window cap

`probe_ownership_degeneracy.py` at (13,6,1,w=2), k = 1 (residual of a pair =

ownership bound `‚Ćä30/8‚Ćč`.  The mechanism, exactly: the extremal's `u‚āĀ = (0,1,1,0,2,2)`
has value-multiplicity `őľ = 2`, every witness has `|S| ‚Č• 4`, so ownership
`‚Č• |S|(|S|‚ąíőľ) = 4¬∑2 = 8` ‚ÄĒ measured exactly.

### O136 ‚ÄĒ THE TAIL STRUCTURE THEOREMS: parity purity kills every odd r > s/2 at every scale (T1, PROVEN); marginal(32) = 1,728,112 is COMPLETE (T4); turn-on is monotone (T3); r_max = 2j‚ąí5 REFUTED by 29 certificates; sharp law r¬≤ ‚Č§ s+1 conjectured at 26/26 ‚ÄĒ and the r=5 taxonomy derivation passes adversarial audit (nubs, 2026-06-12)

Two audited deliverables (independent adversarial legs, both 0.93, zero fatal):
`scripts/probes/genlaw/exclusion/REPORT.md` and `scripts/probes/genlaw/r5tax/DERIVED-99512.md`.

* **T1 [PROVEN, every odd r, every s = 2^j]: parity purity.** The odd-exponent part of the
  balance multiset is exactly the mixed-parity products; its vanishing sum factors as U¬∑V
  over ‚Ą§[ő∂_n], and either factor vanishing forces one parity side empty (distinct fibers
  occupy distinct antipodal classes). **Corollary: N_r(s) = 0 unconditionally for r > s/2**
  ‚ÄĒ the entire deep tail, all scales, no enumeration. (Generalizes O108's L1 and the r=5
  3-torsion argument in one stroke.)
* **T4: s = 32 marginal CLOSED.** N_r(32) = 0 for ALL odd r ‚Č• 7 (r=7: full 215,414,784-config
  sweep; r=7..15: pure-only exhaustive, legitimate by T1; r ‚Č• 17: T1 corollary). **O130's

## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ The level-j staircase is NOT the complete envelope: the ANTIPODAL PENCIL; the d = 4 level-1 rung pin is REFUTED

**Attempt** (#371 round 7): pin the first sub-ceiling rung exactly ‚ÄĒ `őī* = 5/8` for the
`n = 16, d = 2` code on `‚ü®4134‚ü© ‚äÜ F‚āĀ‚āā‚āā‚āą‚āČň£` at budgets `őĶ* < 32/p`, with the good side
"every stack has few bad scalars below `5/8`" (the staircase-completeness hope: only the
deeper level-`j` sign-subset families are bad below a rung, counts `5 ‚Üí 1`).

**Disproof of completeness.** The corrected adversarial probe (`probe_level1_pin.py`;
the old S6 "worst = 1" was a search artifact ‚ÄĒ its pool capped monomial exponents at 4)
found the **antipodal pencil** `(X^h, X^{h+1})`, `h = n/2`: since `x^h = ¬Ī1`, the line
`x^h(1+ő≥x)` *is* the degree-1 word `¬Ī(1+ő≥X)` on a full antipodal half-coset plus the one
rotating cross-coset point `x‚āÄ = ‚ąí1/ő≥`, and the direction `¬Īx` single-deviates there.
**All `n` scalars of the inversion orbit `‚ąí1/‚ü®g‚ü©` are bad at radius `1 ‚ąí (h+1)/n`**, for
every code degree `1 ‚Č§ d ‚Č§ h ‚ąí 1` ‚ÄĒ strictly below the deepest staircase rung (`1/2`)
with count strictly above every deep-rung spectrum (`16 > 5 = N(2,2)` at the instance).
Proven axiom-clean, in general:

```lean
ArkLib.ProximityGap.Level1Rung.antipodal_pencil_epsMCA_lower_bound   -- n bad at 1‚ąí(h+1)/n
ArkLib.ProximityGap.Level1Rung.mcaDeltaStar_le_antipodal             -- őī* ‚Č§ 1‚ąí(h+1)/n at őĶ* < n/p
```

**Consequences (Level1RungPin.lean, all axiom-clean).**
- The `d = 4` (rate 5/16) level-1 rung `1/2` is **refuted as a pin point on its entire
  band**: the pencil count `16` equals the rung budget `K‚āĀ = 16`, so for every
  `őĶ* < 16/p`, `őī* ‚Č§ 7/16 < 1/2` (`deltaStar_lt_levelOne_rung_F12289_d4`); the per-rung
  interior obligation there is **unsatisfiable** (`level1_interior_unsat_F12289_d4`).
- The `d = 2` rung `5/8` **survives**, with its conditional band trapped to
  `[16/p, 32/p)` (`level1_interior_floor16_F12289`); probed worst stack at threshold 7
  is exactly 16 = the pencil. Envelope-exactness there = ONE named obligation
  (`SubCeilingInteriorCeiling` via `subceiling_deltaStar_pin_of_interior`, the general
  per-rung reduction), and the in-tree per-witness subset counting **provably cannot
  discharge it**: absolute scheme cap `C(16,4)/C(7,4) = 52 > 31` (the saturation theorem
  made concrete; `level1_budget_le_subset_cap`).
- The pencil ladder continues: `(X^h, X^{h+2})` gives 8 bad at radius `3/8` (probed,
  exact, `p ‚ąą {97, 12289}`). The refined envelope = staircase ‚äĒ pencil ladder (‚äĒ ...?)
  is the successor question; any future "complete envelope" claim must subsume the
  pencil orbit.

### O156 ‚ÄĒ THE CONSTANT-6 LAW IS TWO-SIDED at n = 8..64, and the ‚Č§-side target is a Beukers‚ÄďSmyth sharpening: M(32) = M(64) = 6 rigorous via the invisibility trichotomy; the witness curve is conjugate-reciprocal exactly as BS's structure theorem demands (normalizer-gap lane, 2026-06-12; follow-up to O155)

  only if p divides one of them (the invisibility trichotomy ‚ÄĒ audited against the
  census skip-paths; note: the naive "p | content" exclusion is INVALID since
  reduction is evaluation, not coefficientwise ‚ÄĒ replaced by the norm/divisibility
  lemma); per-plane pigeonhole: 6 clean primes kill n=32, 11 (Hadamard) / 12 (L1)
  kill n=64; ladders ran 8 and 12 primes, ALL max = 6, bit-identical histograms.
  Combined with O155's char-0 anchor: **M(n) = 6, both directions, n ‚ąą {8,16,32,64}**
  (program-assisted: exact arithmetic + symbolic self-checks; not yet Lean).
* **The Laurent collapse** (from the ‚Č•6 brick design): under ő∂^m = ‚ąí1 the witness
  datum is m-INDEPENDENT ‚ÄĒ z¬∑c = (ő∂‚ąí1)¬≤, ő∂¬≤¬∑d = ‚ąí(ő∂‚ąí1)¬≤(ő∂¬≥+ő∂¬≤‚ąí1),
  ő∂¬≤¬∑a = ‚ąí(ő∂‚ąí1)¬≤(ő∂¬≥‚ąíő∂‚ąí1), b = ‚ąí(ő∂‚ąí1)¬≤, ő∂‚Āī(ad‚ąíbc) = (ő∂‚ąí1)‚Ā∂(ő∂+1)¬≤(ő∂¬≤+ő∂+1): ONE fixed
  M√∂bius map realizes the 6 points at every 2-power level; incidences are ring
  identities for ALL m ‚Č• 2. Lean brick (MobiusCoincidenceWitness.lean) in flight.
* **The ‚Č§6-for-all-n question is exactly a Beukers‚ÄďSmyth sharpening** (lit-gated at
  the source, Number Theory for the Millennium I 2002): BS bound = 22¬∑V (V = 1 for
  bidegree (1,1)); sharp constant OPEN even for BS (16 ‚Č§ C ‚Č§ 22); their ¬ß5.6 covers
  only the symmetric rational family xy + őĽ(x+y) + 1 (max 4). CONSISTENCY FALSIFIER
  PASSED: BS cap non-reciprocal curves at 4V ‚ÄĒ any curve beating 4 MUST be
  conjugate-reciprocal (f ~ fŐĄ(x‚ĀĽ¬Ļ,y‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)) with ‚Ąö^ab coefficients; our witness curve
  verified exactly that (inversion + conjugation returns it with unit factor 1/ő∂).
  So the open branch of the ‚Č§6 theorem is ONLY the conjugate-reciprocal
  abelian-coefficient (1,1)-family ‚ÄĒ explicitly parameterizable via the
  reciprocity relations; census ground truth: count-6 maximizer classes are rich
  (34 at n=16, 210 at n=32, all partial injections ‚ÄĒ finite-list classification
  impossible, the uniform mechanism must be BS's f‚Ä†/seven-polynomial machinery).
