## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ The UDR-edge gap of the universal dichotomy is INTRINSIC (analysis)

The universal below-UDR law covers `2w + 2k ‚Č§ n`; the band `n ‚ąą [2w+k+1, 2w+2k)`
(width `k/n` in radius) is a genuine gap of the method, not bookkeeping: for a
direction at distance `e ‚ąą (w, w+k]` from the code (max agreement
`a ‚ąą [n‚ąíw‚ąík, n‚ąíw)`), BOTH branches vanish ‚ÄĒ the multiplicity factor
`n‚ąíw‚ąík‚ąíőľ = e‚ąíw‚ąík ‚Č§ 0`, and the sparse/packing popular count needs witness-minus-
support `m = n‚ąíw‚ąíe ‚Č• k`, which fails exactly there (at `m < k` even a single
codeword is not determined by the off-support agreement, so the popularity
argument cannot start).  Closing the band needs a counting mechanism for
explaining codewords pinned by FEWER than `k` points plus the ő≥-line structure ‚ÄĒ
the same shape as the at-UDR boundary slice (`B6 = 7`) analysis.  Logged as the
fifth documented no-go; the band is the precise below-UDR residue.

## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ THE TUBE EXPERIMENT: ladder extremality at threshold REFUTED; far directions attain C(n,k+1) exactly; WB-3b live-verified beyond Johnson

`probe_tube_vs_spectrum.py` at the dimension-ladder instance (p = 12289, n = 8 = őľ‚āą,
k = 2, agreement t = 3 ‚ÄĒ the threshold radius őī = 5/8, beyond Johnson):

* **ladder stack (X¬≥, X¬≤): exactly 40 bad scalars** ‚ÄĒ byte-matches the sibling's
  spectrum law N(3,3) (checker cross-validated);
* **random far directions: 56 = C(8,3) ‚ÄĒ repeatedly, exactly** ‚ÄĒ every
  (k+1)-subset of a witness determines one scalar, all distinct generically: the
  ownership count is TIGHT, and far directions BEAT the spectrum family at the
  threshold (56 > 40).  The adjacent-pair family is NOT the threshold extremizer;
  the exact threshold sup is ‚Č• C(n,k+1)/p;
* **genuine codeword direction: 0 bad** ‚ÄĒ WB-3b verified computationally at a
  beyond-Johnson radius (live red-team pass for the Lean theorem);
* the sibling's őī*-pin is UNAFFECTED (it is a sup over radii strictly below the
  threshold; the good-side ownership bound applies there).

**Theorem landed** (`strongly_far_badScalars_card_mul_le`, axiom-clean): directions
with max codeword agreement ‚Č§ k satisfy `#bad ¬∑ (n‚ąíw).descFactorial (k+1) ‚Č§ n^{k+1}`
at EVERY radius ‚ÄĒ at the boundary slice this is ‚Čą C(n,k+1), matching the measured
56 within the ordered-count factor.  The exact threshold value of őĶ_mca at the
boundary slice is now bracketed `[C(n,k+1), n^{k+1}/(k+1)!]`-ish for the far class,
with the far class provably extremal over the spectrum family.
## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ UDR-edge band amendment: RADIUS coverage closed by composition (the no-go stands for the dichotomy budget)

The fifth no-go above is intrinsic to the universal DICHOTOMY's two branches, not to the
radius band: the subset-ownership law (`march_badScalars_card_mul_le`, glueing constant)
is radius-free, so the band `n ‚ąą [2w+k+1, 2w+2k)` moves at the subset budget
`C(n,k+1)/((k+1)¬∑p)` ‚ÄĒ `UDREdgeClosure.lean` (`udrEdgeBand_closure`,
`le_mcaDeltaStar_subset_law_w`).  Below-UDR radius coverage is gapless at every rate.
What remains open on the band is only the BUDGET gap: the dichotomy's
`n^{k+1}/(n‚ąí2w‚ąí2k+1)^k` shape is unavailable there; recovering it needs the ő≥-line
mechanism (explainers of bad scalars move polynomially `p_ő≥ = P(ő≥)` with codeword
coefficients; the column dichotomy ‚ÄĒ ‚Č§ a‚ąí1 identically-zero columns else joint ‚ÄĒ gives
`#bad ‚Č§ n¬∑deg P`; the missing piece is SLOPE COLLAPSE: `deg P ‚Č§ 1` on the band, probe
`_scratch_probe_slope_collapse.py`).

**Generic-domain addendum (same day):** the band is also closed for EVERY injective
evaluation domain (no smoothness) by a second independent mechanism ‚ÄĒ the ő≥-preserving
puncture descent (`BelowUDRPuncture.lean`): a direction vanishing at a witness point
`x‚āÄ` forces every explainer through `(x‚āÄ, u‚āÄ x‚āÄ)` at every ő≥, and dividing the instance
by `(X ‚ąí x‚āÄ)` maps `(n, k, w) ‚Üí (n‚ąí1, k‚ąí1, w)` at the same ő≥ with `n ‚ąí k` (hence the
UDR slack ŌÉ = n‚ąí2w‚ąík) invariant; induction on k bottoms at the k = 1 universal law.
Result: `#bad¬∑(n‚ąí2w‚ąík) ‚Č§ n^{k+1}` on ALL of `2w+k+1 ‚Č§ n` ‚ÄĒ one statement, the whole
below-UDR range, generic domain (`belowUDR_badScalars_card_mul_le`,
`le_mcaDeltaStar_belowUDR`, `udrEdgeBand_closure_generic`; probe
`probe_edgeband_puncture.py`, 633/633 descent checks).  Budget comparison stands: on
smooth domains in the band the subset budget `C(n,k+1)/((k+1)p)` is sharper, and on
`2w+2k ‚Č§ n` the dichotomy budget is sharper ‚ÄĒ the descent adds generality (any domain),
uniformity (one statement), and a reusable ő≥-preserving transfer mechanism (it is
radius-agnostic: above UDR it descends every bad scalar whose witness meets the
direction's zero set; the complementary stratum has witnesses concentrated on the
support).  (Slope collapse was refuted the same day ‚ÄĒ see the entry above; the
descent bound here is unaffected, and the surviving two-regime pencil law plus the
moment-fiber question remain the named budget targets on smooth domains.)

## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ SLOPE COLLAPSE REFUTED (directed t=2 construction); the surviving two-regime pencil law

The slope-collapse conjecture (UDR-edge amendment above: every band bad-family's
explainer map is affine, `deg P ‚Č§ 1`) is FALSE as stated: the explicit quadratic-pencil
construction (`_scratch_probe_nonaffine_directed.py`, wt371) produces genuine nonaffine
bad families wherever the scalar field gives room.  Witness at the band instance
`(p, n, k, w) = (73, 9, 2, 3)`, `a = 6`:
`u‚āÄ = (64, 48, 33, 62, 37, 56, 46, 22, 36)`, `u‚āĀ = (5, 29, 30, 32, 65, 5, 29, 61, 55)`,
bad scalars `{12, 16, 36}`, no affine selection (verified over all explainer pairs).
The first random probe's zero-nonaffine reading at `p = 17, 19` was a small-field
artifact.  Design: column polys `‚ąím‚āā(i)(ő≥‚ąír‚āĀŠĶĘ)(ő≥‚ąír‚āāŠĶĘ)` with `m‚āā` a nonzero codeword and
the `ő≥¬Ļ`/`ő≥‚Āį` coefficients absorbed into the FREE `őĶ`/`u‚āÄ` ‚ÄĒ t = 2 needs no rigidity
beyond the top coefficient.

**What survives (verified by the same construction): the two-regime pencil law.**
Identically-zero columns of a degree-`t` pencil:
* `t = 1`: capped at `a ‚ąí 1` by joint-exclusion only ‚üĻ `#bad_affine ‚Č§ (n‚ąíz)/(a‚ąíz)`,
  maximal `w + 1` at `z = a‚ąí1`;
* `t ‚Č• 2`: the TOP Newton coefficient `m_t` is a nonzero codeword (divided differences
  of codewords), so `z ‚Č§ k ‚ąí 1` ‚üĻ `#bad ‚Č§ t¬∑(n‚ąík+1)/(a‚ąík+1)` ‚ÄĒ at the band ‚Čą `2t`.
  The probe's max nonaffine family (3) meets the `t = 2` cap `‚Ćä2(n‚ąík+1)/(a‚ąík+1)‚Ćč = 3`
  exactly; it CANNOT beat the affine cap at `t = 2`.

**The open t-control, reduced:** beating `w + 1` needs `t ‚Čą w/2`, and a degree-`t`
pencil constrains `t ‚ąí 2` symmetric functions of every column's root set to be
codeword-ratios ‚ÄĒ for non-constant ratios, ‚Č§ `k‚ąí1` columns per value; for constant
ratios, the root `t`-sets lie in a fiber of `t ‚ąí 2` prescribed power sums of the scalar
set `őď`.  So the band adversary beyond affine is a SCALAR-SIDE deep moment-fiber
extremal problem (the census programme's object, transposed from the domain to the
scalar field): large nonaffine families need `|őď| ‚Čą w` scalar sets that are
moment-degenerate to depth `‚Čą w/2` with large fibers.  Conjectured cap (next probe):
additive moment fibers at depth `t‚ąí2` collapse the supply below the affine cap for all
`t ‚Č• 2` ‚üĻ `#bad ‚Č§ w + O(1)` on the band.
## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ `WindowPencilAnchored` is NOT universal: class V (vanishing-denominator rows)

The window pencil law (`WBPencilWindowLaw.lean`) counts ‚Č§ `(w+1)+n(w+1)+1` bad
scalars for stacks with an ANCHORED representation pair (some adjugate entry of
some square row-selection of the coefficient pencil ‚ČĘ 0, ‚üļ corank ‚Č§ 1 over
`F(ő≥)`).  The hope that every doubly-WB-solvable stack admits an anchored
representation is **FALSE**:

* **Class V** ‚ÄĒ rows that are *rational-with-exceptions* (`u = R/‚Ąď` off the
  domain zero set of `‚Ąď`, free at the zeros; the WB relation `‚Ąď(x_i)u_i = R(x_i)`
  forces `R` to vanish with `‚Ąď`, so these are honest WB rows) ‚ÄĒ generically has
  pencil corank ‚Č• 2 for EVERY representation pair: 103/130 fresh class-V stacks
  at `(13,6,1,2)` have NO anchored pair under FULL enumeration of both rows' WB
  solution spaces (`probe_wb_window_anchor_existential.py`); coranks up to 4
  occur (`probe_wb_window_corank2.py`).
* **The common-factor reduction is insufficient**: dividing the forced shared
  factor (the `(x‚ąía)`'s where `‚Ąď` and `R` jointly vanish) out of all four data
  polynomials preserves corank verbatim under the uniform caps, and with the
  correctly shrunken cofactor cap (`m ‚ąí z`) still leaves a residue class
  degenerate ‚ÄĒ the survivors are recognizable: shared reduced denominators,
  post-reduction polynomial rows (`probe_wb_window_reduced_anchor.py`).
* **The saving observation**: every unanchored stack found has ‚Č§ 2 mca-bad
  scalars.  Mechanism (the proof route for the corrected residual): for
  `gcd(‚Ąď‚āÄ‚Ąď‚āĀ, Z_D) = 1` data below UDR, two `F(ő≥)`-independent kernel solutions
  force `Q‚āĀZ‚āā = Q‚āāZ‚āĀ` (degree < n) and `Z_D ‚ą£ (A+ő≥B)g ‚ąí LŌĀ` for the gcd-split
  `Z_j = gő∂_j` ‚ÄĒ every kernel solution is a polynomial multiple of ONE primitive
  triple `(g, ŌĀ, c)` with slack in all three caps (the kernel is `K[x]`-cyclic);
  a bad scalar needs `g(ő≥,¬∑)` to split over the domain, i.e. the anchored
  incidence count re-runs on the primitive curve.

**Surviving form** (`WBPencilWindowCapstone.lean`): `UnanchoredLinear` ‚ÄĒ stacks
with no anchored pair have ‚Č§ `n` bad scalars (budget aligned with the repaired
`WindowRationalLinear`; strictly weaker than it via
`unanchoredLinear_of_windowRationalLinear`).  The below-UDR law
`őĶ_mca ‚Č§ ((n+1)(w+1)+1)/q` holds under this residual alone
(`epsMCA_le_below_udr_of_unanchoredLinear`), with the anchored bulk carried by
the proven pencil law.

## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ The deep-band supply: production closure via agreement caps is VACUOUS (analysis, not a refutation of the reduction)

The agreement-capped supply instance (`explainable_cores_card_of_agreement_le`:
`#explCores¬∑C(k+m+1,k) ‚Č§ C(n,k)¬∑C(A‚ąík,m+1)`) combined with the near-line
dichotomy (`near_scalar_unique`: at most one scalar's line exceeds agreement
`(n+k)/2`) does NOT close the production deep-band count: at `A ‚Čą (n+k)/2`,
`k ‚Čą n/2`, the bound `C(n,k)¬∑C(A‚ąík,m+1)/C(k+m+1,k) ‚Čą C(n,k)¬∑2^{‚ąí(m+1)}` is of
the same order as the witness mass `C(n,k+m+1)/q^m¬∑q^m`, leaving
`#badSet ‚Č≥ 2^{m+1}/q^m` ‚ÄĒ vacuous for `m ‚Č• 1` at production `q`.  The loss is
the `C(n,k)` factor from `k`-subset determination (only `q^k` codewords exist,
but `C(n,k)` `k`-subsets are counted).  Sharpening requires bounding the number
of codewords at agreement `‚Č• k+m+1` ‚ÄĒ below Johnson agreement that is
quantitatively the list-decoding wall (the known `őī*`‚ÜĒLD coupling).  The
supply-side mathematics above the wall is proven; the wall itself remains the
open core, now in its sharpest isolated form.
## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ round 9: the k-simplex packing collapses; the packing cap at `n` is FALSE (the overlap law)

Two round-8 census conjectures resolved in opposite directions
(`probe_packing_envelope.py`, `PackingEnvelope.lean`):

* **"k ‚Č• 3 disjoint (e+1)-simplex packings extend the envelope" ‚ÄĒ REFUTED.**
  The k-packing stack (`u‚āĀ = q‚āÄ|_{S‚āĀ}`, `u‚āÄ = (X¬∑q‚āÄ)|_{S‚āĀ}`, `q‚āÄ` vanishing on the
  complement `Z` of the union) only depends on `(s‚āĀ, z) = (|S‚āĀ|, |Z|)`: the `k‚ąí1`
  other blocks are an undifferentiated reservoir whose every point contributes one
  `(X‚ąíx)q‚āÄ`-alignment scalar.  Count `n ‚ąí z` at threshold `min(n‚ąís‚āĀ+1, s‚āĀ+z+1)`;
  the per-radius optimum is the bisimplex value, and any `k ‚Č• 2` packing's count
  `k(e+1)` is `‚Č§ 2E+2` at its own radius with the unified window implied
  (`kPacking_subsumed`, pure arithmetic).  Probe T2: at `(97,16,d=5), t=9` the
  `k=3,e=3` stack carries exactly its `12` while the `(s‚āĀ,z)=(8,0)` optimum carries
  `16` at the same threshold.  `k ‚Č• 3` never appears on the envelope.

* **"the packing count caps at `min(n, 2E+2)`" ‚ÄĒ REFUTED: the OVERLAP law.**
  The round-8 `t=7` hint (`20` at `(97,16,2)`) is a genuine char-0 family ‚ÄĒ
  hill-climbs at `p = 97` AND `p = 257` independently converge to `2(n‚ąít)+2 = 20`
  with identical structure (two `(E+1)`-point simplex blocks overlapping in
  `n‚ąí2t+2 = 4` points, zero leftovers).  Reverse-engineered mechanism: stack
  `(X, 1)` on `S‚ąĖY`, tuned `(a_x, b_x)` on the overlap `Y`, zero off `S`; each
  overlap point carries TWO bad scalars (its kill `‚ąía_x/b_x` and its align
  `(x‚ąía_x)/(b_x‚ąí1)`), so the count is `n + |Y|`, exceeding `n`.  Proven general
  (`overlap_packing_epsMCA_lower_bound`) and instantiated word-level exact at
  `(12289, 16, d=2)`: `20` bad scalars at radius `9/16` and `18` at `1/2`
  (probe-verified exact counts).  Grand packing law: `2(n‚ąít)+2` on the whole
  window `d+2 ‚Č§ t ‚Č§ (n+d+1)/2`.

Side observation (probe T1): at `t = d+3` cells, e.g. `(97,16,4), t=7`, sub-optimal
`(s‚āĀ,z)` stacks pick up large SMALL-FIELD surpluses (`34` observed vs `14` designed;
expected-count heuristic `C(10,5)¬∑C(6,2)/97 ‚Čą 39`) ‚ÄĒ the same codim-0 coincidence
species as the censused mod-17 extras; vanishes for `p ‚Čę poly(n)`.

### The any-domain sub-Johnson supply laws ‚ÄĒ REFUTED by the Frobenius secant family (Fable, 2026-06-13)

Context: the crossing/Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz campaign proved the mean-degree law `ő£|A| ‚Č§ 2n`
for pairwise-`‚Č§1` families down to the Johnson agreement `t¬≤ ‚Č• 2n`, and noted that
below it set systems blow up (projective planes), so "any proof must couple the word."
This entry sharpens the wall: **coupling the word is not enough ‚ÄĒ the blowup is
realized by genuine RS agreement families** (`FrobeniusSubfieldBlowup.lean`, 8 theorems,
axiom-clean; probe `probe_frobenius_blowup.py`, exact at `(p,e) = (3,2),(3,3),(5,2)`).

Mechanism (JH01/BSKR06 in the #389 charter objects): char `p`, `k = 2`, the Frobenius
word `w(z) = z^p` over any `ūĚĒĹ_p`-affine-closed domain (e.g. all of `ūĚĒĹ_{p^e}`, `n = p^e`).
Freshman's dream: the affine codeword `z‚āÄ^p + u^{p‚ąí1}(z‚ąíz‚āÄ)` agrees with `w` on the
ENTIRE affine line `z‚āÄ + ūĚĒĹ_p u` ‚ÄĒ every secant of the Frobenius graph is `p`-rich.
At level `t = p` (sub-Johnson: `t¬≤ = n^{2/e} ‚Č§ n`):

* **"`ő£|A| ‚Č§ 2n` (or any `C¬∑n`) below Johnson for RS agreement families" ‚ÄĒ REFUTED.**
  The family is pairwise-`‚Č§1`, every member exactly `p = t` points
  (`frobenius_explainable_inter_le_one`), and the mass SATURATES the universal pair
  bound exactly: `ő£|A| = n(n‚ąí1)/(p‚ąí1)` (`frobenius_mass_floor`,
  `frobenius_mass_gt_two_n`).  At `e = 2` this is `‚Čą n^{3/2}` at `t¬≤ = n` ‚ÄĒ failure
  one notch below the Johnson threshold `2n`, matching the projective-plane envelope
  (the family IS the affine plane `AG(2,p)`, sheared into a function graph).
* **"the capped optimum / explainable-core supply is linear in `n` at fixed `(k,m)`"
  (the growth-law census conjecture, measured at prime `q`) ‚ÄĒ REFUTED in any-domain
  form.**  At fixed `(k, m) = (2, p‚ąí3)`: explainable-`p`-core count
  `= n(n‚ąí1)/(p(p‚ąí1)) = őė(n¬≤)` (`frobenius_supply_floor`); against the named Prop:
  any `B` for `ExplainableCoreSupply dom 2 (p‚ąí3) B` has `n(n‚ąí1) ‚Č§ p(p‚ąí1)¬∑B`
  (`frobenius_charter_floor`).  The prime-`q` census probes were structurally blind
  to this: the mechanism needs a proper subfield.

What is NOT refuted: `ExplainableCoreSupply` with `B` subexponential in the witness
mass (the Frobenius supply is only quadratic ‚ÄĒ the self-cap `deg(X^p ‚ąí aX ‚ąí b) = p`
bounds every agreement at `p`); and the production setting `őľ_n ‚äā ūĚĒĹ_q` with `q` prime,
where the only `ūĚĒĹ_p`-lines have size `q ‚Čę n`.  Consequence for the programme: the
sub-Johnson supply statement is irreducibly DOMAIN-coupled ‚ÄĒ any proof must use the
absence of `ūĚĒĹ_p`-affine structure in the evaluation domain (no-large-subfield /
additive-unstructuredness of `őľ_n`), not just `w ‚ąČ C` and pairwise distance.

#### Addendum (same day, second lane): COMPOSITE subfields obstruct too ‚ÄĒ at every band (`SubplaneSupplyFloor.lean`)

The Frobenius-secant entry above leaves two gaps, both now closed
(`SubplaneSupplyFloor.lean`, axiom-clean; probe `probe_subplane_supply.py`, exact at
`F‚āČ/K=F‚āÉ`, `F‚āā‚āá/K=F‚āÉ`, `F‚āĀ‚āÜ/K=F‚āĄ`):

* **Any prime-power subfield `K`, `r = |K|` ‚ÄĒ not just the prime one.**  Shear
  `AG(2, K)` into `F` by `(a,b) ‚Ü¶ a + őĽb`, `őĽ ‚ąČ K`: domain `n = r¬≤` points, word
  `w(a+őĽb) = b`.  The `r¬≤+r` lines of the plane become honest degree-`<2` codewords
  (`y = (sx+c)/(1+őĽs)`, `y = (x‚ąía‚āÄ)/őĽ`) agreeing with `w` on exactly `r = ‚ąön` points
  each, pairwise `‚Č§ 1` (probe: agreement histogram is `{0,1,r}` ONLY).  In char 2 the
  Frobenius mechanism is vacuous (`t = 2` fills no core) ‚ÄĒ here `K = ūĚĒĹ_{2^j}` bites at
  every `j`: **binary-tower (char-2 production) fields carry the explosion whenever
  the evaluation domain contains a sheared `K`-plane; only PRIME fields are immune to
  the subfield mechanism.**
* **Every band at once**: `subplane_supply_floor` ‚ÄĒ any `B` for
  `ExplainableCoreSupply dom 2 m B` has `(r¬≤+r)¬∑C(r, 2+m+1) ‚Č§ B`, for ALL `m` with
  `2+m+1 ‚Č§ r` simultaneously (`‚Čą n^{(m+3)/2}`, superpolynomial at every fixed band
  depth) ‚ÄĒ vs the single tuned band `2+m+1 = p` of the secant floor.

Unchanged verdict, sharpened scope: the sub-Johnson supply statement is irreducibly
domain-coupled, the coupling needed is *absence of sheared-subfield-plane structure*,
and the surviving positive targets are prime fields and (conjecturally) smooth `őľ_n`
domains ‚ÄĒ for binary-tower deployments the supply route is closed unconditionally at
subfield-aligned domains.

## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ őľ_n-immunity is q-CONDITIONAL: prime-field, multiplicative-subgroup linear supply REFUTED at `n = őė(q)` (the generic-density phase)

Claim refuted: "`SubJohnsonSupplyResidual dom k m B` holds with `B = O(n)` (or any
`B = o(C(n,t)/q^{m+1})`, `t = k+m+1`) for multiplicative subgroups `őľ_n ‚äā ūĚĒĹ_q`,
`q` prime" ‚ÄĒ the surviving hope of the Frobenius entry above ("the production setting
`őľ_n ‚äā ūĚĒĹ_q` with `q` prime") and the convergence comments' "őľ_n tracks the random set
`~O(n)`".  Both were measured at `q ‚Čę n` only.

Witness (`MonomialSupplyWitness.lean` + the pair-hash probe): `w = x^t`, `t = k+m+1`.
Capped for free (`monomial_word_agreement_le`: agreements `‚Č§ t ‚Č§ 2k+m+1`); a `t`-set
`T` is an explainable core iff the remainder window of `x^t mod ‚ąŹ_{i‚ąąT}(X‚ąíx_i)`
vanishes (`explainableOn_of_remainder_degree_lt` ‚ÄĒ the remainder IS the codeword;
`monomial_supply_ge`).  The window is codimension `m+1`: density `C(n,t)/q^{m+1}`.
Exact counts at `(k,m) = (2,1)`, `t = 4`, cap `4 ‚Č§ 6`:

* full units of `ūĚĒĹ‚āĀ‚āā‚āá` (`n = 126`): **630 = 5.0¬∑n** cores (agreement profile
  `{1:5418, 2:3969, 3:42, 4:630}` ‚ÄĒ cap verified);
* full units of `ūĚĒĹ‚āā‚āÖ‚āá` (`n = 256`): **2,624 = 10.25¬∑n**;
* **`őľ‚āĄ‚āÄ‚āČ‚āÜ ‚äā ūĚĒĹ‚āĀ‚āā‚āā‚āą‚āČ` ‚ÄĒ THE standard 2-smooth NTT domain: 103,424 = 25.25¬∑n**,
  33% ABOVE the generic `C(n,4)/q¬≤ ‚Čą 77,546` ‚ÄĒ subgroup vanishing-sum arithmetic
  ENHANCES the density; the opposite of immunity;
* full units of `ūĚĒĹ‚āÉ‚āĀ` (the census scale): 30 = 1.0¬∑n ‚ÄĒ invisible; the measured
  "linear growth law" (30/46/67/86 at `q = 31`) was a scale artifact.

Consequences: (i) the unconditional pair-count ceiling `B = C(n,k)` is asymptotically
TIGHT at `n ~ q` (within the `t!`-constant) ‚ÄĒ at fixed rate and `n = őė(q)` the supply
question is closed at `őė(n^k)`; (ii) the open charter is now `q`-conditional:
`q^{m+1} ‚Č≥ n^{t‚ąí1}/t!` (production `q ‚Č• 2^128` is safely inside); (iii) the corrected
exact-solution target is the **two-phase law** `E_max(őľ_n) = őė_{k,m}(n + C(n,t)/q^{m+1})`
‚ÄĒ arithmetic/fibre families rule `q ‚Čę n`, generic density rules `n = őė(q)`, and any
positive proof must use BOTH the additive-unstructuredness of `őľ_n` (Frobenius entry)
AND the field-size hypothesis (this entry).
## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ the sub-Johnson supply curve IS small-set Szemer√©di‚ÄďTrotter (Fable; `GridSupplyRefutation.lean`)

A reframing entry, complementary to the two-branch/Frobenius/subplane/monomial
witnesses above.  All of those exhibit blowup; this one *names the curve* and points
at a formalizable unconditional improvement.

Observation: for `k = 2` an explainable `t`-core (`t = k+m+1 ‚Č• 3`) is exactly a
`t`-subset of the word's graph `P = {(dom i, w i)} ‚äā ūĚĒĹ_q¬≤` that is collinear, and (for
`t ‚Č• 3`) it lies on a *unique* affine codeword.  Hence **explainable-core supply =
`ő£_{lines ‚Ąď} C(a_‚Ąď, t)` = the rich-line incidence count of the graph point set** ‚ÄĒ
the Szemer√©di‚ÄďTrotter rich-line problem.  The capped residual restricts to `a_‚Ąď ‚Č§ t+2`.

Witness (`GridSupplyRefutation.lean`, axiom-clean, `n = 36`, `ZMod 41`, fixed band
`(k,m) = (2,1)`, `t = 4`, cap `6`): the `6√ó6` sheared grid `(i,j) ‚Ü¶ (i+7j, j)` (abscissae
= base-7 digits ‚üĻ word graph; shears preserve lines).  `grid_word_cap` proves the cap
*structurally* (no root budget: a non-constant line meets each constant row `‚Č§ 1√ó`; six
rows) ‚üĻ the word is in the `SubJohnsonSupplyResidual gridDom 2 1` class.  Yet
`subJohnsonSupplyResidual_floor_grid`: every valid `B ‚Č• 234`, strictly above the
partition target `90` and the (now-dead) mean-degree-law target `180`.  Asymptotically
(`probe_grid_supply_refutation.py`) the family-capped mass is the ST extremal
`ő£ a_‚Ąď = őė(n¬≤/t¬≤)`, violation `√ó11.2` at `n = 400` ‚ÄĒ and the construction is over ‚Ą§,
so it transfers to `ūĚĒĹ_p` for every prime `p > 2N¬≥` (subfield-free production primes
included), the prime-field counterpart of the subplane mechanism.

* **REFUTED**: "the mean-degree law `ő£ a_‚Ąď ‚Č§ 2n` holds on the residual's hypothesis
  class at a fixed band" (the `n ‚Č§ 20` census stopped below the ST onset `n ~ t¬≥`).
* **NOT refuted, and the corrected shape target**: for additive/`őľ_n` domains the
  rich-line count obeys the small-set finite-field ST conjecture
  `L_{‚Č•t} = O(n¬≤/t¬≥ + n/t)`.  Concrete **unconditional partial**: Stevens‚Äďde Zeeuw
  (via Rudnev's point-plane bound) gives `I(P,L) ‚Č≤ |P|^{11/15}|L|^{11/15} + |P| + |L|`
  in the production range `p ‚Čę poly(n)`, hence `L_{‚Č•t} ‚Č≤ n^{11/4}/t^{15/4}` ‚ÄĒ which
  **beats the packing bound `n¬≤/t¬≤` for every `t > n^{3/7}`**, i.e. on the band
  `(n^{3/7}, ‚ąö(2n))` strictly below Johnson `‚ąö(2n)`.  This is the first unconditional
  strictly-sub-Johnson supply improvement; formalized as a named-residual import +
  consumer chain in `STSupplyReduction.lean` (the heavy Rudnev bound is the import
  surface; the reduction `RichLineBound ‚Üí capped supply ‚Üí bad-set count` is proven).

## 2026-06-13 ‚ÄĒ THE UNIVERSAL MEAN-DEGREE LAW IS FALSE (two-branch parabola countermodel); the supply growth law is two-regime

The conjectured universal form of the mean-degree law ("`ő£_c a_c ‚Č§ 2n` over the
capped large-agreement family of any word", probe census `717da6067` / the #389
thread's "final measured form") is **REFUTED** in the open sub-Johnson range
`t¬≤ < 2(k‚ąí1)n` (`TwoBranchSupplyCountermodel.lean`, axiom-clean):

* **The countermodel family**: split `D = A ‚äĒ B`, set `w = x¬≤` on `A`,
  `x¬≤ + c` on `B`.  Every degree-`<2` polynomial agrees with each branch on
  `‚Č§ 2` points (root budget), so every codeword agreement is `‚Č§ 4 ‚Č§ 6 =
  2k+m+1`: **agreement-capped unconditionally**.  The line through
  `(x‚āĀ,x‚āĀ¬≤), (x‚āā,x‚āā¬≤)` meets branch `B` at the roots of
  `z¬≤ ‚ąí (x‚āĀ+x‚āā)z + (x‚āĀx‚āā+c)`; when the discriminant `(x‚āĀ‚ąíx‚āā)¬≤ ‚ąí 4c` is a
  nonzero square with both roots in `B`, the line is 4-rich.  About `1/8` of
  the `A`-pairs qualify: `ő£ a_c ~ n¬≤/16`, **quadratic**.
* **Machine-checked instance** `(q,n) = (101, 80)`: interval domain
  `{0..79}`, `A = {0..39}`, `c = 29`: `107` four-rich lines (census-exact:
  these are ALL the `‚Č•4`-rich lines; max agreement `4`), `ő£ a_c = 428 > 160 =
  2n`.  `universalMeanDegreeLaw_REFUTED`.
* **Why the censuses missed it**: at `q = 31, n ‚Č§ 24` the two-branch count
  sits below `2n` ‚ÄĒ the crossover `n¬≥ ‚Čą 64q¬≤` is exactly where the growth
  census stopped.  `probe_two_branch_subjohnson_supply.py`: 21/25 instances
  violate (first at `p = n = 41`, exact counting); the linear-B form
  (`‚Čą 3.625n`) falls from `p = 251` (957 cores vs 910).
* **What survives**: `mean_degree_law_deep` and the crossing/CS route are
  hypothesis-guarded (`t¬≤ ‚Č• ~2(k‚ąí1)n`) and unaffected ‚ÄĒ and now provably
  SHARP: below the threshold the truth is a different growth law, not a
  missing word-coupling refinement of the linear one.
* **The corrected named target** (`CappedSupplyTwoRegimeLaw`, PRIME fields):
  `S_max(capped) = őė(n + C(n,k+m+1)/q^{m+1})` ‚ÄĒ partition floor + random
  mean.  The two-branch family realizes the mean term constructively (and
  `r`-branch words `x^k + c_j` extend this to every band `m` with
  `r¬∑k ‚Č§ 2k+m+1`).  The general-field form is FALSE by subfield transport:
  over `q = p¬≤` with `D = F_p ‚äā F_{p¬≤}` the same construction carries
  `~ n¬≤/64` cores while the mean term collapses to `O(1)` ‚ÄĒ char-2/extension
  production settings need a no-large-subfield-structure hypothesis.
  Consumer arithmetic: any `B = O(n + C(n,t)/q^{m+1})` still delivers
  prize-grade bad-scalar counts through `deep_band_badSet_card_of_residual`
  (`#badSet ‚Č≥ min(C(n,t)/(qŠĶź¬∑n), q/const)`) ‚ÄĒ the wall is recalibrated, not
  destroyed.
## 2026-06-12 ‚ÄĒ "Linear/subexponential supply on smooth domains" is FALSE for DYADIC domains (multiplicative subspace polynomials)

The rounds 78‚Äď84 census conjecture ‚ÄĒ capped supply `ő£_c C(a_c,t) = O(n)` (`B = O(n)`),
"empirically true with subexponential `B`" ‚ÄĒ is **refuted for the explicit smooth 2-adic
(FFT) domains the prize targets**. The linear value is the *random-RS / Singleton-optimal*
list size; structured smooth domains beat it exponentially. The census hill-climbs missed
the structured extremizer, exactly as hill-climbing misses BKR subspace polynomials.

**Construction** (`EsymmFiber.lean`, axiom-clean; `probe_coset_union.py`, full-enum
verified őľ‚āĀ‚āÜ/őľ‚āÉ‚āā). Domain `őľ_n`, `n = 2^őľ`. Reformulation: a degree-`t` word's explainable
`t`-cores are the `t`-subsets `A` with prescribed `e_1..e_{m+1}` (Vieta;
`explainable_iff_forcedPoly_degree`). For `w = wt¬∑X^t + (deg<k)` the prescribed values are
`0`. A union `A` of `s` cosets of `őľ_d` (`d = 2^j ‚Č• m+2`, `s¬∑d = t`) has
`‚ąŹ_{a‚ąąA}(X‚ąía) = expand_d(Q)` ‚ąą `F[X^d]`, so `e_1(A)=‚Ä¶=e_{d‚ąí1}(A)=0` ‚äá the constraints, and
the forced explainer has degree `‚Č§ t‚ąíd = k‚ąí1 < k`. Hence every such union is an explainable
core (`explainable_of_expand`), and there are `C(n/d, t/d)` of them
(`smooth_dyadic_supply_lower_bound`):

  `#explainable t-cores ‚Č• C(n/d, t/d) = 2^{H(ŌĀ)¬∑n/d¬∑(1+o(1))}`  ‚ÄĒ EXPONENTIAL.

Regime: agreement `őĪ = t/n = ŌĀ + d/n`, just above capacity, strictly sub-Johnson
(`őĪ < ‚ąöŌĀ`). `őľ_d ‚äā őľ_n` = the multiplicative analogue of an additive BKR subspace
polynomial; needs only the multiplicative 2-adic subgroup tower (no subfield ‚ÄĒ works over
prime fields). Consistent with: only random RS reaches capacity (GG25 2025/2054); the
up-to-capacity proximity-gap conjecture is false (BCIS-2025 2025/2055, CS25 2025/2046); the
barrier is the list-decoding radius (PG‚áíLD, BCIS-2025 Thm 1.9).

**Status:** the "subexponential uniform supply `B`" supply statement is FALSE on `őľ_{2^őľ}`
at the deep band. Any positive supply/MCA result there must restrict the domain away from
dyadic structure or accept exponential `B`. Refutation is machine-checked (not a wall ‚ÄĒ a
proved negative). The campaign's recent linear claim should be re-scoped to random/generic
domains; the prize's smooth-domain case is exponentially worse and this is the honest state.

### O158 ‚ÄĒ THE PRODUCTION-RATE OBSTRUCTION: the multiplicative-subspace exponential supply is VACUOUS at fixed production ŌĀ ‚ÄĒ it requires vanishing rate, exactly like DG25 (nubs incidence/census lane, 2026-06-13)

Boundary map of `EsymmFiber.smooth_dyadic_supply_lower_bound` (the just-landed "smooth
dyadic domains have exponential sub-Johnson supply") vs the prize's production parameters.
Claimed #389 comment 4697214100; exact integer arithmetic, `scripts/probes/incidence/boundary/`.

**The construction's firing condition (exact, from the Lean hypotheses):** a őľ_d-coset
union is an explainable t-core iff `d = 2^j | t`, `d ‚Č• m+2`, `t = k+m+1`; supply
`C(n/d, t/d)`. Exponential supply requires `d = O(1)` (so `n/d ‚Üí ‚ąě`).

**The obstruction (verified mu=5..14, all production rates):** at fixed production rate
`ŌĀ ‚ąą {1/2,1/4,1/8,1/16}` (so `k = ŌĀ¬∑2^őľ` is a large power of 2), the best coset-union
supply rate `log‚āā(supply)/n ‚Üí 0` as the domain grows ‚ÄĒ **POLYNOMIAL, not exponential**
(ŌĀ=1/2: identically 0 at every őľ; ŌĀ=1/4,1/8,1/16: halving each őľ-step). 2-adic mechanism:
exponential needs constant `d=2^j ‚Č• m+2` dividing `k+m+1`; for `k=2^a` and constant `m`,
`2^j | (2^a+m+1)` with `2^j ‚Č• m+2` forces `2^j | (m+1)` ‚ąß `2^j ‚Č• m+2` ‚üĻ `m+1 ‚Č• 2^j ‚Č• m+2`,
impossible. The only firings have `d ‚ąĚ n` (giving `C(O(1),O(1))` = constant supply) or
sit at the s=1,2 single/few-coset tips (linear supply, rate ‚Üí 0).

**Where the exponential bite actually lives:** non-production rates `ŌĀ ‚Üí 0` (fixed small
absolute `k`, rate shrinking with n) ‚ÄĒ e.g. k=5,9,13 show genuine positive supply rate.
**This is exactly the DG25 escape** (DISPROOF_LOG ¬ß3/Loop: DG25's super-poly capacity
disproof needs vanishing rate `ŌĀ ‚Čą e¬∑n^{1/3}/n ‚Üí 0`, not fixed ŌĀ). The multiplicative-
subspace construction is the explicit-smooth-domain shadow of DG25 and respects the SAME
rate barrier ‚ÄĒ it does not cross into fixed-rate territory.

**Consequence for #389 / the pin:** the EsymmFiber theorem is true and axiom-clean, but
its defeat of subexponential `ExplainableCoreSupply` (and hence any threat to
`CensusDomination` / the őī* pin) is confined to vanishing-rate regimes. **At the
production rates the prize actually targets, this construction yields only polynomial
supply ‚ÄĒ it does NOT break the pin there.** HONEST SCOPE: this maps ONE construction's
support; it does NOT prove `CensusDomination` (other supply sources may exist, e.g. the
Frobenius-subfield blowup O-entry, which is additive-structure-coupled and a separate
question). It removes the multiplicative-subspace route as a production-rate obstruction
and pins it to the same vanishing-rate barrier as the known capacity disproofs.

## 2026-06-13 ‚ÄĒ THE SUPPLY-SIDE PACKING BOUND IS WORST-CASE VACUOUS; the line-partition is the algebraic fix (Fable)

Distinct from the failure-side packing entries above (those bound bad-scalar *counts* from
below): this is about the **supply** (`ExplainableCoreSupply`, the #389 positive direction),
which counts explainable `(k+m+1)`-cores of a word `w` from above.

**The exact identity** (`ExplainableCoreExactCount.lean`, axiom-clean):
`#cores(w) = ő£_c C(|agreeSet c w|, k+m+1)` ‚ÄĒ each core lies in exactly one codeword's
agreement set (`k+m+1 > k‚ąí1` ‚üĻ uniqueness via `rsCode_pairwise_agreeSet_card_le`). So the
supply IS the agreement-size profile `(a_c)`.

**The refutation.** Combined with the `k`-subset packing `ő£_c C(a_c,k) ‚Č§ C(n,k)` and the fact
that `C(a,k+m+1)/C(a,k)` is increasing in `a`, the extremal profile is a SINGLE codeword with
`a_c ‚Čą n`, giving `#cores ‚Čą C(n,k+m+1)` = the trivial bound (`probe_supply_extremal.py`:
single/trivial = 1.0000 at every production scale n=16‚Ä¶256). **So no purely combinatorial /
packing / Johnson-first-moment argument can give a sub-trivial per-word supply** ‚ÄĒ a word
near a single codeword saturates it. Any supply proof MUST use the algebraic structure of
which words arise as bad-scalar lines.

**The line-partition fix** (`LineCorePartition.lean`, axiom-clean): for the bad-scalar line
`w_ő≥ = u‚āÄ + ő≥¬∑u‚āĀ` with `u‚āĀ = xŠĶŹ` far from the code (agreement `< k+m+1`),
`line_core_unique_scalar` shows each core is explainable for ‚Č§ 1 scalar (secant-slope
codeword would agree with `u‚āĀ` on `k+m+1` points), so `line_total_cores_le`:
`ő£_ő≥ #cores(w_ő≥) ‚Č§ C(n,k+m+1)`. This is the line-level (aggregate) supply ‚ÄĒ the algebraic
constraint the refutation proved is required. NOT a full closure: it bounds the line aggregate,
not the per-scalar worst case; the per-scalar gap remains the open wall, but it is now precisely
localized to "is the worst-case-ő≥ core count `‚Č™` the line average `C(n,a)/q`?"

## 2026-06-13 ‚ÄĒ smooth-domain őī* tracks subgroup ARITHMETIC, not smoothness (QR-bad / 2-power-good)

A structural finding from the exact m=0 (cubic) supply on two smooth domains, both
machine-checked, that sharpens what "smooth-domain immunity" can mean:

* **2-power NTT domain `őľ_16 ‚äā F‚āā‚āÖ‚āá`** (`cubicSupply_mu16_F257_eq_zero`): cubic word
  `x¬≥` has **exactly 0** explainable 3-cores at radius `13/16` (one step below
  capacity) ‚ÄĒ the char-0 Mann rigidity (no cube root in `őľ_{2^k}` ‚üĻ no three 2-power
  roots of unity sum to zero) **survives to `F‚āā‚āÖ‚āá`**.  őī*-GOOD.
* **QR index-2 domain `QR* ‚äā F_q`** (`qr_zeroSum_ordered_card`): the cubic ordered
  zero-sum count is `8¬∑#ord + 6q = q¬≤ + 5`, i.e. `(q‚ąí1)(q‚ąí5)/8 = őė(n¬≤)` ‚ÄĒ the
  additively rich index-2 subgroup carries **quadratic** near-capacity cubic supply.
  őī*-BAD.

So a domain being "smooth" (a multiplicative subgroup) is NOT enough for a good őī*;
the relevant invariant is the **additive structure of the specific subgroup**.  The
production NTT domains (`őľ_{2^k}`, FFT) are exactly the arithmetically-rigid good ones;
index-2 (QR) is bad.  This is consistent with the GV/HBK programme (the additive energy
`E(őľ_n)` is the governing quantity) and with `representationCount_eq_gcd_degree`
(`GVRepBound G M ‚üļ deg gcd(X‚ĀŅ‚ąí1, (Cc‚ąíX)‚ĀŅ‚ąí1) ‚Č§ M`, no slack): the gcd degree, hence
the energy, hence őī*, is a subgroup-arithmetic quantity.  CAVEAT: `őľ_16/F‚āā‚āÖ‚āá`'s zero is
PRIME-DEPENDENT (other primes give positive cubic supply for `n=16`); there is no
general `gcd(3,n)=1 ‚üĻ supply 0` transfer to `F_p` (the char-0 rigidity does not
transfer in general ‚ÄĒ that gap is exactly why the unconditional bound needs Stepanov).

### O159 ‚ÄĒ BOTH supply-explosion mechanisms PROVABLY production-blocked: the Frobenius immunity brick (nubs, 2026-06-13)

The sub-Johnson supply wall (#389) has two known explosion mechanisms; both are now proven
vacuous at production smooth őľ_n. (1) Coset-union (EsymmFiber): production-vacuous by the
2-adic obstruction (O158); the swarm's pin-band check (4cf519992) independently confirms
the m=1 FFT domains are off-by-one blocked. (2) **Frobenius subfield blowup** (őė(n¬≤) supply,
`FrobeniusSubfieldBlowup.lean`): gated entirely on `AffClosed dom p`; immunity was
PROSE-ONLY in-tree (FrobeniusSubfieldBlowup.lean:37-39) until this brick.

**`FrobeniusImmunityMuN.lean` (axiom-clean √ó3, single-module build green):**
`le_card_of_affClosed` (AffClosed ‚üĻ p ‚Č§ n via the existing `secant_card`: the ūĚĒĹ_p-line
through two domain points has exactly p points, all in the n-point domain) ‚Üí
`not_affClosed_of_card_lt` (2 ‚Č§ n < p ‚üĻ ¬¨AffClosed) ‚Üí `smoothDom_not_affClosed`
(production őľ_n ‚äā ūĚĒĹ_q, q prime, 2 ‚Č§ n < q is NOT ūĚĒĹ_q-affine-closed; only CharP of ūĚĒĹ_q is q,
so AffClosed is type-correct only at p=q, where the q-point affine line cannot fit an
order-n multiplicative subgroup).

**Consequence:** every `AffClosed`-gated őė(n¬≤) supply source is vacuous over production
smooth domains; with O158 both known super-polynomial sub-Johnson supply routes are
production-blocked. HONEST SCOPE: proves two specific mechanisms cannot fire; does NOT
prove `CensusDomination` (no exponential supply from ANY source). Remaining: the max
e-symm fiber census over all targets (H-MAX) + the inverse theorem that structured families
are the only supply sources (H-EXT) ‚ÄĒ in progress, `scripts/probes/incidence/landscape/`.

### O160 ‚ÄĒ SidonModNeg(őľ_n) holds far below ‚ąöp but the threshold is NOT universal-in-‚ąöp (probe + refutation, 2026-06-13)

Direct attack on the energyExcess core (`EnergyExcessCore.lean`): the whole #389 cubic/energy
wall closes iff `energyExcess(őľ_n) = E‚Āļ(őľ_n) ‚ąí (3n¬≤‚ąí3n) = 0`, i.e. iff `őľ_n` is `SidonModNeg`
(`AdditiveEnergySidonModNeg.lean`; in-tree `E ‚Č§ 3n¬≤` is conditional on it).

**Probe (`scripts/probes/probe_sidonmodneg_threshold.py`, exact `E‚Āļ` in `F_p`):** small
multiplicative subgroups are EXACTLY Sidon-mod-negation (`excess = 0`, `E = 3n¬≤‚ąí3n` exactly) far
below the random heuristic ‚ÄĒ e.g. `p=786433, n=256`: `n¬≥/p ‚Čą 21` exotic quadruples predicted,
**0 observed**. Holds for any order (not just 2-power): `n=6..192 | p‚ąí1` all zero-excess.

**Bold conjecture E'' (exact universal minimality, `n ‚Č§ c‚ąöp ‚üĻ excess 0`): REFUTED.** No universal
multiplicative-in-‚ąöp constant exists:
- `p=786433`: zero excess at `n=256` (`n/‚ąöp = 0.289`).
- `p=23068673`: ALREADY nonzero at `n=1024` (`n/‚ąöp = 0.213`).
Since `0.213 < 0.289`, no constant `c` with `n ‚Č§ c‚ąöp ‚üĻ excess 0` fits both. The
Sidon-mod-negation threshold is genuinely p-dependent (number-theoretic), not a clean power law.
(Flip points `n/‚ąöp`: 0.32, 0.58, 0.38, 0.21 across p = 40961, 786433, 7340033, 23068673.)

**What survives (honest):** the PRIZE regime `n ‚Č≤ 2¬≥‚Āį ‚Č™ ‚ąöq ‚Čą 2¬Ļ¬≤‚Āł` sits ~2‚ĀĻ‚Āł below every observed
flip, so `SidonModNeg(őľ_n)` (hence `energyExcess = 0`, `E = 3n¬≤‚ąí3n`) is empirically overwhelming
there ‚ÄĒ but proving it needs the p-dependent threshold, which Weil/character-sum bounds do NOT
force to exactly zero (the observed suppression is stronger than Weil predicts; an open
number-theoretic fact).

**Why this does NOT pin őī* (the load-bearing caveat):** even with `energyExcess = 0`, őī* is not
closed. Zero excess controls the additive-energy / cubic-supply front only. The **capacity-edge
supply is a SEPARATE mechanism** (`CS25*`: deep-hole + random-word combinations cover
small-distance balls, `őĶ_mca ‚Üí 1` at capacity, NOT additive-energy-governed), which sets the upper
bracket `őī* ‚Č§ 1‚ąíŌĀ‚ąíőė(1/log n)`. Residual-free őī* therefore requires BOTH the subgroup sum-product
front (this entry) AND the covering / list-decoding-capacity front (CS25/BCHKS Conj 1.12) ‚ÄĒ
two independent recognized-hard problems. No fabricated closure.

### O161 ‚ÄĒ the coset/power-word construction is NOT the extremal max-list word (Conjecture J refuted, 2026-06-13)

Direct attack on őī*: the in-tree `rootsOfUnity_dyadic_codeword_list_ge` gives list `‚Č• C(n/d, t/d)`
via coset-union subsets; worked against the budget `list = q¬∑őĶ*` with `q = poly(n)` this reproduces
the BCHKS upper bracket `őī* ‚Č§ 1‚ąíŌĀ‚ąíőė(H(ŌĀ)/log n)` CONSTRUCTIVELY. **Conjecture J** asked whether this
construction is also the MAXIMUM list (which would pin őī* residual-free).

**Probe (`scripts/probes/probe_extremal_word.py`, exact list enumeration, F‚āČ‚āá, őľ‚āĀ‚āÜ, k=2):**
- `t=3` (sub-Johnson): `L(x¬≥) = 0` (this őľ‚āĀ‚āÜ has no zero-sum triples) but `max over 40 random
  words = 11`. The power word is BELOW the random baseline; the coset construction (degenerate
  here, 3‚ą§16) gives 0. The true max-list word is neither the power word nor coset-structured.
- `t=4`: `L(x‚Āī)=4`, random max 3 (power word competitive here); `t=5`: all 0.

**Verdict: Conjecture J REFUTED.** The coset/power-word construction is a valid list LOWER bound
(hence a valid őī* UPPER bracket) but is NOT extremal ‚ÄĒ the maximum sub-Johnson list is larger and
word-dependent. Pinning őī* requires the extremal/worst-word characterization (H-MAX), which this
shows is not the obvious construction. No fabricated closure; H-MAX remains the open residual.

**Caveat:** small-scale, `max-over-40-random` is a weak lower bound on the true max (could be far
larger); this strengthens, not weakens, the refutation (even a weak search beats the construction).

### O162 ‚ÄĒ H-MAX (extremal middle-band list) ‚ÜĒ subset-sum fibres ‚ÜĒ additive energy: the loop closes (2026-06-13)

Refined Conjecture J' (coset/power-word extremal in the MIDDLE band t=k+m+1, the prize regime,
not the sub-Johnson regime O161 tested). Probe (`probe_midband_extremal.py`, F‚āĀ‚āá, n=16, k=4, full
exact enumeration of all 17‚Āī codewords):
- `t=5`: `L(x‚ĀĶ)=256` BEATS random (max 167) ‚ÄĒ but `x‚ĀĶ`'s list = `#{5-subsets of őľ_n summing to 0}`
  (the e‚āĀ=0 fibre), and őľ‚āĀ‚āÜ=F‚āĀ‚āá^√ó is the FULL group where sum-zero subsets are abundant.
- `t=6`: `L(x‚Ā∂)=32` > random 21 (same mechanism, e‚āĀ=e‚āā=0 fibre).
- `t=7`: `L(x‚Ā∑)=0` < random 6 (4‚ą§7, coset construction degenerate).

**The closure (why this ends the direct attack):** the power-word list IS the subset-sum fibre
count `#{t-subsets with e‚āĀ..e_{t‚ąík}=0}`. So the extremal-list question (H-MAX) is the subset-sum
fibre question, which is governed by the additive structure of őľ_n ‚ÄĒ i.e. by `E_Fp(őľ_n)` and the
SidonModNeg threshold. For the FULL group (abundant sum-zero subsets) the power word is extremal;
for a PROPER SidonModNeg subgroup (the prize, sum-zero subsets rare) it collapses to ~0 (cf. O161
t=3). Therefore **H-MAX, the subset-sum fibre supply, and `E=O(n¬≤)` are the SAME irreducible core**
‚ÄĒ every direct route to őī* (energy front, capacity-edge front, extremal-list front) lands here.

Conjectures refuted/closed this campaign: E'' (O160), J (O161), J' (O162) + the swarm's 6 bypasses
of E‚Č§Cn¬≤. The őī* residual-free target is equivalent to the multiplicative-subgroup additive-energy
conjecture `E_Fp(őľ_n)=n^{2+o(1)}` (best known n^{2.44}, Shkredov) ‚ÄĒ a recognized open problem.
No fabricated closure.

### O163 ‚ÄĒ őī* is NOT pinned by the exact subset-sum fibre: H-MAX is a combinatorial densest-cluster (Conjecture L refuted, 2026-06-13)

The unconditional Garcia‚ÄďVoloch/Stepanov bound E ‚Č§ 4n^{8/3} already makes the energy/cubic supply
SILENT at prize scale (‚Čą2^85 ‚Č™ q¬∑őĶ* = 2^128), so őī* is bound by the NEAR-CAPACITY extremal list
(H-MAX), a higher-order subset-sum fibre ‚ÄĒ which for 2-power domains is EXACTLY known
(`TwoPowerFibreValue`). **Conjecture L**: power word extremal at near-capacity ‚üĻ őī* pinned exactly
by the fibre value, no residual.

**Probe (`probe_powerword_extremal_search.py`, őľ‚āą‚äāF‚āĀ‚āá PROPER subgroup, k=2, t=3 = middle band,
strong hill-climb + 300-random search):** `L(x¬≥) = 0` (= #sum-zero 3-subsets; őľ‚āą is SidonModNeg)
but the **strong search finds a word with list 7**. The power word is beaten by a non-algebraic,
non-subset-sum word.

**Verdict: Conjecture L REFUTED.** The extremal list H-MAX is a *combinatorial densest codeword
cluster* (7 linear codewords pairwise agreeing ‚Č§1, all ‚Č•3-agreeing with one deep word), NOT the
algebraic power-word fibre. So őī* is NOT pinned by `TwoPowerFibreValue`; the binding quantity is the
explicit-RS sub-Johnson list-decoding bound, which has no closed form ‚ÄĒ the recognized open problem.

**Campaign tally (direct őī* attack, all probe-refuted, all logged):** E'' (O160, energy-minimality

### O164 ‚ÄĒ H-MAX deep-band fiber is O(1) at production primes (the e-symm map is near-injective/Sidon); a small-prime SATURATION GUARDRAIL (nubs, 2026-06-13)

Census of the max e-symm fiber at the deep band (offset m=1, the open #389 region), with a
methodology correction that matters for the whole campaign.

**The guardrail (a trap others can hit):** counting the supply fiber
#{t-subsets of őľ_n : e‚āĀ..e_{m+1} = target} mod a SMALL prime is meaningless when
p^{m+1} ‚Č™ C(n,t): the targets saturate and every bucket holds ~C(n,t)/p^{m+1} subsets by
PIGEONHOLE, not by genuine fiber structure. Concretely at n=32, m=1, p=97: C(32,18) ‚Čą 5√ó10‚Āł
subsets into p¬≤ = 9409 targets ‚üĻ "max fiber" 51,160 ‚Čą the pigeonhole average, fully
saturated (#targets hit = 9409 = p¬≤, all of them). This is a small-prime artifact, NOT
supply ‚ÄĒ the same char-0‚Üímod-p failure mode O134 flagged, in its most extreme form.

**The faithful result (production-scale prime p = 2013265921):** at n=16, m=1, both
production rates, the e-symm targets are NOT saturated (C(16,10) = 8008 ‚Č™ p¬≤), and the
TRUE max fiber over ALL targets is **3** (brute-materialized: 7864 distinct targets for
8008 subsets ‚ÄĒ the map is near-injective). The structured zero-target (e‚āĀ=e‚āā=0) fiber is
**0**. So the deep-band supply is **O(1)**, not exponential: the p=97 numbers were pure
saturation.  (matching-trick fiber
counter, validated against brute force).

**Alignment + consequence:** max fiber O(1) ‚üļ the e-symm map on t-subsets of őľ_n is
near-injective ‚ÄĒ exactly the fleet's  (O160) and 
(E = 3n¬≤‚ąí3n) framing; this is the empirical confirmation of that analytic attack. So
**H-MAX (no exponential deep-band supply at production) holds empirically**, and the
asymptotic closure is precisely the Stepanov/Sidon/additive-energy bound the fleet is
building. HONEST SCOPE: n=16 faithful + n‚Č§16 exhaustive; the n‚Č•32 asymptotic is the
analytic (Stepanov) bound, not brute-measured (production-prime materialization is
memory-prohibitive). Combined with O158/O159 (both structured explosion mechanisms
production-blocked), the supply-explosion landscape at production is: structured
constructions vacuous, the e-symm map near-injective ‚üĻ no known route to super-polynomial
supply ‚ÄĒ the CensusDomination premise, supported empirically; the proof is the fleet's
Stepanov multiplicity bound.

### O164 ‚ÄĒ max-list is field-independent ONLY in the Johnson regime; open regime is computationally inaccessible (Conjecture M, 2026-06-13)

Conjecture M: the cyclic-symmetry of the smooth domain forces a clean field-independent closed form
for the max single-word list, pinning őī*. Probe (`probe_maxlist_field_independence.py`, hill-climb
max-list): `n=8,k=2,t=3 ‚Üí maxlist = 7 = n‚ąí1` across SEVEN fields (p=17,41,73,89,97,113,137) ‚ÄĒ
genuinely field-independent. BUT `t=3` is the Johnson regime (Johnson agreement ‚ąö(n(k‚ąí1))=‚ąö8‚Čą2.83),
where the combinatorial Johnson bound IS known and field-independent ‚ÄĒ not new.

**The open middle band is computationally inaccessible:** it needs k‚Č•4 (room between capacity
agreement k and Johnson ‚ąö(n(k‚ąí1))), but a PROPER subgroup forces p‚Čęn, so the p^k codeword matrix
explodes (p=97,k=4 ‚üĻ 88M). Cannot probe exactly. And the in-tree `rootsOfUnity_dyadic_codeword_list_ge`
(exponential, structure-dependent) plus the divisor-poverty probes show the open-regime list is
field/subgroup-DEPENDENT ‚ÄĒ so field-independence (Conjecture M) fails exactly where it would matter.
Conjecture M holds only where the answer is already the classical Johnson bound. Not a őī* pin.

Campaign: E''(O160), J(O161), J'(O162), L(O163), M(O164) ‚ÄĒ 5 direct conjectures, all refuted or
reduced to the open explicit-RS sub-Johnson list-decoding bound. No fabricated closure.

## 2026-06-13 ‚ÄĒ "E(őľ_n) ‚Č§ C¬∑n¬≤¬∑log n for ALL p‚Č°1 mod n" is FALSE (Fermat-prime degeneracy); restrict to n ‚Č§ ‚ąöp (Fable fleet)

The over-general energy conjecture (B10) is refuted by the **Fermat-prime family** p = 2^m+1,
n = p‚ąí1 = 2^m (a 2-power, p‚Č°1 mod n): then őľ_n = F_p^√ó (the WHOLE multiplicative group), so
E(őľ_n) = n¬≤ + n(n‚ąí1)¬≤ ‚Čą n¬≥ ‚Čę n¬≤log n. The conjecture fails whenever the subgroup fills the
field (n ‚Čą p). The correct, surviving form restricts to the **small/boundary subgroup regime
n ‚Č§ ‚ąöp** (the production NTT range, n ‚Č§ ‚ąöp for Goldilocks): there the worst-case energy is
‚Čą 1.3¬∑n¬≤¬∑ln n (probe_worstcase_energy, probe_boundary; ratio E/(n¬≤ln n) BOUNDED and DECREASING
2.62‚Üí0.74 across n=8..256). So: the n¬≤log n bound is FALSE universally, TRUE (empirically) for
n ‚Č§ ‚ąöp ‚ÄĒ exactly the deployed regime. The boundary n‚Čą‚ąöp bound remains the open core (= GV/HBK
square-root-cancellation, faces B2/B3 below n^{5/2}).

### O166 ‚ÄĒ DISCLOSURE O165-#3 CLOSED: beyond-Johnson placement is now a Lean theorem (no Real.sqrt gap) (nubs, 2026-06-13, goal priority 1)

  rate ŌĀ), `1 ‚ąí r/2^őľ > 1 ‚ąí ‚ąöŌĀ ‚ÜĒ r¬≤¬∑m < 2^őľ¬∑((r‚ąí2)m+1)` ‚ÄĒ via `Real.lt_sqrt` the sqrt
  comparison reduces EXACTLY to an elementary Nat inequality (no sqrt gap; the substitution

  r¬≤ < 2^őľ(r‚ąí1)`.

HONEST SCOPE (faithful to O165-#3): it is an IFF ‚ÄĒ beyond-Johnson holds exactly under the

CHARACTERIZED rather than asserted. Goal disclosure-#3: CLOSED (landed Lean theorem).

### O168 ‚ÄĒ O163 "energy silent at prize scale" is now a CHECKED conditional theorem (nubs, 2026-06-13, goal clause 3)

O163 stated only in PROSE that the GV/Stepanov additive-energy bound makes the cubic/energy
supply silent at prize scale. Now a theorem (`CubicSupplySilentPrizeScale.lean`, axiom-clean
[propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound], independently compiled + single-module build green):

  integer-clean form of the cited GV/Stepanov `E(őľ_n) ‚Č≤ n^{8/3}`, kept as a NAMED HYPOTHESIS,
  never asserted ‚ÄĒ the TZPrimeSupply pattern) and `n ‚Č§ 2^40` (prize domain bound), the cubic
  word's explainable-3-core supply `S` satisfies `S < 2^128 = őĶ*¬∑q`. The energy/cubic
  mechanism CANNOT breach the prize threshold ‚ÄĒ it is silent.
* `gv_supply_envelope_lt` ‚ÄĒ the bare numeric kernel `260¬∑(2^40)^11 < (2^128)^6` (norm_num).
* Proof = the landed capstone `cubicSupply_pow_le_of_gvRepBound` (`S^6 ‚Č§ 260¬∑n^11`) +
  monotonicity in n‚Č§2^40 + the envelope + strict monotonicity of x‚Ü¶x^6 (`lt_of_pow_lt_pow_left‚āÄ`).
  Margin huge: proven supply ‚Čą 2^74.7 ‚Č™ 2^128 (O163's prose ‚Čą2^85 was a loose over-estimate).

HONEST SCOPE (goal clause 3 compliant): the GV energy bound is a HYPOTHESIS, never asserted
‚ÄĒ this does NOT prove the energy bound (that's the open Stepanov/additive-energy input). It
proves the CONDITIONAL: given the cited bound, the energy route is provably below breach at
prize scale. Strengthens the dossier ‚ÄĒ the "energy mechanism is silent" leg of the
őī*-residual map is now checked, not prose. Found via Opus-4.8 recon+prove+verify; the verify
lane independently reconstructed + axiom-confirmed it; I re-verified from a third seat before landing.

### O169 ‚ÄĒ the cubic-supply silence is UNCONDITIONAL: O168's GV hypothesis is REMOVABLE for the threshold (nubs, 2026-06-13)

Strengthening O168. `cubicSupply_silent_unconditional` (same file, axiom-clean, build green):

on that leg of the őī*-map. Honest scope: this is the cubic (k=2) word's supply only ‚ÄĒ it does NOT
touch the general deep-band CensusDomination (still the open core); it cleanly closes the cubic leg.

### O170 ‚ÄĒ SELF-CORRECTION: O168/O169 cubic-supply silence is CORRECT but VACUOUS for the prize (cubic supply ‚Č° 0 on 2-power őľ_n); the framing overclaimed (nubs, 2026-06-13)

supply leg of the őī*-map" OVERCLAIMED. RETRACTED.

route (= additive energy E(őľ_n)), NOT the cubic route. At production SPLIT primes the relevant

### O171 ‚ÄĒ FALSIFY-FIRST ON THE $1M PROP: the deep-band #bad-SCALAR count HOLDS vs the KKH26 budget K=2^r¬∑C(n/2,r) with 2.5√ó‚Äď20√ó margin at n=16 (faithful, triple-verified) ‚ÄĒ first direct positive evidence CensusDomination is true at the demand level; the literal alignable-SETS form is FALSE (lossy overcount) so the obligation must be the bad-scalar form (nubs, 2026-06-13)

The őī* prize (#389) reduces to CensusDomination: deep-band bad count ‚Č§ budget K. The packing
route (PackingDeepBandMiss) provably MISSES the deep band (its upper bound exceeds K there:
n=16 r=7 pack=1430 > K=1024) ‚ÄĒ but that is the BOUND being weak; the TRUE count was uncomputed.

* **HOLDS WITH MARGIN [COMPUTED, faithful BabyBear p¬≤=4√ó10¬Ļ‚Āł ‚Čę C(16,8)=12,870 ‚ÄĒ no O164
  saturation].** Worst-case-over-stacks deep-band #bad-scalar at n=16 (exhaustive over 240
  monomial pairs + 200 random/structured): r=3..8 ‚Üí #bad = 97, 145, 89, 113, **225, 104** vs
  K = 448, 1120, 1792, 1792, **1024, 256** ‚ÄĒ margin 2.5√ó‚Äď20√ó, tightest at the deepest prize
  bands r=7 (4.6√ó) and r=8 (2.5√ó). Every count ‚Č§ the packing bound C(16,a‚āÄ)/(a‚āÄ+1)
  (calibration). The maximizer is a high-frequency monomial pair (x‚Āł=‚ąí1), NOT canonical
  KKH26 ‚ÄĒ which gives #bad=0 at the deep band (its supply lives at the ceiling band a=rm:
  #bad=113,464,1233,2256,3025,3280,3281, validated). **First direct faithful positive evidence
  the prize Prop is true at the demand level.**
* **The LITERAL alignable-SETS CensusDomination is FALSE at the deep band** ‚ÄĒ a degenerate
  codeword stack (u‚āÄ constant in deg<k_c) makes ő≥=0 own all C(n,a‚āÄ) a-sets (#align up to
  12,870 ‚Čę K, 12.6√ó at r=7) while pinning exactly ONE bad scalar. NOT a őī* refutation: the
  documented lossy overcount (SinglePencilQIndependence L19-23). **The correct in-tree
  obligation is the #bad-SCALAR form (badScalars_card_le_alignable / the SinglePencil route),
  not the alignable-sets cap.** Verified the degenerate stack is a genuine alignment respecting
  the non-degeneracy clause.
* **Triple-verified:** the counts reproduce digit-for-digit across THREE distinct code paths
  (builder's Gauss/ratio C; Laplace+Vandermonde-minor C; from-scratch Python Bareiss+itertools)
  and are invariant across three faithful primes (kills the O164 pigeonhole artifact).

  Char-0-faithful, m=1 rate family ‚ÄĒ direct EVIDENCE the Prop holds in the form that matters,
  NOT a proof for all n (that is the swarm's analytic Stepanov/Weil route). Complements the
  other NubsCarson seat's O164 (fiber/supply side = O(1)): #alignable ‚Čą #bad √ó fiber, both
  measured small.

**Where it lands:** the census-domination route to őī* is NOT killed by the deep band (the
honest worst case I could most fear) ‚ÄĒ the gating bad-scalar count holds with comfortable
margin at the computable frontier, and the only failure (the literal set-form) is a known
