## ORBIT-COUNT lever is RULE-3-INCOMPATIBLE (thickness-invariant) ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-15

**Object:** the orbit count `N_pencil(őī) = #orbits` of the bad-őĪ set under `őĪ ‚Ü¶ őĪ¬∑ŌČ^{b‚ąía}`, the governing
count in the in-tree crossing law `OrbitCountCrossingLaw.lean`: `I_pencil(őī) ‚Č§ n ‚üļ N_pencil(őī) ‚Č§ gcd(b‚ąía,n)`.
The file's "Honest scope" leaves OPEN whether `N_pencil` stays bounded (O(1)) at constant rate in the
small-gap window. This is the rule-3 (thinness-essentiality) audit of that object.

**Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_407_orbit_count_thinness.py`. Exact, subset-driven (a bad-őĪ at threshold
t is an over-determined t-subset whose order-k divided-difference windows all vanish; each DD is AFFINE in
őĪ, so per t-subset ‚Č§1 candidate őĪ = common root over the t‚ąík windows; union over far t-subsets = exact bad
set, NO O(q) scan). PROPER subgroups őľ_n < F_q*, multiple primes per (n,k) spanning thick ő≤‚Čą2.06 ‚Üí thin ő≤‚Čą4.5.

**Result (n=8, k=2, ŌĀ=1/4 = prize rate), 5 primes p‚ąą{73,193,521,4073,11593}, ő≤=2.06,2.53,3.01,4.00,4.50:**
  max-over-far-pencils orbit count at the binding band:
    thr=3 (őī=0.625): maxN = 6 ‚ÄĒ IDENTICAL across all 5 ő≤ (same extremal pencil (2,6)).
    thr=4 (őī=0.500): maxN = 2 ‚ÄĒ IDENTICAL.
    thr=5 (őī=0.375): maxN = 1 ‚ÄĒ IDENTICAL.
  EXACTLY thickness-invariant: same value, same extremal direction, ő≤=2.06 ‚Üí 4.50.

**Confirmation (n=16, k=4, ŌĀ=1/4, thr=7 = the őī*=9/16 binding band):**
  THICK p=257 (ő≤=2.00): maxN=2, extremal pencil (4,12).
  THIN  p=65537 (ő≤=4.00, Fermat): pencil (4,12) maxN=2, I=4 ‚ÄĒ IDENTICAL on the worst direction.

**Verdict:** `max_pencils N_pencil` at the binding band is thickness-invariant ‚üĻ a thickness-monotone
orbit-count bound CANNOT prove CORE (false in the thick window, rule 3). The orbit-count REFORMULATION
inherits the SAME rule-3 obstruction as the moment/energy family: the crossing law is an exact, axiom-clean,
BGK-FREE structural restatement, but the governing count is thickness-invariant ‚ÄĒ the orbit basis buys
structure, not a thinness discriminator. Extends the route-elimination map to the orbit-count brick (#5).

**Nuance (honest):** the free-action factorization `I = N¬∑S` holds on the extremal pencil (verified n=8
pencil (2,6): I=12=6¬∑2, all orbits size 2, őĪ=0‚ąČbad, p-independent across p=73,4073) but `factOK` over the
FULL sweep goes False at low thr ‚ÄĒ some pencils have mixed orbit sizes (degenerate near-directions /
fixed-point orbit), so the clean `I=N¬∑S` is a per-pencil (worst-direction) statement, looser than a uniform
hypothesis. Consistent with the file's őĪ=0-fixed-point caveat; the in-tree theorems remain correct.

**Scope:** small n (8 full band, 16 worst-pencil); maps the rule-3 CHARACTER of the orbit-count object,
does NOT prove the asymptotic. p-independence of the count is consistent with the campaign's PROVEN
p-independence. No Lean change (empirical thickness-invariance; proving const-slack would be BGK). No CORE
closure. Probe-first, exact, honest.

## BoundedHalfBasisIndep (the named char-p prize core): FALSE at the prize prime + thinness signal CLOSES at large n ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-15

**Object:** the freshest unification's named open core (`BoundedCyclotomicIndep.lean`, `BridgeBounded.lean`,
`AntipodalBalanceBounded.lean`, capstone `CountAntipodalBounded.lean`):
> `BoundedHalfBasisIndep ŌČ N C` := no nonzero `g ‚ąą [‚ąíC,C]^N` (N = 2^{m-1} = n/2) with `ő£_j g_j ŌČ^j = 0` mod p.
> Chain: `BoundedHalfBasisIndep ‚üĻ count-antipodal ‚üĻ char-p Wick ladder ‚ąÄr ‚üĻ CORE`. "Prize = that one named
> hypothesis at prize support ‚ÄĒ open = BGK." `BridgeBounded` claims the chain needs only **C = 4**, "independent
> of tuple size". `BoundedCyclotomicIndep` says prize support is `C ~ 2 ln q ‚Čą 22`.

This is the rule-3 (thinness-essentiality) + at-the-prize-prime audit of that object. Define
`C*(n,p) = min max|g_j|` over nonzero half-basis relations (BHBI(ŌČ,N,C) is TRUE iff `C < C*`).

**Probes (exact, PROPER subgroups őľ_n ‚ää F_p*, prize ő≤‚Čą4, never n=q‚ąí1):**
`scripts/probes/probe_407_bounded_cyclotomic_indep.py` (MITM exact small-box),
`probe_407_bhbi_verify_n16.py` (independent full-brute, dual-engine), `probe_407_bhbi_verify_n32.py`
(LLL shortest + exact integer verification), `probe_407_bhbi_rule3_thinness.py` (thin-vs-thick control).

**RESULT 1 ‚ÄĒ C* erodes BELOW the chain's required C=4 at the prize prime, at fixed ő≤=4:**
| n  | prize prime p‚Čąn‚Āī | ő≤    | C*(thin) | chain needs C=4 |
|----|------------------|------|----------|-----------------|
| 8  | 3457‚Äď7681        | ~4.0 | **7‚Äď8**  | HOLDS (margin)  |
| 16 | 65537            | 4.00 | **4**    | **BREAKS (C*=4)** |
| 32 | 1048609,1179649  | ~4.0 | **1**    | **BREAKS (C*=1)** |

**DUAL-ENGINE VERIFIED (n=16, p=65537, ŌČ=64, primitive 16th root, ŌČ^8=‚ąí1):** independent full-brute
confirms `BHBI(ŌČ,8,3)` TRUE (no relation, all |g|‚Č§3) and `BHBI(ŌČ,8,4)` FALSE with explicit witness
**g=(‚ąí4,‚ąí4,‚ąí4,‚ąí1,‚ąí1,‚ąí1,0,0)**, exact `ő£ g_j ŌČ^j = ‚ąí196611 = ‚ąí3¬∑65537 ‚Č° 0`. So **C*(n=16,prize prime)=4
exactly** ‚üĻ the chain's `BoundedHalfBasisIndep ŌČ 8 4` is **precisely, machine-checkably FALSE**, refuting
the `BridgeBounded` "C=4 suffices, independent of tuple size" required-support claim AT the prize prime.
**n=32 (p=1048609,1179649):** verified height-1 sign-relations `ő£ ¬ĪŌČ^j = ¬Īp` (support 11/16, ŌČ genuine
primitive 32nd root) ‚üĻ even `BHBI(ŌČ,16,1)` is FALSE.

**RESULT 2 (the rule-3 wall ‚ÄĒ the real map) ‚ÄĒ the THINNESS signal of C* CLOSES at large n:**
Control: thin 2-power ŌČ half-basis vs generic THICK config of same size N, same prime, median/5 seeds.
| n  | p (ő≤‚Čą4)  | thin C* | thick C* (med) | verdict |
|----|----------|---------|----------------|---------|
| 16 | 65537    | 4       | 2              | **THIN>thick (thinness-essential)** |
| 32 | 1048609  | 1       | 1              | **TIE (NOT essential)** |
| 32 | 33554593 (ő≤=5) | 2 | 2          | **TIE (NOT essential)** |

C* is a thinness discriminator at n=16 (thin 4 vs thick 2) but the gap **closes by n=32** (thin==thick,
at both ő≤=4 and ő≤=5). At N=16 the prize-regime prime (p~n‚Āī~10‚Ā∂) is small enough that ~3^16 sign-vectors
into ~10‚Ā∂ residues FORCE a short relation for ANY config (Minkowski/pigeonhole) ‚ÄĒ the 2-power structure
buys no detectable independence advantage.

**CONSTRAINT LEMMA / VERDICT:** the `BoundedHalfBasisIndep` lever, while it CORRECTLY NAMES the open core
(`BoundedHalfBasisIndep ‚üĻ Wick ladder ‚üĻ CORE` is a sound reduction), is (a) FALSE at the prize prime
already at small C (C*=4 @ n=16, C*=1 @ n=32 ‚ÄĒ far below both C=4 and 2 ln q), and (b) its C* becomes
THICKNESS-(near-)INVARIANT at large n (thin==thick by n=32). So **a thinness-monotone bound on C* CANNOT
prove CORE** ‚ÄĒ the C*-of-the-half-basis object inherits the SAME rule-3 obstruction as the moment / energy
/ orbit-count family. The named hypothesis correctly localizes the wall but cannot be discharged by a C*
(bounded-relation-height) thinness argument: the discriminating content lives ABOVE the bounded-relation
floor, not in it. Mapped wall (rule-4 WIN), not a closure.

**HONEST SCOPE:** C* upper bounds via LLL at n‚Č•32 (exact verify of each witness; exact MITM at n‚Č§16).
Small n (8,16 exact; 32,64 LLL-UB). The thin==thick TIE at n=32 is robust across two primes; the n¬≤‚Āī/n‚Ā∂
LLL row was an LLL-resolution artifact (no relation found in thick configs at the largest prime) and is
EXCLUDED. Does NOT refute the prize (the reduction is sound; the hypothesis being false-where-needed IS the
BGK wall). No Lean change (empirical refutation + thickness-invariance map; axiom-clean trivially). No CORE
closure. Probe-first, dual-engine on the headline, honest on the rule-3 closure.

### STRENGTHENING (same lever, finer invariant): the height-1 relation COUNT is also non-discriminating ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-15
Follow-up to the C* closure above. A finer invariant than C\* is the NUMBER of height-1 (sign) relations
`#{g‚ąą{‚ąí1,0,1}^N : ő£ g_j ŌČ^j = 0}`. If thin had systematically FEWER, the bounded-indep deficit would be
graded (a surviving thinness signal). Probe `scripts/probes/probe_407_bhbi_relcount.py` (exact, vectorized
3^16 count). **Result (n=32, N=16, FOUR prize primes p‚Čąn‚Āī, ő≤=4.000):**
  | p | thin # | thick # (med/5) | direction |
  |---|--------|------------------|-----------|
  | 1048609 | 32 | 16 | THIN>thick (anti-rule-3!) |
  | 1048897 | 16 | 24 | thin<thick (rule-3) |
  | 1049057 | 32 | 19 | THIN>thick |
  | 1049089 | 16 | 21 | thin<thick |
The count SIGN-FLIPS above/below thick across primes at FIXED ő≤=4 ‚ÄĒ NO monotone thinness direction. At
the prize prime the 2-power coset's arithmetic (closure under doubling: ŌČ^{2j} relations) can even create
MORE low-height relations than a generic set (anti-rule-3). Confirms decisively: neither C\* NOR the
bounded-relation count is a thinness discriminator in the prize regime ‚üĻ the bounded-cyclotomic-independence
lever is rule-3-incompatible at large n by both invariants. (n=16: zero sign-relations for thin AND thick,
consistent with C\*>1 there.) Strengthens the mapped wall; no new receipt (same object as push 1fa2d5e58).

## EvenDirectionIncidenceCollapse (reverse 2-adic descent leg) is FALSE ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-15

**Object.** `EvenDirectionDescent.lean` proves the FORWARD leg of the 2-adic even-direction far-line
incidence descent unconditionally: `I_{n/2}` injects into `I_n(even direction x^{2a'})`
(`explainableScalars_sq_pullback_subset`). The clean prize-useful identity `I_n(even) = I_{n/2}` needs
the REVERSE containment `I_n(even) ‚äÜ I_{n/2}`, named as the open Prop `EvenDirectionIncidenceCollapse`
(consumed only as a hypothesis in `explainableScalars_sq_pullback_eq_of_collapse`). The file's honest
caveat: the `RS[őľ_n,2k]` codeword explaining a full even-direction line need not be the even pullback of
an `RS[őľ_{n/2},k]` codeword ‚ÄĒ its ODD part can carry agreement on a non-fibre-symmetric witness set.

**REFUTATION (exact, `scripts/probes/probe_407_even_direction_collapse.py`).** n=16, k=2, binding band
(half threshold s'=3, full threshold s=6 = 2s' per the fibre-doubling budget), far even line
`(x^{2k}, x^{2(k+1)})` on őľ_16 vs `RS[őľ_16,4]` against the descended `(x^k, x^{k+1})` on őľ_8 vs `RS[őľ_8,2]`:

| p   | \|I_n(even)\| | \|I_{n/2}\| | fwd (half‚äÜfull) | rev (full‚äÜhalf) | EQ |
|-----|---------------|-------------|------------------|------------------|----|
| 97  | 56            | 40          | TRUE             | **FALSE**        | no |
| 113 | 40            | 40          | TRUE             | TRUE             | yes|

At p=97 there are **16 scalars bad for the full even direction on őľ_16 but NOT bad on őľ_8**.
INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED (exact max-agreement, `verify`): ő≥ ‚ąą {14,17,21,23} each have full-domain
max-agreement = **6** (‚Č• 6 ‚üĻ bad against deg<4) while the squaring-descent half-line has max-agreement
only **2** (< 3 ‚üĻ NOT bad against deg<2). So the agreement-6 on őľ_16 is realized by an `RS[őľ_16,4]`
codeword whose odd part supplies ‚Č•4 of the 6 agreement points on a non-fibre-symmetric set that does not
descend ‚ÄĒ exactly the named caveat, now with explicit witnesses.

**CONSTRAINT LEMMA / VERDICT.** `EvenDirectionIncidenceCollapse` is FALSE in general (prime-dependent:
fails at p=97, holds at p=113 for the same n,k,band). Therefore the clean 2-adic even‚Äďodd identity
`I_n(even) = I_{n/2}` does NOT hold, and the squaring descent is genuinely **one-sided** (forward lift
only). Any descent/Stepanov argument that consumes the reverse collapse as a step is blocked: the
even-direction incidence on őľ_n strictly EXCEEDS `I_{n/2}` at the binding band (odd-part agreement is
non-descending). The forward leg `I_n(even) ‚Č• I_{n/2}` is the wrong direction for the prize upper bound,
and the reverse leg that would fix it is now refuted. Mapped wall (rule-4 WIN), not a closure; no Lean
change (the open Prop stands correctly named ‚ÄĒ this records that it is FALSE, so it cannot be discharged).
Probe-first, exact, dual-checked. Does NOT refute the prize (the forward brick is sound; the reverse
identity it leaves open is simply false, which is itself the obstruction).

## DFT-uncertainty quantitative core: classical UP bound VACUOUS + genuine far-line max-zeros is SUB-Johnson (two-small-coset), with the n/2-growth EXPOSED as the őľ_{n/2} even-subgroup artifact ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-15

**Lane.** Adversarial audit of the freshest conceptual frame (KB `INSIGHT-smooth-domain-hardness-IS-the-
uncertainty-principle.md`): the prize restated as the quantitative DFT-uncertainty question for Z_{2^őľ} ‚ÄĒ
for f on Z_n with DFT-support T={0..k‚ąí1,a,b}, is max-zeros s* = ‚ąö(kn) (Johnson) or k+őė(n/log n) (floor)?
The INSIGHT asserts "smooth n=2^őľ ‚üĻ s* grows ~‚ąö(kn) (Johnson)". Tested EXACTLY (rank characterization,
no coeff search), proper őľ_n, prize-band p‚Č°1(n), index m=(p‚ąí1)/n‚Č•2, multi-prime (incl. Fermat F_4=65537),
NEVER n=q‚ąí1, k=3.

**Object (exact).** s*(n,p,a,b) = max|Z|, Z‚äÜZ_n, with rank([w^{tz}]_{z‚ąąZ,t‚ąąT}) < |T|=k+2 (a nonzero
T-spectral f vanishing on Z). Computed exactly over every (k+1)-subset = every minimal-support codeword,
maximized over far-line directions (a,b).

**RESULT 1 ‚ÄĒ classical uncertainty LOWER bound is VACUOUS for the prize.** Donoho‚ÄďStark
|supp f|¬∑|supp f^| ‚Č• n with |supp f^|=k+2 ‚üĻ s* ‚Č§ n(1‚ąí1/(k+2)) = 0.8n (k=3). That is a FULL POWER OF n
ABOVE Johnson ‚ąö(kn) (n=2^20: DS-permitted 838860 vs Johnson 1773). Tao's strong UP (s*‚Č§k+1=capacity)
holds ONLY for n PRIME, not 2^őľ. ‚üĻ the "weak UP" frame is NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT: weak UP only permits
large s*, gives NO ‚ąö(kn) ceiling. The uncertainty frame, via known quantitative bounds, does not separate
Johnson from floor as a theorem. Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_407_uncertainty_bound.py`.

**RESULT 2 ‚ÄĒ the INSIGHT's "smooth s*‚ÜíJohnson/n-2 growth" is DEGENERATE (two-layer rule-2 trap, exact).**
Maxing over directions gave ALL s*=6,10,18 (s*‚ąík=3,7,15) at n=8,16,32 binding at a=n/2 ‚ÄĒ but witness audit
EXPOSES these as subgroup-supported:
- Layer 1: a=n/2 (antipodal / I=q‚ąí1 pencil) inflates s* by EXACTLY +1, p-independent across 4 primes each.
- Layer 2 (the trap prior a‚Č†n/2 filters MISS): the s*‚Čąn/2 values are the EVEN-SUBGROUP őľ_{n/2}=‚ü®2‚ü© vanishing
  set. n=32 dir(17,18) "nondeg" (a,b‚Č†16, gcd odd) s*=17 witness = {all 16 EVEN residues}‚ą™{1} = őľ_16 + 1 pt.
  When a or b sits near n/2, w^{ax} is ~constant on the even subgroup ‚üĻ rank collapse ‚üĻ spurious n/2 zeros.
  Correct degeneracy notion = WITNESS contains a ‚Č•n/4 subgroup-coset (NOT just a‚Č†n/2).
Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_407_uncertainty_witness_audit.py` (exact witness dump + subgroup/antipodal test).

**RESULT 3 ‚ÄĒ the GENUINE (subgroup-saturation-excluded) far-line max-zeros is SUB-Johnson, two-small-coset.**
Excluding witnesses containing a ‚Č•n/4 subgroup-coset, exact, multi-prime:
| n  | genuine s* | s*‚ąík | Johnson ‚ąö(kn) | witness structure |
|----|-----------|------|---------------|-------------------|
| 8  | 4         | 1    | 4.90          | interval {0,1,2,3} |
| 16 | 6         | 3    | 6.93          | two őľ_2-cosets {0,1,2}‚ą™{8,9,10} |
| 32 | 8         | 5    | 9.80          | two őľ_4-cosets {0,1,8,9,16,17,24,25} |
Genuine s* = 4,6,8 (s*‚ąík=1,3,5, +2 per doubling) sits STRICTLY BELOW Johnson at all three n, gap WIDENING
(Johnson √ó‚ąö2 per doubling vs genuine +2). Binding witness is ALWAYS two cosets of a SMALL subgroup. This
INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMS c.348 (far-line max-agreement ‚Č§ Johnson, cannot reach floor 1‚ąíŌĀ‚ąíőė(1/log n)) from the
DFT-uncertainty side, and pins the EXACT witness mechanism (super-(k+1) far-line agreement on őľ_{2^őľ} comes
ONLY from low-subgroup coset blocks: saturated ‚üĻ n/2 degeneracy; two-small-coset ‚üĻ sub-Johnson).
Probes: `scripts/probes/probe_407_maxzeros_exact.py`, `probe_407_maxzeros_sweep.py`.

**VERDICT (mapped constraint, rule-4 WIN; CORE not closed, not faked).** Corrects the INSIGHT KB ("smooth s*
grows ~‚ąö(kn)" ‚ÄĒ the apparent growth is the őľ_{n/2} subgroup artifact; the GENUINE far-line s* is SUB-Johnson)
and nails the exact TWO-LAYER degeneracy (a=n/2 ‚Üí +1; even-subgroup witness ‚Üí +n/2) that has been generating
false floor-signals in the uncertainty/lacunary lanes. The classical uncertainty LOWER bound (DS/Meshulam) is
vacuous (off by a full power of n). Does NOT refute the prize: it confirms (from a new, c.348-immune PROOF-
object angle) that the far-line/uncertainty face cannot reach the floor; the open core remains the per-frequency
BGK ‚ąö-cancellation wall. p-independent, exact, multi-prime, proper subgroups, never n=q‚ąí1.

## wf-NK (#444): Khovanskii / fewnomial bound on the binding over-determined incidence ‚ÄĒ REFUTED as a tight bound

**Lens.** The binding far-line incidence `I(a,b;r*)` counts ő≥ such that `f_ő≥(X)=X^a+ő≥X^b‚ąíh(X)`
(`h‚ąąRS[k]`, deg<k) vanishes on ‚Č• n‚ąír* points of őľ_n. `f_ő≥` is a SPARSE Laurent polynomial:
support `{a,b,0,‚Ä¶,k‚ąí1}` = at most `m=k+2` distinct exponents (CONFIRMED exact on all binders).
Khovanskii fewnomial theory bounds solution counts of a sparse system by the number of monomials
`m`, degree-free and field-free ‚ÄĒ formally the right regime for the now-combinatorial object.

**Measured binding data (exact, p-independent; n=16 in-tree verified e167dd9de):**
- n=8 k=2: r*=4 size=4 binder(5,2) `I=9`, support {0,1,2,5}, m=4=k+2
- n=12 k=3: r*=6 size=6 binder(6,4) `I=13`, support {0,1,2,4,6}, m=5=k+2
- n=16 k=4: r*=10 size=6 binder(10,4) `I=89` (PRIME), support {0,1,2,3,4,10}, m=6=k+2

**Why it fails (precise, two-sided):**
1. Classical Khovanskii `2^C(m,2)¬∑(n_var+1)^m` (1 eliminated ő≥-variable) = 1024/32768/2097152 ‚ÄĒ
   OVERSHOOTS I by 100√ó‚Äď24000√ó and grows EXPONENTIALLY in k. Gives őī*‚Üí1: vacuous.
2. Descartes-on-circle / cyclic `2(m‚ąí1)=2(k+1)` = 6/8/10 UNDERSHOOTS I=9/13/89 ‚ÄĒ it caps
   roots-PER-ő≥, not the COUNT of ő≥, so it is not even a valid upper bound on I.
3. I(n) is NOT a fewnomial-polynomial sequence: I(16)=89 is PRIME; the (n/2‚ąí1)¬≤ form (9,25,49)
   is REFUTED by it (matches n=8 only coincidentally). A degree-free fewnomial bound is a
   *polynomial* in (n,k); a non-polynomial prime-valued target cannot be matched tightly.
4. The fewnomial cap governs the per-ő≥ root count, but I is a UNION over C(n,size) witnesses; the
   distinctness collapse (89 distinct out of 8008 size-6 witnesses, n=16) is governed by how the
   forced ő≥'s COINCIDE ‚ÄĒ the additive/multiplicative structure of őľ_n, i.e. the BGK residual the
   lens is structurally blind to.
5. The sharpest fewnomial-on-a-cyclic-group bound (Tao uncertainty: nonzero fn on ‚Ą§/n with t
   nonzero Fourier coeffs has ‚Č§ n‚ąít zeros) requires n PRIME; for n=2^őľ it is VACUOUS. The prize's
   defining smooth highly-composite domain is exactly what defeats the fewnomial/uncertainty lens
   (corroborates in-tree 6507e61aa).

**Net:** the loosest VALID fewnomial bound is `C(n,size)` (= the NH union bound itself); fewnomial
theory adds nothing tighter. Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_wf3NK_fewnomial_incidence.py` (self-
contained, fast). Tag: **refuted** as a tight degree-free/p-free bound on I(n); the (k+2)-sparsity
and the precise failure mode are proven-per-fixed-n. ‚ÄĒ wf-NK

## cosh-MGF / exact-Bessel-saddle CORE bound CAPS at ‚ąö(ő≤/(ő≤‚ąí1))¬∑floor ‚ÄĒ "beats at n=8" is a small-n artifact (¬ß7.8, #444) ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-15

**Lane.** ¬ß7.8 of #444: the cosh-MGF identity (`Frontier/CoshMGFIdentity.lean`, axiom-clean in-tree)
`ő£_b cosh(‚ÄĖő∑_b‚ÄĖy) = ő£_r (q¬∑E_r/(2r)!) y^{2r}` gives, by per-b domination, the root-free max-free CORE
upper bound `M(n) = max_b‚ÄĖő∑_b‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ B(y) = arccosh(ő£_b cosh(‚ÄĖő∑_b‚ÄĖy))/y` (‚ąÄy>0). For G=őľ_n the char-0
even-moment GF is the Bessel law (`DyadicEnergyK1.lean`): `ő£_r E_r y^{2r}/(2r)! = I‚āÄ(2y)^{n/2}`, so the
CHAR-0-IDEALIZED bound (the BEST the mechanism gives IF char-0 energies held char-p) is
`B_char0(n,p) = min_{y>0} arccosh(p¬∑I‚āÄ(2y)^{n/2})/y`. The dossier (¬ß7.8) flags it as beating the floor
`‚ąö(2n log m)` at n=8 (8.56<9.99). NO prior worker probed whether this survives to larger n.

**RESULT 1 ‚ÄĒ "beats at n=8" is a SMALL-n ARTIFACT; the bound asymptotes to ~1.155√ófloor, never AT it.**
PART A (ő≤=4, p~n^4, m~n^3, char-0-idealized, wide-saddle):
| n | floor | B_char0 | B/floor | verdict |
|---|-------|---------|---------|---------|
| 8 | 9.99 | 7.90 | 0.791 | BEATS |
| 16 | 16.32 | 15.06 | 0.923 | BEATS |
| 32 | 25.80 | 26.58 | 1.030 | WORSE |
| 256 | 92.29 | 105.75 | 1.146 | WORSE |
| 16384 | 976.7 | 1137.1 | 1.164 | WORSE |
| 1048576 | 9339 | 10851 | 1.162 | WORSE |
B/floor ‚Üí ~1.16 (a CONSTANT factor ABOVE the floor), never reaching it. BEATS only n‚Č§16 at ő≤=4
(n‚Č§512 at fixed prize m=2^128, then crosses + grows). The n=8 BEATS is finite-n only.

**RESULT 2 ‚ÄĒ MECHANISM (analytic): the saddle COLLAPSES the exact Bessel to its GAUSSIAN/Wick leading term.**
At small y, `log I‚āÄ(2y) = y¬≤ ‚ąí (1/4)y‚Āī + ‚Ä¶`, so `(n/2)log I‚āÄ(2y) ~ (n/2)y¬≤` ‚ÄĒ THE WICK TERM (the ‚ąíy‚Āī/4
curvature is lower-order). Then `B ~ min_y [log p + (n/2)y¬≤]/y = ‚ąö(2n log p)` at `y* = ‚ąö(2 log p/n)`.
Floor = `‚ąö(2n log m)`. So **B_char0/floor ‚Üí ‚ąö(log p/log m) = ‚ąö(ő≤/(ő≤‚ąí1))** (m~n^{ő≤‚ąí1}, p~n^ő≤). ő≤=4 ‚üĻ
**‚ąö(4/3)=1.1547** (numeric 1.16); ő≤=5 ‚üĻ 1.118. NUMERIC CONFIRMATION (B_char0 vs ‚ąö(2n log p), ő≤=4):
MATCH to <0.5% at n=256..16M. The Bessel curvature (the only place a thin advantage could live) is
provably LOWER-ORDER and cannot close the log p/log m ratio.

**RESULT 3 ‚ÄĒ the char-0 bound is a VALID upper bound on the TRUE char-p M (PART B, exact FFT, multi-prime).**
n=8 at p=4129/28697/2000081 and n=16,32 at prize-band primes: B_char0 ‚Č• M_true (FFT period sup) at
every prime ‚ÄĒ the idealized bound is a genuine over-estimate of the real periods, so the cap is on a
VALID upper-bound mechanism, not a broken bound.

**RESULT 4 ‚ÄĒ RULE-3 thinness gate (PART C): thin-sensitive but only LOWER-ORDER.**
Thin 2-power Bessel `I‚āÄ(2y)^{n/2}` vs Gaussian/Wick `e^{ny¬≤/2}` feeding the SAME bound: THIN-BEATS-GAUSS
at EVERY n (n=8: 8.31<12.01; n=32768: 1664.1<1664.9). So the I‚āÄ curvature IS a genuine thin advantage
over generic-Wick (rule-3 PASS), but the gap thin-vs-gauss shrinks relative to the floor as n grows ‚üĻ
insufficient: the thin win is real but cannot cross the ‚ąö(ő≤/(ő≤‚ąí1)) wall.

**VERDICT (rule-4 mapped wall; CORE not closed, not faked).** The cosh-MGF / exact-Bessel-saddle
mechanism (¬ß7.8) caps at **‚ąö(ő≤/(ő≤‚ąí1))¬∑floor** (‚Čą1.155√ó at ő≤=4), NEVER at the floor, EVEN granted the
char-0 energies ‚ÄĒ so it fails BEFORE the ¬ß2 char-p DC-term obstruction (independent of it). The dossier's
"beats the floor at n=8" is a genuine small-n artifact. The decisive structural reason: at the saddle the
exact Bessel `(n/2)log I‚āÄ(2y)` collapses to its Wick leading term `(n/2)y¬≤` ‚ÄĒ so this IS the ¬ß4
second-order/meta-theorem wall at the MGF level, now QUANTIFIED as the ‚ąö(ő≤/(ő≤‚ąí1)) log-ratio gap. Removes
¬ß7.8 as a live hope: the cosh-MGF is a sharper SUBSTRATE but not a sharper BOUND. Probe:
`scripts/probes/probe_407_cosh_bessel_saddle_scaling.py` (wide geometric-grid saddle, large-z I‚āÄ
asymptotic, exact-FFT char-p cross-check, rule-3 thinness gate, analytic confirmation). Axiom-clean
(Python-only, no Lean changed). Co-authored wakesync.

## [C09] Jacobi-Sum Diagonal Energy Closure for the 6th Moment (r=3 single-rung) ‚ÄĒ REFUTED (false claim) / reduces-to-trivial

**Conjecture.** Express E_3(őľ_n) via Jacobi sums (|J|=‚ąöp) + order-8 cyclotomic numbers, claim
E_3(őľ_{2^őľ}) ‚Č§ 15n¬≥ exactly in char p above the order-8 Jacobi-degeneracy resultant, and thereby
"pin the r=3 rung of M(őľ_n) ‚Č§ ‚ąö(2n log p)."

**VERDICT: the load-bearing claim is FALSE.** There is no "r=3 rung of ‚ąö(2n log p)". The moment chain
(`GaussPeriodMomentBound.worstCaseIncompleteSumBound_of_energyBound`) gives, from a single rung r,
`M_r = (q¬∑(2r-1)‚Äľ¬∑n^r)^{1/2r}`. The formula ‚ąö(2n log p) is the value AFTER minimizing over r at the
optimum r* ‚Čą ln q. A single fixed rung r=3 gives `M_3 = (q¬∑15¬∑n¬≥)^{1/6}`, dominated by `q^{1/6}`.

**Quantitative kill (probe `scripts/probes/probe_c09_r3_rung_useless.py`, commit 0ba4197bb):**
prize regime n=2^30, q=n^ő≤:
- ő≤=4 (q=2^120): r=3 gives M_3 = 2^{5.65}¬∑n ‚Čą **50¬∑n** ‚ÄĒ WORSE than the trivial bound M ‚Č§ n.
- ő≤=5 (q=2^150): r=3 gives M_3 = 2^{10.65}¬∑n ‚Čą **1608¬∑n** ‚ÄĒ far worse than trivial.
- M_r first beats trivial (M<n) only at r=5 (ő≤=4) / r=10 (ő≤=5); reaches the ‚ąö(2n log p)‚Čą12.9‚ąön
  target only at r* ‚Čą ln q ‚Čą 83 (ő≤=4) / 104 (ő≤=5).
- Algebraic: r=3 beats trivial iff q < n¬≥/15, i.e. ő≤ < 3 ‚ÄĒ OUTSIDE the prize band (ő≤‚Č•4). So in the
  prize regime r=3 NEVER beats trivial, let alone reaches Johnson or capacity.

**Even granting E_3 ‚Č§ 15n¬≥ in char p, r=3 pins NOTHING past Johnson** ‚ÄĒ it sits on the trivial/negative
side. And the E_3 ‚Č§ 15n¬≥ char-p step is itself the SAME open residual `RepThree` char-p transfer
(`GaussianEnergyThreeRepThree.lean` reduces `GaussianEnergyBound G 3` to the order-6 antipodal-pairing
residual; the Jacobi/cyclotomic-number reframing is a different bookkeeping of that same char-0‚Üíchar-p
crossing, still open at prize scale). So the conjecture is doubly non-closing: (1) its arithmetic
("r=3 rung of ‚ąö(2n log p)") is FALSE, and (2) its only nontrivial content (E_3 char-p) is the named
open residual, not new proven math.

Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_c09_r3_rung_useless.py` (commit 0ba4197bb). Python-only; no Lean changed.

## [C23] GM-MDS Dual-Zero-Pattern Certificate for őľ_n ‚ÄĒ SECRETLY-OPEN (existence-vs-fixed horn)

**Date:** 2026-06-14. **Verdict:** `secretly-open` (the step that would carry it past Johnson is
the open one; for the GM-MDS certificate over fixed őľ_n it is provably FALSE ‚ÄĒ the certificate
vanishes). **Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_c23_gmmds_fixed_vs_generic.py`, commit `ce07c4033`.

**The claim.** Encode the L+1 candidate codewords as a GM-MDS dual zero-pattern Z; it satisfies the
Brakensiek‚ÄďGopi‚ÄďMakam |Z_S| ‚Č§ (k‚ąí1)|S| solvability criterion (because the far-line agreement set is
őľ_gcd-coset-closed, orbit-count law L3), so Lovett Thm 1.7 gives realizability at field size ‚Č§ (k‚ąí1)!
< q, capping the list at k‚ąí1 and pinning őī* = capacity ‚ąí őė(1/log n).

**The defect (existence-vs-fixed).** In-tree `LovettThm17 F n` is a *generic/symbolic* statement:
the union polynomial family `pFamUnion(V,k)` is `LinearIndependent` over `MvPolynomial (Fin n) F` ‚ÄĒ
i.e. the symbolic generalized-Vandermonde determinant (a polynomial in the *eval points*) is not the
zero polynomial, so the pattern is realizable for SOME (generic) choice of points, and field size
‚Č§ (k‚ąí1)! is the number of points the construction is *free to pick*. The prize FIXES the eval set to
őľ_n (a proper subgroup). The relevant certificate is then that symbolic det *evaluated* at the fixed
x_i ‚ąą őľ_n. C23 conflates "realizable for some eval set" with "realizable for the prescribed őľ_n".

**The wall (already in-tree).** At the fixed smooth domain őľ_n the certificate `det(ő∂^{ő≤_j¬∑i})` is
nonzero **iff** the abacus n-core of the shape is empty (`HOMDSSmoothObstruction.homds_det_ne_zero_iff_nCoreEmpty`),
and for the past-Johnson extremal shape (a rectangle a^h, h = L < n codewords) that holds **iff n | a**
(`RectNCore.rectBeta_nCoreEmpty_iff`). Generic list widths (n ‚ą§ a) ‚Üí NONEMPTY core ‚Üí the fixed-őľ_n
certificate VANISHES. The cyclic symmetry x^n=1 that makes the domain "smooth" is exactly what
annihilates the GM-MDS certificate in the capacity regime.

**Probe evidence (proper őľ_n, p prime ‚Čą 64¬∑n¬≥ ‚Čę n¬≥, n < p‚ąí1, NEVER n = p‚ąí1).** For n ‚ąą {16,32,64},
k = n/2, every list height h ‚ąą {2,4,k}, every past-Johnson width a (Johnson agreement and Johnson+1):
- GM-MDS criterion: granted True. Symbolic det at GENERIC random huge-prime points: nonzero (Lovett
  applies, generic realizable).
- **The SAME det evaluated at the fixed őľ_n over F_p: ZERO** for every n ‚ą§ a (the past-Johnson regime).
  It is nonzero only at the n | a "lucky" widths, which are not the adversarial list shapes.
The in-tree dichotomy `nCoreEmpty(a^h) ‚áĒ n|a ‚áĒ őľ_n-det ‚Č† 0` reproduced over fixed őľ_n with 0 mismatches.

**Why not `reduces-to-johnson`.** C23's conclusion (list ‚Č§ k‚ąí1, őī* past Johnson) would indeed be past
Johnson IF the fixed-őľ_n certificate were nonzero. It is not ‚ÄĒ GM-MDS provides no closed argument that
reaches past Johnson here. **Why not a hidden open Lovett lemma.** `lovettThm17_unconditional` and
`lovettPrimitiveCase_unconditional` are proven sorry-free in tree, so the GM-MDS engine itself is
closed; the conjecture's "LovettPrimitiveCase reduced-not-closed" is outdated. The genuinely missing
step is fixed-őľ_n nonvanishing in the past-Johnson regime ‚ÄĒ the open structured/p-dependent bound ‚ÄĒ
and for the GM-MDS certificate it is provably FALSE. So GM-MDS cannot be the off-BGK lever; matches
the route-elimination meta-theorem (closed/cyclotomic objects cap at Johnson).

Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_c23_gmmds_fixed_vs_generic.py` (commit `ce07c4033`). Python-only; no Lean changed.

## 2026-06-14 (#444 C42): SECRETLY-OPEN ‚ÄĒ CZ25 Subspace-Design List-Recovery Coordinate-Fiber Cap (R2)

**Conjecture C42 (gmmds-homds, feas 4).** Via the CZ25 subspace-design framework, claim the
őī-close codewords on a far line span dimension ‚Č§ r (`CZ25CoordFiberCap`), so list-recovery gives
a constant list PAST Johnson; the determining-tuple existence is axiom-clean in-tree. REDUCES-TO:
CZ25 subspace-design list-recovery + in-tree `exists_determining_tuple` (axiom-clean) +
`CZ25CoordFiberCap`.

**Verdict: secretly-open** (and where actually proven in-tree, only reaches Johnson). The
load-bearing claim "the őī-close codewords span dim ‚Č§ r" IS the in-tree OPEN Prop
`CZ25CoordFiberCap`, not a theorem. This is a clean structural/logical horn ‚ÄĒ no őľ_n number-theory
counterexample needed; the audit is of the in-tree CZ25 / Guruswami‚ÄďWang chain itself.

**The audit (all from axiom-clean in-tree files).**
1. `CZ25CoordFiberCap` is a `def ‚Ä¶ : Prop` (a named hypothesis), the affine-flat coordinate-fiber
   cap `‚ąĎ_i #{c‚ąąL : c_i=f_i} ‚Č§ ((|L|‚ąí1)¬∑ŌĄ(r‚āÄ)+1)¬∑n`
   (`ListDecoding/CZ25SpanBoundBridge.lean:92`). The workbench
   (`PROXIMITY_PRIZE_WORKBENCH.lean:142`) lists it verbatim as the **R2 GAP**.
2. The ONLY unconditional, axiom-clean discharge of `CZ25CoordFiberCap` in-tree is
   `cz25CoordFiberCap_of_ncard_le_one` (`ListDecoding/CZ25UniqueDecodingSlice.lean:80`), whose
   hypothesis `hle` is "every candidate list has ‚Č§ 1 codeword" = the **unique-decoding /
   sub-Johnson** regime. Past Johnson a coordinate fiber is a full affine flat of size `q^{dim} ‚Čę
   dim+1` (the documented "q^dim vs dim+1" obstruction, `CZ25SpanDimension.lean:292‚Äď302`); the cap
   is then OPEN. (Probe: at q=2^30, dim‚Č•2 the flat exceeds the naive linear cap by >1e17√ó.)
3. Every other in-tree path is conditional `*_of_*`:
   `cz25CoordFiberCap_of_interp_and_multiplicity` ‚üł {BRICK-I, BRICK-V, BRICK-W, BRICK-L}, and:
   - **BRICK-W** (`GWDirectionFinrankLe`) was MACHINE-REFUTED as false for genuine codes
     (see "R2 / GW kernel mis-wiring catch" entry above; `GWDirectionScopedWiring.lean`).
   - **BRICK-L** (`GWAffineFiberCharge`) was REFUTED as literally stated (empty close list ‚áí
     `finrank ‚Č§ ‚ąí1`) and re-reduced to `GWFiberCardCharge`, whose own honesty note states it is
     "**as deep as the GW capacity theorem itself ‚Ä¶ the irreducible algebraic kernel**"
     (`GWFiberChargeRepair.lean:52‚Äď59`); discharged ONLY on the singleton/sub-Johnson slice
     (`GWFiberCardCharge_holds_of_singleton`).
4. `exists_determining_tuple` IS axiom-clean (as C42 says) ‚ÄĒ but it **consumes** the dimension
   bound as a hypothesis `hr : finrank F H ‚Č§ r`; it does NOT produce it
   (`SubspaceDesignLineDecodable.lean:81`). So the "span dim ‚Č§ r" C42 calls closed is the *input*,
   supplied by the OPEN `CZ25CoordFiberCap`.
5. Framework mismatch: this is the FOLDED-RS / s-folded subspace-design route (carrier
   `őĻ ‚Üí Fin s ‚Üí F`, hypothesis `IsSubspaceDesign s ŌĄ C`). The PRIZE object is PLAIN RS over an
   explicit őľ_n (s=1), where the design profile collapses (ABF26 Lemma 2.17 repair: `ŌĄ ‚Č• ŌĀ‚ąí1/n`)
   and the worst-case far-line incidence is not controlled by any nonvacuous design budget ‚ÄĒ
   exactly GG25's plain-RS gap (R1).

**Mechanism.** C42 reduces past-Johnson list-decoding to `CZ25CoordFiberCap`, the named OPEN
in-tree Prop (= the irreducible GW capacity kernel, "as deep as the whole theorem"), proven ONLY
on the sub-Johnson / unique-decoding slice (`|L| ‚Č§ 1`). The "span dim ‚Č§ r" is the open input, not
a closed fact. Hidden open step = the affine-flat coordinate-fiber cap past Johnson.

Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_c42_cz25_coordfibercap_open.py` (structural audit + q^dim-vs-dim+1).

## C01 "Antipodal Sumset Saturation Pin" (Lam-Leung + Pl√ľnnecke-Ruzsa): REFUTED ‚ÄĒ offset is n/4, NOT log‚āā(n) ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-14

**Conjecture C01:** the bad-scalar far-line crossing obeys `w ‚Č§ k + log‚āā(n/gcd(b‚ąía,n))`; maximizing
over `gcd(b‚ąía,n)=1` (`d=n/gcd=n`) pins `őī* = (1‚ąíŌĀ) ‚ąí log‚āā(n)/n`, claimed to beat Johnson for all
`ŌĀ‚ąą{1/2,1/4,1/8,1/16}` once `n‚Č•16`. Claimed reduction: in-tree Lam-Leung coset/isolated split +
Beukers-Smyth isolated count + Pl√ľnnecke-Ruzsa `|rA|‚Č§K^r|A|`.

**Verdict: refuted-false.** C01 IS the already-killed `őī*=(1‚ąíŌĀ)‚ąílog‚āā(n)/n` candidate (commit
`ce8cb602e`, n=64) re-stated with an antipodal-sumset *mechanism*; the mechanism's own structural
prediction (`offset = log‚āā(n/gcd)`) is contradicted by the exact char-0 far-line incidence.

**Decisive datum ‚ÄĒ the crossing offset `s*‚ąík` is `n/4` (LINEAR), not `log‚āā(n)`:** exact char-0
`(k+1)`-subset divided-difference engine (= in-tree `probe_char0_deltastar_n64_BIG.py`), proper
subgroup `őľ_n<F_p*`, `p` prime, `p‚Čęn¬≥`, NEVER `n=p‚ąí1`, two primes per point:

| n  | k | ŌĀ    | measured `s*‚ąík` | C01 `log‚āā(n)` | campaign `n/4` | őī* (=3/4‚ąíŌĀ) |
|----|---|------|-----------------|---------------|----------------|-------------|
| 16 | 2 | 1/8  | **4**           | 4             | 4              | 0.62500     |
| 32 | 2 | 1/16 | **8**           | 5             | 8              | 0.68750     |
| 64 | 2 | 1/32 | **16**          | 6             | 16             | 0.71875     |

At `n=16` `log‚āā16 = 16/4 = 4` *coincide* ‚ÄĒ that accidental tie is the entire basis of C01-B. They
diverge at `n=32` (5 vs 8; data=8) and blow apart at `n=64` (6 vs 16; data=16, off by 10). The
worst `n=64` pencil is `(2,34)` (`b‚ąía=32=n/2`, `gcd=32`, `d=2`), NOT a `gcd=1` direction ‚ÄĒ so even
C01-A's "maximizer is `gcd=1`, `d=n`" premise fails at `n=64`; and where a `gcd=1` pencil ties for
the offset (`n=32`, pencil `(8,17)`), its offset is `8=n/4`, NOT `log‚āā(d)=log‚āā32=5`. Either way the
`log‚āā(n/gcd)` bound is loose by `őė(n)`.

**Which horn:** `őī*=3/4‚ąíŌĀ` is a CONSTANT 1/4 below capacity (`s*‚ąík=n/4` is linear, so the gap
`(cap‚ąíőī*)=1/4` is `ŌĀ`-uniform and does NOT shrink). This is the rigorous char-0 far-line UPPER bound
OFF the BGK wall ‚ÄĒ it does not track the `őė(1/log n)` floor at all, and `őī*=(1‚ąíŌĀ)‚ąílog‚āā(n)/n` is
simply a wrong (too-aggressive-toward-capacity) value. The Pl√ľnnecke-Ruzsa `K^r` multiplier the
conjecture invokes is exactly the slack that makes `log‚āā(n/gcd)` an over-tight (false) bound rather
than the true `n/4`: restricted subset-sums of antipodal cosets do NOT saturate at coset size in the
window interior; the bad set is a free-interpolation arc (consistent with the P4 Mann/Lam-Leung
refutation at `DISPROOF_LOG` "char-0 I_0(őī) has NO clean ŌĀ-uniform CLOSED FORM").

**p-independence (char-0 faithful):** worstI band identical across the two primes at n=16,32; the
small `¬Ī32` deltas at n=32/64 in raw counts are the `+1`/`ő≥=0`-edge multiplicity wobble, irrelevant
to the integer offset (always exactly 4,8,16).

**Probes committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c01_worst_direction.py` (flushed; gcd-class offset
table n=16,32, dual-prime), `scripts/probes/probe_c01_antipodal_sumset_pin.py` (general engine);
re-confirmed with the in-tree `probe_char0_deltastar_n64_BIG.py --n 64 --k 2`
(`w_cross=18, s*‚ąík=16=n/4, őī*=0.71875`). Consistent with commit `ce8cb602e`.

## [C21] (gmmds-homds) Affine-General-Position Restriction: őī*=capacity via genpos far-line incidence ‚ÄĒ REFUTED

**Claim:** the worst-case far-line candidate list (bad-alpha messages of `RS[k]` that
őī-agree with `x^a+őĪ x^b`) is **affinely independent** in the window interior, so the
in-tree `mds_genpos_list_bound` caps it at `L<budget`, pinning `őī*‚Č•1‚ąíŌĀ‚ąíc/log n` past
Johnson. Defense: "affine independence is forced because any low-degree őľ_n-supported
vanishing is capped at `2(k‚ąí1)<a` roots by Mann/Conway-Jones."

**Verdict: refuted-false.** The premise confuses two unrelated objects. `mds_genpos_list_bound`
(`ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/HigherOrderMDSListGenPos.lean:104`) requires `hindep :
LinearIndependent K (fun j => m_{j+1} ‚ąí m_0)` ‚ÄĒ affine independence of the **candidate
message list** in the `k`-dimensional message space. That has nothing to do with root
counts of őľ_n-supported vanishings; it is hard-capped by `dim = k`: any set of messages
(deg `< k` polys) has affine rank `‚Č§ k+1`. The moment the list exceeds `k+1` elements ‚ÄĒ
which it does massively in the deep window interior ‚ÄĒ the family is **necessarily affinely
DEPENDENT** and `mds_genpos_list_bound` is vacuous. This is exactly the affinely-dependent
line/incidence case the in-tree docstrings flag as the open higher-order-MDS direction
(`HigherOrderMDSListGenPos.lean:24-26`, `LineListBound.lean:28-29`,
`MonomialLineListBridge.lean:37-38`: "the RS[k+1] list concentrates to its first moment,
i.e. the list-decoding prize itself").

**Decisive datum ‚ÄĒ list size I vs affine rank R, exact over F_p, p>>n^3, őľ_n PROPER subgroup,
NEVER n=p‚ąí1** (`scripts/probes/probe_c21_genpos_affine_rank.py`, commit `545a978274`):

| n  | k | pencil (a,b) | thr (deep interior) | list I | affine rank R | cap k+1 |
|----|---|--------------|---------------------|--------|---------------|---------|
| 8  | 2 | (3,2)        | 3                   | **40** | 3             | 3       |
| 8  | 2 | (4,2)        | 3                   | 17     | 3             | 3       |
| 8  | 4 | (5,4)        | 5                   | **40** | 5             | 5       |
| 8  | 4 | (6,4)        | 5                   | 20     | 5             | 5       |
| 16 | 4 | (5,4)        | 5                   | **2256**| 5            | 5       |

Everywhere the deep-interior list is nontrivial, `I >> R` and `R` saturates at exactly
`k+1` (the affine-rank cap). The worst-case list is affinely dependent by a factor of
`I/R = 13.3, 5.7, 8, 4, 451`. C21's affine-independence premise is false precisely where
it is needed.

**Which horn:** secretly-open masquerading as closed via a false premise. C21's only
beyond-Johnson lever (`mds_genpos_list_bound`) is **vacuous on the worst-case object**;
the real worst case is the affinely-dependent far-line incidence = the open BGK / line-ball
core (`OpenCoreConditionalPin.WorstCaseIncidenceBounded`). Mann/Conway-Jones caps the
INNER per-witness őľ_n-root count, not the OUTER affine rank of the bad-alpha message family;
conflating them is the error. Consistent with the route-elimination meta-theorem (genpos
caps at Johnson; past Johnson needs the open p-dependent BGK moment) and with
`probe_407_lacunary_pencil_Npencil.py` (deep-interior I jumps far above budget n).

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c21_genpos_affine_rank.py` (commit `545a978274`).

## C20 (#444): Khovanskii fewnomial real-analogue + char-faithful complexification ‚ÄĒ REFUTED (false premise) ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-15

**Claim.** The ragged (non-coset) őľ_n-roots of a `(k+2)`-nomial `f=X^a+ő≥X^b‚ąíh` (`deg h<k`)
are bounded by Khovanskii's `n,p`-INDEPENDENT fewnomial count `2^{C(k+2,2)}(k+2)`, so
`s* ‚Č§ core(n/2)+const(n)`, giving `őī*=1/2` past Johnson for `ŌĀ<1/4`. Coset stripping and char
transfer cited as in-tree proven (`FactorizationRigidity`, `RigidityReductionPrizeScale`).

**The fatal premise is false.** Khovanskii's fewnomial theorem bounds **POSITIVE REAL**
solutions (a degree-free count, valid because real sign-variation is limited). There is **no
real-analogue over fields with roots of unity**: the number of roots of a `t`-nomial in
`őľ_n ‚äā F_p` (or even `őľ_n ‚äā ‚Ąā`) is governed by the **DEGREE**, not the monomial count.

**Machine/numeric witness (extends in-tree `_RThinSqrtNKRefuted.lean` to prize-band primes).**
The `k=2` family `f=(X^m+1)(X‚ąí1)=X^{m+1}‚ąíX^m+X‚ąí1` is a `(k+2)=4`-nomial (support `{0,1,m,m+1}`).
Its `őľ_{2m}`-root set `S={x:x^m=‚ąí1}‚ą™{1}` is **genuinely ragged** (coeff at `X^1` is `‚Č†0` ‚üĻ not a
`őľ_{d'}`-coset-union for any `d'>1`, by factorization rigidity) of size `m+1 = n/2+1`. Over prize
primes (`p` prime, `p‚Č°1 mod n`, `p>n^3`, `n<p^{1/4}`, dyadic `n=2^őľ`, NEVER `n=p‚ąí1`):

| n | m | p | ragged őľ_n-root count | Khovanskii cap `2^C(4,2)¬∑4` | violation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 512 | 256 | 68719484929 | **257** | 256 | yes |
| 1024 | 512 | 1099511630849 | **513** | 256 | yes |
| 2048 | 1024 | 17592186062849 | **1025** | 256 | yes |
| 4096 | 2048 | 281474976768001 | **2049** | 256 | yes |

The count is exactly `m+1`, őė(n); the alleged "constant" cap `256` is a fixed number ‚ÄĒ the gap
is unbounded. Violation begins at `n=512`.

**Why even the "salvageable" reading fails to reach past Johnson.** Per C20's own coset-stripping,
suppose one only bounds the ragged EXCESS over the largest coset core. The probe shows
`S = (őľ_m-coset of size n/2) ‚ą™ {one straggler}`: the excess is `1=O(k)` (correct, and exactly the
in-tree `ragged_excess_le_degree`), but the **coset core itself is n/2** = the antipodal `őľ_{n/2}`
coset, i.e. the **Johnson-radius-sized** object. So `s* ‚Čą core = n/2` ‚üĻ `őī* = 1‚ąíŌĀ`?? No: the
core IS the closed/antipodal/cyclotomic object the route-elimination meta-theorem proves caps at
**Johnson** (`1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ`). Khovanskii contributes nothing to the core, and the core is where all the
mass is. C20 gives no past-Johnson lever.

**Which horn:** refuted-false (false load-bearing premise ‚ÄĒ Khovanskii has no roots-of-unity
analogue), degenerating to reduces-to-johnson on the salvageable reading. Consistent with the
prior DISPROOF_LOG entry **wf-NK (#444)** (fewnomial adds nothing tighter than the `C(n,size)`
union bound; Tao-uncertainty needs `n` prime, vacuous for `n=2^őľ`).

**Probes committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c20_khovanskii_ragged_root_count.py`,
`scripts/probes/probe_c20_khovanskii_ragged_certificate.py` (commit `fb3785b58`).

## C17 (#444) ‚ÄĒ Kelley-Owen Dilation-Pencil "Sharp Constant" s* ‚Č§ ‚ąö((k+1)n) as EQUALITY: REFUTED ‚Üí reduces-to-johnson

**Claim.** Generalize the proven trinomial bound (`¬Ĺ+‚ąön` roots, `t=3`, `k=1`) to `(k+2)`-term
faces via the `k`-dimensional dilation pencil, asserting the sharp double-count gives
`s* ‚Č§ ‚ąö((k+1)n) + O(k)` as an **EQUALITY realized by the antipodal pencil**, pinning `őī*` exactly
at `1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ(1+o(1))`. Reduces to in-tree `KelleyOwenDilationPencil.pencil_card_core` +
`stepanov_sqrt_bound` + Heath-Brown-Konyagin Stepanov for the `k`-term case.

**Why it falls (two horns at once).**

1. **The "sharp equality / upper bound" is FALSE for the `(k+2)`-face.** The in-tree
   machine-checked witness `_RThinSqrtNKRefuted` is exactly a `(k+2)`-sparse face (`k=2`):
   `P(X) = (X^m+1)(X‚ąí1) = X^{m+1} ‚ąí X^m + X ‚ąí 1`, support `{0,1,m,m+1}` (`t=4=k+2`),
   `m=n/2`. Its root set in `őľ_n` is `{x : x^m=‚ąí1} ‚ą™ {1}`, of size `n/2+1` ‚ÄĒ **linear in `n`,
   not `‚ąön`**, and `> ‚ąö((k+1)n)` for every `n‚Č•8`. So the dilation-pencil double-count is not
   even a valid worst-case upper bound (let alone a tight equality): the `k`-dimensional pencil's
   blocks overlap in a whole coset core, `M(S) ‚Č• n/2‚ąí1` by `PencilAutocorrelation.autocorr_ge_coset_core`,
   and the Corr√°di/Fisher count collapses to `r ‚Č§ őė(n)`. The antipodal pencil does NOT realize
   `‚ąö((k+1)n)`; it realizes `n/2`. Probe `probe_c17_dilation_pencil_sharp.py` reproduces
   `|S|=n/2+1` over proper dyadic `őľ_n`, `p` prime, `p~n^4 ‚Čę n^3`, `n‚Č†p‚ąí1` (commit `b73fa70e8`).

2. **Even where the bound IS an upper bound, it lands EXACTLY at Johnson.** With `k=ŌĀn`,
   `‚ąö((k+1)n) ~ ‚ąö(ŌĀn¬∑n) = n‚ąöŌĀ` = the Johnson agreement count, i.e. radius `1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ` = the Johnson
   radius. The conjecture's own stated conclusion ("pins `őī*` at `1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ(1+o(1))`") is the Johnson
   radius verbatim. The `‚ąö` upgrade special to `t=3` (1-D pencil, overlap-1) degrades to `‚ąö(kn)`
   for `t=k+2` (`k`-D pencil, overlap-`k`) ‚ÄĒ documented in `_KelleyOwenDilationPencil.lean`
   ¬ß"Honest scope". Heath-Brown-Konyagin Stepanov gives the same `‚ąö(kn)`; it never reaches the
   `n`-independent isolated-excess budget (`Kelley Conj 3.2 / KO Heuristic 1.4`, recognized open).

**Which horn:** `reduces-to-johnson` (the in-tree bricks it cites are real and axiom-clean, but
they prove only the `t=3` `‚ąön` rung and the `t=k+2` `‚ąö(kn)`=Johnson degradation), with the
sharp-equality premise outright `refuted-false` by `_RThinSqrtNKRefuted`. Same family and same
witness as the prior **C20 (Khovanskii ragged root count)** entry above. Khovanskii/HBK Stepanov
adds nothing past Johnson; the coset core (the closed/antipodal object) is where the mass is and
caps at Johnson by the route-elimination meta-theorem.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c17_dilation_pencil_sharp.py` (commit `b73fa70e8`).

## C19 (#444): Mason-Stothers ABC degree-multiplicity pin via antipodal square descent ‚ÄĒ REFUTED (vacuous proof mechanism; the bound it claims to deliver IS the open core) ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-15

**Claim.** After antipodal even/odd descent, the isolated (ragged) witnesses inject into the
`őľ_{n/2}`-roots of `F = E¬≤‚ąíu¬∑O¬≤` with `E = u^{a/2}+ő≥u^{b/2}‚ąícE`, `deg E,O < k/2`. C19 asserts
**Mason-Stothers** (`Polynomial.abc` in Mathlib) on the coprime triple `X=E¬≤, Y=‚ąíu¬∑O¬≤, Z=F`
(`X+Y=Z`) with *small* `rad(u¬∑O¬≤)` forces the distinct-root count `‚Č§ 3k/2+1`, flat in `n`, giving
`őī*=1/2‚ąíO(ŌĀ)` past Johnson at `ŌĀ=1/16`. Cites in-tree `_IsoSparsityMasonStothers` (`O(k)` sparsity)
+ `_AntipodalEvenOddDescent`.

**The proof mechanism is VACUOUS ‚ÄĒ Mason-Stothers never beats the trivial `deg F` bound on the
ragged triple.** MS gives `max(deg X,deg Y,deg Z) ‚Č§ rad(X¬∑Y¬∑Z)‚ąí1 ‚Č§ rad(E)+rad(u¬∑O¬≤)+rad(F)‚ąí1`.
For the prize shape with `a,b ~ n`:
- `deg F = deg(E¬≤) = a ‚Čą n` (trivial bound);
- `rad(E) ‚Čą (a‚ąíb)/2 ‚Čą n/2` ‚ÄĒ the sparse binomial head `u^{a/2}+ő≥u^{b/2}` re-injects an `‚Čą n/2`
  radical that `deg O<k/2` does **not** control;
- `rad(u¬∑O¬≤) ‚Č§ 1+deg O < 1+k/2` ‚ÄĒ the ONLY small radical (the only `deg O<k/2` lever);
- `rad(F) ‚Čą deg F ‚Čą n` ‚ÄĒ in the genuinely-**ragged** case `F` is squarefree of full degree.

So MS reads `a ‚Č§ (a/2)+(k/2)+a‚ąí1 = (3a/2)+(k/2)‚ąí1 ‚Čę a`. The two `‚Čą n` radicals `rad(E)~n/2`,
`rad(F)~n` DROWN the small `rad(u¬∑O¬≤)`. This is exactly the in-tree analytic statement
`mason_stothers_iso_feed_vacuous` (`_IsoSparsityMasonStothers.lean:122`), whose own docstring says
"MS-direct yields a *proven* O(k) sparsity, and **reduces** |iso|‚Č§poly(k) to a named isolated /
non-coset root conjecture; it does **not** beat the general Kelley/BGK count."

**Machine/numeric witness (exact over GF(p), proper `őľ_n`, `p` prime, `p>n¬≥`, NEVER `n=p‚ąí1`).**
Built extremal prize-shape `P=x^a+ő≥x^b‚ąíc` (a,b even, deg c<k) planting `k+1` agreement points,
ran the antipodal descent, computed actual polynomial radicals (squarefree parts) of `E¬≤`, `u¬∑O¬≤`,
`F` over `F_p`, and tested `rad(E)+rad(u¬∑O¬≤)+rad(F)‚ąí1 < deg F` (= "MS beats trivial"):

| n | k | a | b | O‚Č°0? | degF | radE¬≤ | rad(uO¬≤) | radF | MS rhs | MS beats? |
|---|---|---|---|------|------|-------|----------|------|--------|-----------|
| 64 | 4 | 28 | 24 | no (ragged) | 28 | 14 | 2 | 28 | 43 | **no** |
| 64 | 8 | 56 | 40 | no (ragged) | 56 | 28 | 4 | 54 | 85 | **no** |
| 128 | 8 | 86 | 14 | no (ragged) | 86 | 43 | 4 | 85 | 131 | **no** |
| 128 | 16 | 86 | 34 | no (ragged) | 86 | 43 | 8 | 86 | 136 | **no** |

In **every genuinely-ragged (`O‚ČĘ0`) row ‚ÄĒ the ONLY case C19's mechanism targets** ‚ÄĒ MS rhs ‚Čą 3n/2
‚Čę deg F: MS is strictly vacuous. The only rows where MS "beats" the trivial bound are the
**pure-coset `O‚Č°0` case** (then `F=E¬≤` is a perfect square, `radF=radE¬≤=n/2`, `rad(uO¬≤)=0`), which
is **NOT** the ragged/isolated witnesses C19 needs ‚ÄĒ it is the in-tree closed-form antipodal case
the route-elimination meta-theorem already proves **caps at Johnson**. And even there MS only
recovers `root count ‚Čą n/2`, nowhere near the claimed `3k/2+1`.

**The deeper structural point.** C19 conflates (i) the *measured* fact `|iso| ‚Č§ 3k/2+1` flat in n
(real ‚ÄĒ confirmed by probes here and by `_AntipodalEvenOddDescent`'s `IsolatedCountResidual`) with
(ii) a *proof* that Mason-Stothers delivers it (false). The bound `|iso| ‚Č§ poly(k)` is **exactly**
the open isolated/non-coset root count = the recognised Kelley/BGK general-position cancellation one
2-adic level down (`IsolatedCountResidual`, `IsolatedNonCosetCountResidual`), which both in-tree
bricks explicitly NAME as the open residual they do NOT discharge. Mason-Stothers touches none of
it: the `T`-sparsity of `F` does not bound roots in a subgroup (`u^e‚ąí1` is 2-sparse with `e` roots),
and the MS radical bound is drowned by the sparse-square head.

**Which horn:** **secretly-open** ‚ÄĒ the bound C19 claims Mason-Stothers proves IS the open BGK/Kelley
isolated-root count; MS is provably vacuous on the ragged triple, so the past-Johnson pin requires
the unresolved residual. Degenerates to **reduces-to-johnson** on the only reading where MS is
non-vacuous (the pure-coset `O‚Č°0` antipodal core, capped at Johnson by the meta-theorem). Consistent
with **C20 (#444)** (no roots-of-unity Khovanskii analogue), **wf-NK (#444)** (fewnomial adds
nothing tighter than the union bound), and the in-tree `mason_stothers_iso_feed_vacuous` /
`IsolatedNonCosetCountResidual` self-documented gap.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_conjecture_refute_C19_mason_stothers.py` (self-contained,
sympy GF(p); exact radicals + ragged/pure-coset split).

## C32 (#444) ‚ÄĒ BGK Sum-Product Sup-Norm via BSG + 2-power antipodal collision ‚ÄĒ **SECRETLY-OPEN**

**Claim.** The BGK sum-product bound `M(őľ_n) ‚Č§ n^{1‚ąío(1)}` (ineffective) can be *sharpened* to the
prize target `‚ąö(n log(p/n))` past Johnson by combining the Balog‚ÄďSzemer√©di‚ÄďGowers (BSG) structure of
low-energy subgroups with the proven antipodal-only (`z + (‚ąíz) = 0`) collision structure at 2-power.

**Horn: secretly-open.** This conjecture *is the open core itself* (the prize statement
`max_{b‚Č†0}|ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n} e_p(bx)| ‚Č§ ‚ąö(2n log m)`, see `the-prize-isolated-direct-bgk-statement.md`),
re-stated with a *named proof strategy* attached. The strategy (BSG + antipodal) supplies **nothing
new**; the half-power gap remains exactly the named open BGK sup-norm bound.

**Why BSG is vacuous here (machine witness, proper `őľ_n`, `p` prime, `p ‚Čę n¬≥`, NEVER `n=p‚ąí1`).**
BSG is a *high-energy* theorem: it extracts an approximate subgroup from a set whose additive energy is
*large*. But `őľ_n` is at the **minimum-energy endpoint** ‚ÄĒ it is Sidon, and *jointly* Sidon with every
dilate. Measured exactly over GF(p):

| n | p | p/n¬≥ | M | log M / log n | prize ‚ąö(2n log(p/n)) | E¬≤(őľ_n,őĺőľ_n) | diagonal n¬≤ |
|---|---|------|---|----------------|----------------------|--------------|-------------|
| 8  | 5009   | 9.78 | 7.48  | **0.968** | 10.15 | 64   | 64   |
| 16 | 16417  | 4.01 | 13.11 | **0.928** | 14.90 | 256  | 256  |
| 32 | 131297 | 4.01 | 19.21 | **0.853** | 23.08 | 1024 | 1024 |

- **(A)** The true sup-norm sits at `log M / log n ‚Čą 0.85‚Äď0.97 = BGK SOTA (n^{1‚ąío(1)})`, **not 1/2**.
  No square-root cancellation is visible; the prize exponent `1/2` is nowhere in the measured data.
- **(B‚Ä≤)** The joint dilate energy `E‚Āļ(őľ_n, őĺőľ_n)` is **diagonal-only = n¬≤** for every tested dilate őĺ
  ‚ąČ őľ_n. So `őľ_n` is already the *best possible* additive input. BSG's hypothesis ("large energy ‚áí
  approximate subgroup") is vacuously satisfied: **there is no excess energy to harvest.** BSG +
  best-possible energy gives **zero exponent gain** ‚ÄĒ consistent with Kowalski 2024 (arXiv:2401.04756)
  and dual-assault L8: the BGK losses are **regime-driven (n = p^ő≥, ő≥ < 1/2), not seed-energy-driven.**
- **(C)** The sup-norm-from-energy conversion `M^{2r} ‚Č§ ő£_b|ő∑_b|^{2r} = p¬∑E_r` needs near-Gaussian `E_r`
  up to `r ~ log p`. Measured `E_r/((2r‚ąí1)!!n ≥)` departs from the Gaussian baseline already at small r
  (char-p wrap excess), and BSG/sum-product controls **only low-order (r=2,3)** energy. The deep-moment
  object at `r ~ log p` is exactly the named open residual, untouched by BSG.

**The structural point.** The antipodal-only collision structure (Mann/Conway‚ÄďJones, in-tree
`AntipodalBalanceBounded`) governs which char-0 vanishing relations exist; it provably **caps at
Johnson** (route-elimination meta-theorem). Combining a Johnson-capped closed object (antipodal) with
a high-energy theorem (BSG) whose hypothesis is vacuous on a Sidon set produces no past-Johnson lever.
The "sharpening" is just the original open BGK bound under a new name. The half-power `‚ąö(p)/n` deficit
is the recognised ~25-year-open analytic-NT problem (di Benedetto `n^{0.989}` needs `p^{1/4}<H<p^{1/2}`,
*outside* the prize band `n ‚Č™ ‚ąöp`).

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c32_bsg_antipodal_supnorm.py` (self-contained, sympy GF(p);
exact sup-norm, additive/dilate energy, r-th energy vs Gaussian). Commit `4bdcc12ea`.

## [C36] Heath-Brown-Konyagin Single-Curve Stepanov at the Prize Boundary ‚ÄĒ REFUTED-FALSE (2026-06-15)

**Claim.** The proven single-curve HBK Stepanov bound `M(mu_n) <= min(n^{5/8} p^{1/8}, n^{3/8} p^{1/4})`
"becomes nontrivial (below n) exactly at the prize boundary via a sharpened auxiliary-degree budget,"
giving a past-Johnson sup-norm ‚ÄĒ the elementary (Mathlib-formalizable) route via
`StepanovWeilEngine.weil_form_card_lt` + `exists_stepanov_auxiliary`.

**Verdict: REFUTED-FALSE.** Elementary arithmetic (probe
`scripts/probes/probe_c36_hbk_stepanov_boundary.py`). Writing `p = n^beta`, the two HBK bounds are, as
powers of n: `e1 = 5/8 + beta/8` and `e2 = 3/8 + beta/4`, so `M <= n^{min(e1,e2)}`.

- **Sub-trivial (below n) only for `beta < 3`** (`e1<1 <=> beta<3`, `e2<1 <=> beta<2.5`). At `beta=3`
  exactly, `e_min = 1` (equals trivial). The **prize band is `beta in {4,5}`** (`n < p^{1/4}`), where
  `e_min = 9/8 = 1.125` (beta=4) or `5/4 = 1.25` (beta=5): the bound is **WORSE than the trivial `n`**,
  by a factor `n^{1/8}` (= 2^3.8 at n=2^30) to `n^{1/4}` (= 2^7.5). It is not "nontrivial below n"; it
  exceeds n. This matches the in-tree literature ledger verbatim:
  `docs/kb/deltastar-407-reading-list-gaussperiods.md` line 60-62 ("Non-trivial only for `n >~ p^{1/3+e}`
  ... Prize regime? NO ‚ÄĒ VACUOUS") and line 112 ("HBK ... NO (vacuous)"), and
  `docs/kb/proximity-paley-gauss-period-litsweep-2026-06-13.md` line 70-71 (HBK applies for
  `n in [p^{1/3}, p^{1/2}]` only; below `p^{1/3}` "HBK is VACUOUS").
- **Past-Johnson? Never.** Johnson scale needs `e_min <= ~1/2`; `e1<=1/2` is impossible (`beta<=-1`) and
  `e2<=1/2 <=> beta<=1/2` (`p<=sqrt(n)`, deep in the FORBIDDEN full-group regime `n=p-1`). For every
  legal `beta`, HBK is `>= n^{5/8} >> n^{1/2}`. Even where (illegally) sub-trivial it is "sub-trivial but
  `>> sqrt(n)`" (litsweep line 70) ‚ÄĒ it never crosses Johnson, let alone past it.
- **The "sharpened auxiliary-degree budget" cannot help.** The exponent is fixed by the Stepanov
  degree/multiplicity accounting: the `p^{1/2}`-strength saving in the HBK exponents comes from the
  Frobenius relation `x^q=x` plus the QR-character multiplicity `g^{(q-1)/2}` (encoded in
  `weil_form_card_lt` via the `g^((q-1)/2)` factor and the `2A+deg g<q` block window). But
  `StepanovStructuredVacuous.lean` (`mu_n_roots_simple`, `stepanov_collapses_to_degree`) PROVES the
  subgroup relation `X^n-1` is SEPARABLE (char `p` odd, `p` nmid `n=2^mu`), so it manufactures NO
  multiplicity: Stepanov on `mu_n` collapses to the trivial `deg P` bound. The sqrt-saving the HBK
  exponents encode does not even apply to the subgroup object; tightening the linear-algebra constant in
  `exists_stepanov_auxiliary` moves no EXPONENT. `StepanovWeilEngine.lean`'s own "Honest scope" docstring
  (lines 29-32) states it carries "no `sqrt(q)`-strength claim by itself" and "even the full Weil bound
  recovers only the Johnson radius `sqrt(rho)`, never the past-Johnson delta*."

**Mechanism class.** Same horn as the entire HBK/Stepanov family: the proven bound is VACUOUS in the
prize band (`beta>=4`, `n<p^{1/4}`), exceeding even the trivial `n`; it is non-vacuous only at `n>p^{1/3}`
(beta<3, forbidden) where it is still `>> sqrt(n)` (never Johnson). The route is `q^{1/3}`-blind to the
thin prize subgroup ‚ÄĒ exactly as `deltastar-407-reading-list-gaussperiods.md` and the campaign DEAD-ROUTES
note ("Weil/sqrt(q): vacuous for n<sqrt(q)") already record.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c36_hbk_stepanov_boundary.py`.

## 2026-06-15 (#444 C28): Gross-Koblitz p-adic Gamma evaluation of the period argument ‚ÄĒ SECRETLY-OPEN

**Conjecture C28 (gauss-period-exact):** "Use Gross-Koblitz `tau(chi) = -pi^{s(chi)} prod Gamma_p(<..>)`
to evaluate the period `eta_c` exactly as an algebraic integer and claim the explicit p-adic Gamma
factors pin the complex arguments `arg tau(chi_j)` tightly enough to force `max_c|eta_c| <= C sqrt(n log p)`
past Johnson. This is an exact evaluation, not an estimate." Reduces-to: Gross-Koblitz + Stickelberger
+ in-tree period decomposition.

**Verdict: secretly-open.** Gross-Koblitz is a **p-adic** identity. It pins (i) the p-adic valuation
`v_p(tau(chi)) = s(chi)/(p-1)` (= Stickelberger), and (ii) the p-adic UNIT of `tau(chi)` via Morita's
`Gamma_p`. The complex (archimedean) MAGNITUDE `|tau(chi)| = sqrt(q)` is the char-independent Weil/Gauss
fact, already known and already used in-tree. The floor is `eta_b = (1/f) sum_j conj(chi^j(b)) tau(chi^j)`,
each `|tau| = sqrt(q)`, so `max_b|eta_b|` is the **sup over b of a DFT of f Gauss-sum vectors** ‚ÄĒ a function
of the **joint ARCHIMEDEAN PHASE** `{arg tau(chi^j)}_j` and the maximal coherence over the p frequencies b.
GK says nothing direct about the archimedean argument `arg tau(chi)` in C; that is precisely the OPEN
BGK joint-phase non-conspiracy. So pinning the floor from GK requires the open lemma ‚ÄĒ not closed.

**Probe (`scripts/probes/probe_conjecture_refute_C28_grosskoblitz_period.py`, commit `6b3ae67e0`):**
proper `mu_n` (n=8,16,32, 2-power), p prime, `p >> n^3` (margin 2x-20x), never `n=p-1`. Verified the
Gauss-period decomposition reproduces `eta_b` exactly (recon err ~1e-11), then compared the actual floor
`B = max_b|eta_b|` against a random-archimedean-phase null (replace each `arg tau(chi^j)` by a uniform
random phase, keep `|tau|=sqrt(q)`, same DFT sup). If GK forced sub-random coherence, B would sit
SYSTEMATICALLY BELOW the null (`gap_sigma >> 0`). Result: `gap_sigma <= 0` in 8/10 cases, percentile of B
in the null 0.46-1.00; B sits AT or ABOVE the random-phase mean (n=16,32 small-margin cases B notably
above). GK phases are statistically indistinguishable from random for the DFT sup ‚ÄĒ they add NO archimedean
rigidity. Consistent with `deltastar-407-route3-gauss-phase-pseudorandomness-2026-06-13.md` (floor IS the
random-phase value; the residual core is Gauss-sum joint phase non-conspiracy = BGK) and the
open-avenue inventory (Stickelberger/Hasse-Davenport "orthogonal" to the sup-norm handle).

| n | p | p/n^3 | B/sqrt(n) | null_mean/sqrt(n)~ | pctile | gap_sigma |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 8 | 1553 | 3.03 | 2.539 | 2.39 | 0.77 | -0.64 |
| 8 | 10289 | 20.10 | 2.728 | 2.78 | 0.47 | 0.20 |
| 16 | 12401 | 3.03 | 3.130 | 2.68 | 0.96 | -2.05 |
| 32 | 65537 | 2.00 | 4.457 | 2.85 | 1.00 | -7.82 |

**Net:** C28 is NOT a closure. The "exact evaluation" is genuine but lands in the WRONG metric (p-adic),
leaving the archimedean sup-of-DFT ‚ÄĒ the BGK joint-phase wall ‚ÄĒ fully open. Falls on the secretly-open horn.

## 2026-06-15 ‚ÄĒ CONJECTURE C30 "Sawin‚ÄďShusterman Large Sieve for Gauss Sums (orthogonality over the family)" SECRETLY-OPEN / REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c30_sawin_shusterman_largesieve.py)

Conjecture C30 (issue #444, algebraic-geometry, feasibility 1): apply the large sieve /
orthogonality over the Gauss-sum family to bound the second moment, and claim an *amplified*
version reaches the MAX `max_c|ő∑_c|` PAST Johnson rather than just the `‚ąön` average. REDUCES-TO:
Sawin‚ÄďShusterman large sieve for Gauss sums (family orthogonality) + Parseval + in-tree exact
large-sieve identity.

Verdict: **secretly-open** (with a reduces-to-johnson sub-finding). Both readings of "large sieve"
fall on a horn; the amplification adds NOTHING over the diagonal moment it is built from. Probe
`scripts/probes/probe_c30_sawin_shusterman_largesieve.py`, commit
`41fdfecce98c858843fab46311c8b969ae16d577`. All rows measured prize-faithfully: PROPER `őľ_n`
(`n = 2^őľ`, `z^{n/2} = ‚ąí1`), `p` PRIME, `n | p‚ąí1`, `p ‚Čę n¬≥`, NEVER `n = p‚ąí1`.

**This object is already characterized in-tree** (this is the third pass over it):
`Frontier/LargeSieveParsevalCollapse.lean` (axiom-clean: `largeSieve_eq_two_parseval`,
`parseval_le_largeSieve`, `largeSieve_sup_ge_parseval_sup`) and
`docs/kb/deltastar-407-large-sieve-dimension-obstruction-2026-06-13.md`. C30 does not escape
either finding.

**Horn 1 ‚ÄĒ the MULTIPLICATIVE Sawin‚ÄďShusterman family is OVER-DIMENSIONED in the prize regime
(the named machinery does not even fire).** The literal "Gauss-sum family" is `{g(Ōá_s)}_{s‚ąąZ/f}`,
`f = (p‚ąí1)/n`. A large sieve over an `N`-character family has discrepancy `~ N/‚ąöq`; at depth `r`
the rank is `d_r ~ f^r/r!`, so the discrepancy is `~ f^r/(r!‚ąöq)`, effective (`<1`) **iff** `f ‚Č§ ‚ąöq
‚üļ (p‚ąí1)/n ‚Č§ ‚ąöp ‚üļ n ‚Č• ‚ąöp`. The prize regime is `n ‚Č™ ‚ąöp` (ő≤ ‚Č• 4 ‚üĻ `n = p^{1/4} ‚Č™ p^{1/2}`).
Measured over-dimensioning factor `f/‚ąöp = ‚ąöp/n` (probe):

| n | ő≤ | f=(p‚ąí1)/n | ‚ąöp | f/‚ąöp | n‚Č•‚ąöp? | Sawin‚ÄďShusterman sieve |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 8 | 4.00 | 516 | 64.3 | 8.03 | no | VACUOUS |
| 16 | 4.00 | 4096 | 256.0 | 16.0 | no | VACUOUS |
| 2¬≥‚Āį | 4.0 | ~1.24e27 | ~1.15e18 | **~1.07e9** | no | VACUOUS |
| 2¬≥‚Āį | 5.0 | ~1.33e36 | ~3.78e22 | **~3.52e13** | no | VACUOUS |

The family-orthogonality machine is structurally vacuous by a factor `‚ąöp/n` (`= n` at ő≤=4) exactly
in the prize regime. This is the geometric mirror of the additive-combinatorial wall (BGK/Shkredov
want `n` LARGE; Deligne/large-sieve wants `n ‚Č• ‚ąöp`; the prize `n ‚Č™ ‚ąöp` defeats both).

**Horn 2 ‚ÄĒ the AMPLIFIED additive large sieve is SATURATED and its sup IS the open energy moment.**
The `2r`-th moment in the frequency variable is a pure additive count: `ő£_b|ő∑_b|^{2r} = q¬∑E_r(őľ_n)`
(verified to machine precision at every row, `|q¬∑E_r ‚ąí ő£|ő∑|^{2r}| ‚Č§ 1e‚ąí7` for the elaborated rows).
The additive large-sieve RHS `(M+őī‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)¬∑‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ‚āā¬≤ = (q+q)¬∑E_r = 2q¬∑E_r` is **exactly twice** the Parseval
value ‚ÄĒ the sieve strictly LOSES a factor 2 (in-tree `largeSieve_eq_two_parseval`). So the amplified
sup `(q¬∑E_r)^{1/2r}` is just the Parseval/moment sup, and "reaching the MAX past Johnson" is exactly
the question "is `E_r ‚Č§ (2r‚ąí1)‚Äľ¬∑n^r` to depth `r ‚Čą ln q`?" ‚ÄĒ the OPEN BGK/energy moment (face 3 of
the open core), NOT a theorem one may invoke. Measured sup descent (n=16, ő≤=4, `M/‚ąön` must reach
the Johnson-passing floor `‚ąö(2 log(p/n)) ‚Čą 4.08`):

| r | E_r/Wick | sup via Parseval /‚ąön | sup via LargeSieve /‚ąön | if-Wick-granted /‚ąön | past Johnson? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 1.000 | 256.0 | 362.0 | 256.0 | no |
| 3 | 0.823 | 9.65 | 10.84 | 9.97 | no |
| 5 | 0.522 | 5.64 | 6.04 | 6.01 | no |
| 8 | 0.173 | 4.44 | 4.64 | 4.96 | no |

Even GRANTING the open Wick bound the sup is still above Johnson at every feasible `r`; the descent
past Johnson lives at `r ‚Čą ln q ‚Čą 90`, which is precisely the char-`p` energy transfer that is OPEN
for the prize `n = 2¬≥‚Āį` (norm bound `q > (2r)^{n/2}` covers only `n ‚Č≤ 40`). The large sieve adds
nothing past the diagonal ‚ÄĒ `largeSieve_sup_ge_parseval_sup` proves it is always the WRONG side of
the exact moment.

**Net:** C30 names a real machine (Sawin‚ÄďShusterman family orthogonality) but it is the same large
sieve already collapsed in-tree. In the multiplicative reading it is dimension-obstructed (vacuous by
`‚ąöp/n ~ 10‚ĀĻ` in-regime); in the additive reading it is saturated to the exact moment and the
"amplified sup reaching the MAX past Johnson" IS the open BGK energy bound `E_r ‚Č§ (2r‚ąí1)‚Äľn^r` at
`r ‚Čą ln q`. No new non-BGK lever; the open core is not discharged. Consistent with the
route-elimination meta-theorem: every closed/orthogonality/second-moment object caps at Johnson, and
passing it requires the open p-dependent moment.

## C31 (issue #444) ‚ÄĒ Katz Vertical/Horizontal Sato-Tate "support edge bounds uniform sup" ‚ÄĒ SECRETLY-OPEN

**Date:** 2026-06-15. **Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_c31_satotate_support_edge.py` (commit f86e902f7).

**Claim:** the period-family geometric monodromy yields a limiting Sato-Tate measure whose SUPPORT
EDGE bounds `max_c|ő∑_c| ‚Č§ ‚ąö(2n log p)` past Johnson (reduces-to Katz vert/horiz Sato-Tate +
Rojas-Le√≥n classification + Deligne Weil II).

**Verdict: SECRETLY-OPEN.** Two independent horns, both confirmed prize-faithfully (proper őľ_n,
n=2^őľ, p prime, p‚Čęn¬≥, m=(p-1)/n>1, never n=p-1):

- **HORN-EDGE (category error, load-bearing).** A Sato-Tate measure governs the LIMITING
  DISTRIBUTION of the normalized value `z_c = ő∑_c/‚ąön`; its support is the closure of attainable
  normalized values. By Rojas-Le√≥n (monodromy = GL(1)^f, only Hasse‚ÄďDavenport relations), `z_c` is a
  normalized sum of f=(p-1)/n independent unit Gauss phases ‚üĻ COMPLEX GAUSSIAN by CLT. Measured:
  `E|z|^{2r}/r! ‚Üí 1` in low orders (0.999, 1.31, ‚Ä¶), the Gaussian signature; the limiting measure has
  support = **all of C, NO compact edge**. So there is no "support edge" object to bound the sup. The
  factor ‚ąö(2 log p) is the EXTREME VALUE of this unbounded Gaussian over the p/n cosets:
  `max|z| / ‚ąö(2 ln(#cosets)) = 0.71 ‚Üí 0.81 ‚Üí 0.89 ‚Üí 0.96` (climbs to 1 as coset count grows) ‚ÄĒ i.e.
  the union-bound tail max ~ ‚ąö(n log(p/n)), which IS the OPEN BGK/Paley sup-norm, not an edge.
- **HORN-DIM (inherited from C13).** Monodromy torus dim = f=(p-1)/n ~ 2^128, not őľ/n. Deligne/Weil II
  effective discrepancy = f/‚ąöq ~ ‚ąöp/n ~ 2^46‚Ä¶2^56 ‚Čę 1 ‚ÄĒ catastrophically vacuous for n ‚Č™ p^{1/4}.
- **HORN-VERT/HORIZ.** Both vertical (vary sheaf/Ōá) and horizontal (vary field, q‚Üí‚ąě) Sato-Tate are
  DISTRIBUTIONAL limiting statements; neither controls the DETERMINISTIC MAX over a thin designed
  subgroup at one FIXED prize prime.

Net: the "support edge" is precisely the open BGK extreme value (the conjecture asserts its own open
conclusion). Consistent with the route-elimination meta-theorem and the prior C13 SECRETLY-OPEN
finding. No new non-BGK lever; the open core is not discharged.

## [C37] (issue #444) ‚ÄĒ Burgess-Type Amplification for the Thin Dyadic Subgroup Sum ‚ÄĒ REFUTED-FALSE (2026-06-15)

**Date:** 2026-06-15. **Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_C37_burgess_amplification.py` (commit e9697347a).

**Claim:** a Burgess-style shifted-multiplicative amplification of the period sum
`S_a(őľ_n)=ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n} e_p(ax)` "recovers cancellation in the prize band by exploiting many small shifts
within őľ_n," giving `M(őľ_n) ‚Č§ ‚ąö(n log p)` past Johnson (reduces-to Burgess machinery + in-tree antipodal
+ char transfer).

**Verdict: REFUTED-FALSE.** The load-bearing Burgess step is structurally inapplicable to a thin
multiplicative subgroup, demonstrated by direct probe over proper őľ_n (n=2^őľ, p prime, p‚Čęn‚Āī, ő≤=5,
n<p^{1/4}, never n=p-1).

- **What Burgess actually does (and what the claim mis-states).** The Burgess amplifier for a SHORT
  character sum `T=ő£_{x‚ąąI}Ōá(x)`, `|I|=N`, works because `I` is an **interval**: the load-bearing first
  step replaces `T` by an average over translations `T ‚Čą (1/|Sh|)ő£_{t‚ąąSh}ő£_{x‚ąąI}Ōá(x+t)`, which is nearly
  lossless precisely because `|(I+t)‚ą©I| = N‚ąí|t| ‚Čą N` for the relevant small shifts. The 2r-th moment
  over `t` is then a COMPLETE multiplicative correlation sum bounded by Weil/‚ąöp. The whole gain
  `N^{1‚ąí1/r}p^{(r+1)/(4r¬≤)}` is nontrivial only for `N ‚Čę p^{1/4}`. The conjecture's phrase "many small
  shifts WITHIN őľ_n" conflates this with multiplicative shifts: `x‚Ü¶xh` for `h‚ąąőľ_n` merely PERMUTES őľ_n
  (zero amplification, `S_a` is fixed up to the `a‚Ü¶a h` relabel), while the only structurally-real shift
  is the ADDITIVE translation Burgess uses ‚ÄĒ and that needs the set to be an interval.
- **The amplification is identically zero for a subgroup (probe, decisive).** Measured the load-bearing
  overlap `|(őľ_n+t)‚ą©őľ_n|` over 600 random nonzero `t`, strictly in the prize band p‚Čęn‚Āī (ő≤=5):

  | őľ | n | p (prime) | ő≤=log_n p | p/n¬≥ | mean `|(őľ_n+t)‚ą©őľ_n|` | n¬≤/p (random-set value) | max |
  |---|---|-----------|-----------|------|----------------------|--------------------------|-----|
  | 4 | 16 | 1048609 | 5.00 | 256 | **0.0000** | 0.000229 | **0** |
  | 5 | 32 | 33554593 | 5.00 | 1024 | **0.0000** | 0.000030 | **0** |
  | 6 | 64 | 1073741953 | 5.00 | 4096 | **0.0000** | 0.000004 | **0** |
  | 7 | 128 | 34359740801 | 5.00 | 16384 | **0.0000** | 0.0000004 | **0** |
  | 8 | 256 | 1099511630593 | 5.00 | 65536 | **0.0000** | ~0 | **0** |
  | 10 | 1024 | 1125899906856961 | 5.00 | 10‚Ā∂ | **0.0000** | ~0 | **0** |

  (At the ő≤=3 boundary `p‚Čąn¬≥`, the in-tree run gives mean `n¬≤/p ‚Čą 0.005‚Äď0.11`, max 2 ‚ÄĒ still negligible.)
  The subgroup behaves like a RANDOM set under additive translation: `E|(őľ_n+t)‚ą©őľ_n| = n(n‚ąí1)/(p‚ąí1) ‚Čą
  n¬≤/p ‚Üí 0` for `n<p^{1/4}` (since `n¬≤/p < p^{1/2}/p = p^{‚ąí1/2}`). Translating the summation set by any
  small `t` annihilates the overlap, so the Burgess average `‚Čą(1/|Sh|)ő£_t ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n}Ōá(x+t)` carries NO
  information about `ő£_{x‚ąąőľ_n}Ōá(x)`. **Amplification efficiency = mean_overlap/n = 0.** There is nothing
  to amplify.
- **The threshold is exactly the prize-excluded side.** Burgess (and every short-interval/GRH variant)
  is non-trivial only for `N‚Čęp^{1/4}`; the whole prize band is `n=p^őī` with `őī‚ąą[0.135,0.215] < 1/4`
  (ő≤>4). This is the same `p^{1/4}` wall that excludes di Benedetto et al. (the in-tree gaussperiods
  reading list, line 79-81: "Requires n>p^{1/4}; the prize has n<p^{1/4} strictly") and is the exact
  twin of the **C36 REFUTED-FALSE** Heath-Brown‚ÄďKonyagin Stepanov entry: both are `q^{1/3}`/`p^{1/4}`-blind
  to the thin prize subgroup. "Many small shifts" cannot move the threshold because the gain is
  exponent-fixed by the completion-to-Weil step, not by a constant.
- **No char-transfer / antipodal rescue.** The antipodal structure of `őľ_{2^őľ}` (Lam‚ÄďLeung: only the
  pair relation `z+(‚ąíz)=0`) governs the CHAR-0 additive-energy bound; it says nothing about additive
  translates of őľ_n in F_p, and the probe's overlap-collapse holds AT the prize prime regardless. The
  char transfer brick (L5) transfers incidence, not a Burgess interval-gain that does not exist.

**Mechanism class.** Same horn as the entire interval/short-sum family (C36 Stepanov, all Weil/‚ąöq): the
method's nontriviality threshold (`N>p^{1/4}`) is precisely the prize-excluded boundary, and its
load-bearing translation-amplification step is identically lossless only for an INTERVAL ‚ÄĒ for a thin
multiplicative subgroup the additive shift-overlap is a measured 0 (random-set `n¬≤/p`), so the amplifier
produces no cancellation. Matches the DEAD-ROUTES note ("Weil/‚ąöq: vacuous for n<‚ąöq") and the
gaussperiods reading-list headline ("even GRH/Burgess control intervals not subgroups, and need
n>p^{1/4}"). No new non-BGK lever; the open core is not discharged ‚ÄĒ the bound it claims to deliver
(`‚ąö(n log p)` for n<p^{1/4}) IS the open BGK/Paley sup-norm.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_C37_burgess_amplification.py`.

## C38 (#444) ‚ÄĒ Multiplicative Energy Concentration via BSG on Bad Scalars ‚ÄĒ **SECRETLY-OPEN** (premise REFUTED) ‚ÄĒ 2026-06-15

**Claim.** The set of bad scalars `ő≥` (for a far monomial pencil) has *small multiplicative
energy* by Balog‚ÄďSzemer√©di‚ÄďGowers ("since each contributes a structured agreement set"), so BSG
extracts an approximate multiplicative subgroup, Pl√ľnnecke‚ÄďRuzsa controls its product set, and the
proven antipodal coset closure forces `|bad ő≥| ‚Č§ budget ~ n` PAST Johnson. REDUCES-TO: BSG +
Pl√ľnnecke‚ÄďRuzsa + in-tree `FactorizationRigidity` coset closure + orbit-count law L3.

**Two independent kills, both machine-witnessed (exact over proper `őľ_n`, `p` prime, `p ‚Čę n¬≥`,
NEVER `n=p‚ąí1`; probes `probe_c38_bsg_scalar_energy.py`, `probe_c38_bsg_scalar_coset_followup.py`).**

**(1) The "small multiplicative energy" premise is FALSE ‚ÄĒ the bad set is a multiplicative COSET
UNION (maximal energy).** Computed the exact bad-scalar set `B(a,b,őī) = {őĪ : x^a+őĪx^b is őī-close to
RS[őľ_n,k]}` for far pencils, then its multiplicative energy `E_mult(B)`, product set `|B¬∑B|`, and
coset structure. Across all non-degenerate rows `B` is **exactly a union of cosets of `őľ_n`**
(`coset|H|=8` at n=8, `coset|H|=16` at n=16 ‚ÄĒ verified by `b¬∑őľ_n ‚äÜ B ‚ąÄb‚ąąB`):

| n | k | ŌĀ | p | (a,b) | őī | J | cap | \|B\| | budget n | E_mult | \|B¬∑B\| | E/\|B\|¬≤ | structure |
|---|---|---|---|-------|---|---|-----|-----|--------|--------|-------|--------|-----------|
| 8 | 2 | 1/4 | 4129 | (3,4) | 0.637 | 0.500 | 0.750 | 24 | 8 | 7680 | 48 | 13.3 | coset \|H\|=8 |
| 16 | 2 | 1/8 | 65537 | (3,4) | 0.841 | 0.646 | 0.875 | 400 | 16 | 5754880 | 4928 | 36.0 | coset \|H\|=16 |
| 16 | 2 | 1/8 | 1048609 | (3,4) | 0.772 | 0.646 | 0.875 | 48 | 16 | 61440 | 96 | 3.8 | coset \|H\|=16 |

A coset union has the *maximal* multiplicative energy for its size (`E_mult ‚Čą 56‚Äď100% of |B|¬≥`,
i.e. `13‚Äď36√ó` above the Sidon/random floor `|B|¬≤`), with a *tiny* product set `|B¬∑B| ‚Čą 2n`
(products stay in a bounded number of cosets). This is the **polar opposite** of the small-energy /
multiplicatively-Sidon set BSG needs. C38's premise is not merely unverified ‚ÄĒ it is *false on the
actual object*. (Mirror of the C32 vacuity: BSG is a HIGH-energy theorem; its hypothesis is
vacuous/inverted on these structured sets. Here, worse: `B` is the extremal coset, so BSG's
*conclusion* ‚ÄĒ "approximate multiplicative subgroup" ‚ÄĒ is **given for free** by `FactorizationRigidity`
and supplies nothing new.)

**(2) BSG/PR run BACKWARDS ‚ÄĒ they never bound `|B|`; the cardinality is the open object.** Even
granting structure: BSG (`E_mult ‚Č• |B|¬≥/K ‚üĻ approx subgroup`) and Pl√ľnnecke‚ÄďRuzsa
(`|B¬∑B| ‚Č§ K^{O(1)}|B|`) take a *cardinality* and *produce* structure; neither yields an *upper bound
on `|B|`*. The only Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz relation runs the wrong way: `E_mult(B) ‚Č• |B|‚Āī/|B¬∑B|` (verified:
`7680 ‚Č• 24‚Āī/48 = 6912`), i.e. `|B| ‚Č§ (E_mult¬∑|B¬∑B|)^{1/4}`. To force `|B| ‚Č§ n` you must first bound
`|B¬∑B| ‚Č§ n` (and `E ‚Č§ n¬≥`). But `|B¬∑B|` = the number of cosets the product spans = the **orbit-count
law L3 input** (`I_pencil = N_pencil¬∑S`, `S = n/gcd(b‚ąía,n)`), whose unconditional bound is `‚Č§ n` via
`gcd(b‚ąía,n) ‚Č§ n` ‚ÄĒ exactly the **Johnson-capped** object (meta-theorem: every closed/coset/
orbit-count object caps at `1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ`). Measured `|B| = 25n` and `|B¬∑B| = 308n` past Johnson
(n=16, őī=0.841): the bad set is genuinely large past Johnson, and bounding its coset count below
budget is precisely the p-DEPENDENT BGK sup-norm `M(őľ_n) ‚Č§ C‚ąö(n log(p/n))` (open; SOTA `n^{1‚ąío(1)}`,
di Benedetto `n^{0.989}`). BSG/PR are p-INDEPENDENT low-order tools (their trilinear input is exactly
p-independent, see D9); they cannot supply the p-dependent moment.

**Verdict: secretly-open**, with the stated mechanism **refuted**. The "small multiplicative energy"
premise is false (the bad set is a maximal-energy coset union); BSG's structure conclusion is free
and inert (`FactorizationRigidity` already gives it); and the actual cardinality step ‚ÄĒ counting the
cosets below budget past Johnson ‚ÄĒ is the open BGK/Paley sup-norm. C38 collapses to **the same wall
as C32** (BSG vacuous), and to **reduces-to-johnson** on the only reading where the coset-closure /
orbit-count L3 bound is the operative lever (it caps at `1‚ąí‚ąöŌĀ`). Consistent with O11 (bad count =
subset-sumset size of the free set), the energy-method cap L8, and the p-independent-trilinear
obstruction D9.

**Probes committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c38_bsg_scalar_energy.py`,
`scripts/probes/probe_c38_bsg_scalar_coset_followup.py` (self-contained, exact GF(p), proper `őľ_n`,
`p` prime `p‚Čęn¬≥`, NEVER `n=p‚ąí1`). Probe commit `be92452b1`.
